Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
iPOSpays
iPOSpays - Release Notes
January 2026
New Features
Bringing VISA CEDP Support on iPOSpays
Unlock better interchange opportunities with Visa CEDP support on iPOSpays. Send level 3 invoice-level transaction data—including line items, taxes, and purchase details—using flexible solutions built for ISOs, ISVs, and merchants of all sizes.
Introducing Enroll & Pay on iPOSpays
iPOSpays now integrates with Enroll & Pay, letting merchants offer a card-based loyalty program directly on Dejavoo Gateway terminals. Customers can enroll instantly, earn rewards, and receive discounts—no separate app or account needed.
Automatic Risk Alert Notifications
We’re excited to announce the launch of Automatic Risk Alert Notifications, a new enhancement designed to keep ISO administrators informed and proactive—without unnecessary noise.
Automatic New User Verification Alerts
We’re introducing Automatic New User Verification, an important security enhancement designed to ensure greater oversight and accountability when new users are added to the system.
Control Agent Ability to Edit Merchant Fees
Stay in control. A new Edit Fee privilege gives ISOs the power to prevent unauthorized fee changes while keeping agents productive.
Enhancements
Extended Character Limit for Custom Tags
More room, more clarity. Custom Tags now support up to 24 characters, so your labels are clear and descriptive everywhere.
New Commodity Code Added
Categorize like a pro. A new Automotive & Light Truck Maintenance Commodity Code helps merchants track relevant transactions effortlessly.
December 2025
New Features
Auto-Enable Store-Wise Settlement Reports for New Merchants
New merchants automatically receive store-wise settlement reports via email, with pre-populated merchant emails and consolidated end-of-day totals.
Bug Fixes
Incorrect “Expires On” Date Displayed in Merchant Invoicing
Fixed an issue where invoices showed incorrect or illogical expiry dates; the Expires On field now consistently reflects the correct timeline across all invoice types and payment methods.
Correct Payment Method Displayed in Merchant Invoicing
Resolved a display issue where completed invoices showed “Send Payment Link” instead of the actual payment method, ensuring Card and Bank (ACH) payments now appear accurately.
Incorrect Fee Applied on Debit Pre Auth Transactions When TCD Was Disabled
Corrected an issue where debit Pre Auth transactions had a Dual Pricing fee applied even with TCD disabled; debit transactions now process at the original authorized amount with no unintended fees.
November 2025
New Features
Introducing Customized Checkout Page & Payment Links!
Merchants can now design and personalize their checkout pages directly within the iPOSpays Portal. This feature helps align the payment experience with brand identity—allowing merchants to customize colors, fonts, logos, button labels, and even add personalized messages and custom fields.
Bank Payments Now Supported on the Redesigned Hosted Payment Pages
Merchants can now accept direct Bank (ACH) payments on the redesigned HPP & GHPP, offering a faster, secure, and low-cost alternative to card payments.
Pockyt Integration now Available on the Redesigned Hosted Payment Page
Merchants can now accept PayPal and Venmo on the redesigned HPP through Pockyt, improving conversion rates with popular digital wallet options.
Bank (ACH) Standalone Refund for CloudPOS
Merchants can now issue Bank (ACH) refunds without an original transaction, maintaining consistent and secure refund workflows directly from CloudPOS.
Cash Refund Support for CloudPOS
Merchants can now process cash refunds on the CloudPOS → Pay Now screen
Enhancements
Breakup Screen Now Optional on HPP & GHPP
Merchants can now enable or disable the Breakup Screen on payment links for a faster, more streamlined checkout experience via S.T.E.A.M.
Changed “ACH” to “Bank” for Better Readability
The ACH label has been updated to “Bank” across iPOSpays, making bank-based payment options clearer and easier to select.
Bank (ACH) Transaction Flexibility: Optional & Mandatory Tags
Merchants can now enable or disable optional ACH fields at the store level, giving more control over how Bank (ACH) data is captured and displayed.
Support for Canadian Postal Codes in CloudPOS
CloudPOS now accepts both U.S. ZIP and Canadian postal codes, improving usability for Canadian merchants and customers.
Auto-Enable Store-Wise Settlement Reports for New Merchants
New merchants automatically receive store-wise settlement reports via email, with pre-populated merchant emails and consolidated end-of-day totals.
September 2025
New Features
Introducing Merchant Invoicing on iPOSpays
Designed for ISOs, this powerful tool lets you effortlessly bill your merchants for services like POS, CloudPOS, QuickBooks, and more—while giving you full flexibility to set amounts as you see fit.
New Pockyt Integration on Dejavoo Terminals
Merchants can now accept PayPal and Venmo through Pockyt on CloudPOS and Dejavoo standalone POS devices.
Introducing Command and Communication Center on iPOSpays
ISOs can now remotely manage and message merchant payment terminals through the Command and Communication Center.
Apple Pay Support in Freedom to Design
Merchants using Freedom to Design with embedded checkout can now accept Apple Pay for faster, secure payments.
New JavaScript SDK for Google Pay in Freedom to Design
With the latest JavaScript SDK for Freedom to Design, merchants and integrators can now accept Google Pay directly on fully customized checkout pages.
Enhancements
Categories for Batch Settlement Receipt
Merchants can now sort batch receipts by transaction or card type on Dejavoo terminals for easier settlements.
Apply TBP Fee on Cash Transactions
Merchants can now apply TBP fees to cash payments.
Additional Options in Invoice Module
Merchants can now easily manage, resend, mark as paid, or delete invoices in the iPOSpays portal.
Card Brand MIDs Now Mandatory for 3D Secure
To improve security and compliance, merchants must now have all Card Brand MIDs set up when configuring 3D Secure on iPOSpays.
August 2025
New Features
Expanded Multi MID Support for CloudPOS with PIN pad
CloudPOS merchants using an external PIN pad can now take advantage of Multi MID support—no hardware changes needed. Route transactions through the right MID effortlessly, streamline reconciliation, and maintain secure access across stores.
Street Check in Card Validation Risk Rule
A new Street Check option has been added to the Card Validation risk rule, enabling AVS-based street address matching. When enabled, mismatched addresses trigger declines—helping ISOs and merchants block suspicious card-not-present transactions across all processors.
New UI for Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP)
We’ve refreshed the GHPP interface to align with the modern look and feel introduced across iPOSpays platforms. This update brings visual consistency with the Send Payment Link page—no changes required to your existing setup.
Storewise Settlement Email Report
Merchants can now automatically receive a consolidated settlement report by email—either instantly after each batch or as a daily summary. This update improves reconciliation across all TPNs under each store and supports both merchant-wide and store-specific recipients.
Enhanced EBT Capabilities: Contactless Payments
Merchants can now accept EBT payments via EMV chip—just like any other card. No new hardware needed; if your device supports chip and contactless, you’re ready.
Store-Level EBT Number Configuration
EBT numbers are now managed at the store level instead of the merchant level. This ensures each store can be set up with the right EBT number—perfect for multi-state businesses.
Recurring Payments – Now With “Custom” Frequency Option
Merchants can now break free from fixed billing cycles with the new Custom frequency option in recurring payments. Whether it’s every 10 days, 45 days, or any interval in between, this update lets you tailor payment schedules to match customer preferences or unique agreements—right from the iPOSpays portal.
In App SDK for iPOSgo! (iOS)
Bring payment processing directly into your iOS app—no deep links, no external dependencies, no iPOSgo! install required. The new In App SDK for iOS empowers developers to create seamless, embedded checkout experiences with full in-app control.
iPOSgo! Deep Linking SDK V2
The iPOSgo! Deep Linking SDK for iOS just got more powerful. Version 2 introduces new parameters that give integrators granular control over every step of the in-app payment flow—including tips, fees, and screen visibility—delivering a smoother, more customizable Tap to Pay experience.
Enhancements
Enhanced WorldPay Support: 3D Secure & HSA Card Acceptance
WorldPay now supports 3D Secure authentication and HSA card acceptance, boosting fraud protection and expanding payment options for healthcare and government merchants.
June 2025
New Features
Check as a Payment Type
With this update, merchants can now log checks as a valid payment type directly on their POS terminals.
Itemized Billing on Send Payment Link
Merchants can now offer detailed, transparent billing using the Send Payment Link feature with itemized billing support.
New Terms and Conditions Enforcement on Login
To reinforce legal compliance and improve data protection standards, a new Terms and Conditions agreement has been implemented on the iPOSpays login page.
Google Pay Now Available in WooCommerce Embedded Checkout
We’ve integrated Google Pay into the WooCommerce iPOSpays Payment Gateway plugin’s embedded express checkout, giving merchants a faster, more streamlined payment option for their customers.
3D Secure Management Moved to Add-On Features
To simplify configuration, ISOs can now enable or disable 3D Secure (3DS) for merchant stores directly from the Add-On Features section.
Enhancements
Merchant Code Support Extended to iOS TPNs
We’ve expanded merchant code support under Merchant Settings → Generate ECOM/TOP Merchant Keys to now include iOS TPNs in addition to TOP TPNs.
New Minor Channels Support for WooCommerce
We’ve added minor channel support for WooCommerce-Redirect and WooCommerce-Embedded. It’s behind-the-scenes goodness that improves transaction mapping, reporting accuracy, and platform tracking. Clean data = happy integrations.
FEED Enhancements for Settlement and Transaction Data
We added a new baseAmount field for cleaner settlement insights. And for those on the latest version, we’ve removed the negative signs on refund and void transactions.
Bug Fixes
Improved Digital Campaign Refresh Without Param Changes
We’ve fine-tuned the logic behind digital ad delivery to ensure a more consistent and reliable display on terminals.
Improved Template Permissions for Add-On Users
We’ve updated how template permissions work for add-on ISO users to make things clearer and more consistent.
Fix: Cardholder Name Highlighted Incorrectly on GHPP with GPay
Cardholder Name field on GHPP with GPay no longer triggers false validation errors, ensuring a smoother and more accurate checkout experience.
May 2025
New Features
Redesigned Send Payment Link Page
Completely revamped the Send Payment Link page to deliver a faster, cleaner, and more intuitive payment experience.
ACH Support in Transact API
Expanded the capabilities of our iPOS Transact API to support ACH (Automated Clearing House) transactions.
Bug Fixes
Fix for Missing RKL Device Alerts
Resolved an issue where RKL-related device alerts were missing after transactions or parameter downloads on QD4 devices.
Consistent Batch Close Time Display
Fixed a mismatch in batch close times between POS terminals and the Closed Batch tab on the iPOSpays portal.
EBT Cash Transaction Receipt Labeling
Fixed incorrect benefit type labeling on physical receipts for EBT cash transactions to match portal and SPIn logs.
April 2025
New Features
Introducing Settle By Store
Settle By Store enables merchants with multiple TPNs under a single store to settle batches instantly for all TPNs at once or select specific ones to settle.
Introducing Digital Ad on Screen
The Digital Ad on Screen feature allows merchants to display advertisements on their terminals when the screen is idle.
Apple Pay Now on iPOSpays
This update introduces Apple Pay support on iPOSpays, allowing merchants to accept Apple Pay as a payment method when sending payment links and processing transactions through checkout pages.
Template Management on iPOSpays
ISOs and Agent Offices can now create, edit, and delete parameter templates, streamlining configuration across multiple TPNs.
Separate “DvStore” Module on iPOSpays
DvStore is a new module on iPOSpays that allows ISOs to assign third-party applications to TPNs from a centralized location.
iPOSpays Secured by Memcyco
iPOSpays is now secured with Memcyco’s advanced phishing protection to ensure a safer and more reliable user experience.
Enhancements
Elavon Support for Google Pay & Apple Pay Transactions
Google Pay and Apple Pay transactions can now be processed through Elavon, expanding support for seamless digital payments.
Elavon Support for 3D Secure on iPOSpays
Elavon now supports 3D Secure on iPOSpays for enhanced fraud protection across Send Payment Link, HPP, and GHPP transactions.
Elavon Support for Card Validation Risk Rule
Elavon transactions now support the iPOS S.A.F.E card validation rule, automatically voiding approvals with invalid CVV or ZIP codes for enhanced fraud protection.
Firmware Upgrades Now Handled Automatically
Firmware upgrades are now applied automatically after parameter updates, eliminating the need for manual intervention via the iPOSpays portal.
View More Data With the “Today” tab on iPOSpays
The Today tab on the iPOSpays dashboard now offers an expanded view with filters for trend, device type, card type, channel, and mode.
Parameter-Based CVV Prompt for Google Pay
Google Pay transactions now follow CVV parameter settings on iPOSpays, ensuring accurate CVV handling as configured.
Bug Fixes
Redirecting URL Fixed on HPP API
The HPP API now correctly redirects failed transactions to the designated Failure URL instead of the Return URL.
QD Terminal TPNs: Temporarily Disabled
Creation of QD series TPNs is temporarily restricted for Elavon, Fiserv, and Worldpay due to pending processor certifications.
February 2025
New Feautures
Process Payments With Elavon
The iPOSpays portal is now certified on Elavon’s processing platform, allowing ISOs to onboard merchants to the iPOSpays portal with Elavon.
Google Pay Now Available for WooCommerce
Google Pay is now integrated into the WooCommerce iPOSpays Payment Gateway plugin, allowing merchants to offer a faster and more convenient payment option at checkout.
Clearer Transaction Updates via SMS & Email
This update ensures that SMS and email notifications sent to customers are tailored to each transaction type.
Custom Tags for ACH
This update allows merchants to customize the tag labels for ACH transactions on CloudPOS.
Enhancements
Redesigned Payments Section for WooCommerce Plugin
The Settings page now appears first instead of Payment Methods in our WooCommerce plugin on WordPress with additional enhancements to the user experience.
GHPP as a Privilege
This enhancement brings the ability to enable/disable GHPP (Generic hosted payment page) as a privilege for merchants during/after they are onboarded to the portal.
Refunds Now Supported for Transactions Older Than 90 Days
Merchants can now process refunds for transactions older than 90 days.
Warning Message for Recurring Payments
An error message will now appear when a user attempts to delete a card in the My Customers module if the card has a recurring payment linked to it.
Third Party Apps Filter
A new filter has been added to the Edit Parameters and Download Logs sections of the iPOSpays portal.
One Additional Auth Per Transaction
This update limits the number of additional authorizations allowed to just per transaction.
CVV not Mandatory for Google Pay Transactions
With this update, the CVV will no longer be sent to the processor for Google Pay transactions, even if entered in the designated field.
Cardholder Name for Paya ACH
With this update, the customer’s first and last name provided during a Paya ACH transaction will now be displayed as the cardholder name in the My Customers module and the Transactions module.
Bug Fixes
Fixed Reminder Emails/SMS for Send Payment Link on iPhones
This update is for customers using iPhones who faced issues when clicking the reminder link sent via email/SMS for Send Payment Link transactions.
Video Link Added for Domain Whitelisting in WooCommerce Plugin
A help video link has been added to the WooCommerce plugin settings to assist merchants in resolving domain whitelisting issues.
Delete User Fixed
This update addresses functionality issues with the Delete User button.
Device Alerts Now Available for Deep Linking Transactions
Device alerts are now properly handled, ensuring that success and failure logs are recorded.
December 2024
New Features
Enhance Security With 3D Secure on iPOSpays
This update brings 3DS security to all iPOSpays transactions performed on Send Payment Link, Hosted Payment Page (HPP), and the Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP).
Want to Simplify Tip Management for Your Merchants?
This update enables merchants to view untipped and server reports of other TPNs within the same store from a single terminal.
Help Your Merchants Save Big With L2/L3 Data in SPIn Requests
Merchants using SPIn can send Level 2 and Level 3 data directly from their POS application to the integrated Dejavoo payment terminal.
Enhancements
Auto-Populate for Saved Cards on Google Pay
Enjoy a faster, more convenient checkout experience with automatically filled street and ZIP code details for Google Pay transactions using saved cards.
Reminder Emails for Send Payment Link
Reminder emails are automatically sent to customers, ensuring they don’t miss their payments, even at the last minute.
Bug Fixes
Accurate Email Displayed in Invoice
This update resolves an issue in the invoice module of the merchant’s CloudPOS.
Accurate Tip & Tax Displayed for Itemized Billing Receipts
This update addresses an issue in CloudPOS’s Itemized Billing feature.
Special Characters Accurately Reflected in Email Communications
This fix ensures that special characters are now accurately reflected in email communications.
Fixed Downloading Filtered Report in Transactions Module
This update ensures that only the filtered data is downloaded from the Transactions module.
Fixed Last 4 Digits PAN Filter in Invoices Module
This update resolves an issue in the CloudPOS Invoices module where the PAN filter failed to display results when a merchant entered the last 4 digits of the PAN.
Consistent Data in Merchant Dashboard
This update resolves an issue where the data displayed on the closed batch card in the merchant dashboard did not match the data shown when the “Closed Batch” was opened.
Renamed “View by Summary” to “View by Trend”
This update renames the “View by Summary” filter within the cards of the iPOSpays Dashboard module to “View by Trend.”
Changed Default Filter Within Dashboard Cards
With this update, the “View by Device” filter is now displayed by default, ensuring a more streamlined user experience when interacting with dashboard cards.
November 2024 (Second Release)
New Features
ACH Support in Recurring Payments
This update introduces ACH as a payment option for recurring payments.
Email Templates Redesigned
With this enhancement, all email communications sent from iPOSpays will feature a modernized look and feel, reflecting our new brand identity.
Enhancements
ACH Refund Issue Resolved
This update addresses functionality issues with the Paya ACH Refund transactions.
Bug Fixes
Delete Customer Issue Resolved
This update resolves an issue that prevented merchants from deleting customers within the “My Customer” module.
L2/L3 Commodity Code Issue Resolved
This update resolves an issue that occurred while setting up Level 2/Level 3 auto-filling from the iPOSpays portal.
November 2024 (First Release)
New Features
White Label iPOSpays
This update introduces iPOSpays’ white-labeling feature, enabling ISOs to customize the portal, POS terminals, and iPOSgo! for a fully branded, tailored customer experience.
FEED Activation From Portal
With this update, we’ve introduced the ability to activate FEED directly from the portal, enabling live tracking via webhook for platforms like POS, CloudPOS, Tap to Pay on iPhone, and Tap to Pay on Android for seamless monitoring and control.
Dashboard as a Privilege
With this update, ISOs and Merchant admins can now control access to the iPOSpays Dashboard by enabling or disabling it as a privilege when creating new users.
CloudPOS Customization
With this update, merchants can now customize the requirement for phone number or email address entry on the CloudPOS - Pay Now screen, making both fields optional or mandatory based on their needs.
Get Customer Signature for CloudPOS + PIN pad
A new checkbox in CloudPOS allows merchants to enable or disable the signature capture screen that appears on the connected POS terminal after completing a transaction.
ECard Added as a Gift Host
With this update, the ECard has been added as a gift host to the iPOSpays portal.
Enhancements
View By Device
The “View By Device” filter in the “Merchants Boarding” card for ISOs on the iPOSpays Dashboard can be displayed as a pie chart.
Bug Fixes
White Label iPOSpays Bug Fixes
Logo Display Issue: Fixed a bug where the iPOSpays logo appeared on the Hosted Payment Page (HPP) and Generic HPP when no logo was uploaded. Fav Icon Disappearance: Resolved an issue where the fav icon on the browser’s tab disappeared after completing a transaction on the HPP or GHPP. Incorrect Recipient Name: Corrected the MFA email to display the correct recipient name selected by the ISO.
Store DBA Name Fixed in Invoice
With this update, the store DBA name will now appear correctly in the E-invoice email, even with special characters on the store DBA name.
Updated Password Reset Box Messages
With this update, the message on the password reset box on the iPOSpays portal will now provide specific feedback based on the user’s input to prevent generic “Invalid password” error messages from being displayed for incorrect entries.
iPOSpays Password Reset Glitch Fixed
This fix ensures the password reset link now properly directs to the reset page, even if other portal tabs are open.
Duplicate Settlement Report Issue Fixed
This update addresses an issue where, in some instances, merchants received duplicate settlement reports via email after settling a batch.
Google Pay Issue Resolved
This update addresses functionality issues with the Google Pay feature.
October 2024
New features
Cash Transactions Support for CloudPOS
This update introduces an additional payment option for merchants using CloudPOS, allowing them to accept cash transactions through the Pay Now section.
Quarterly Recurring Payments
The Recurring Payments feature has been updated to allow merchants to set up quarterly payments, enabling them to schedule payments every three months starting from a chosen date.
Card Validation Risk Rule
iPOS S.A.F.E has a new risk rule that checks for incorrect CVV and/or ZIP codes. If a transaction is approved but fails either check, it will be automatically voided, and the funds will be released back to the customer’s account.
Display Merchant and Store Logos
ISOs can now display either the merchant or store logo on Dejavoo gateway-powered terminals’ home screens, not just the ISO logo.
Enhancements
Excel File Attached to Settlement Report Email
Settlement report emails now include an Excel attachment alongside the PDF, applicable to POS, CloudPOS, and iPOSgo! apps.
Bug Fixes
Configurable Merchant VAT Registration Number
ISOs can now configure a merchant’s unique VAT Registration Number (up to 20 characters) in the iPOSpays portal, ensuring accurate Level 3 transaction data and helping merchants qualify for lower interchange fees.
Fixed Expected Average Column
The Decline Sales Trend Excel report now correctly labels the Expected Average column, resolving the previous issue where it was mislabeled as Last 90 Days.
Invoice Date for Send Payment Link Transactions
The issue with incorrect invoice generation dates for Send Payment Link transactions has been resolved, ensuring accurate invoice records in the Invoices Module.
September 2024 (Second Release)
New Features
New Payment Method: Google Pay
Merchants processing with CloudPOS, Gateway-powered payment terminals, and Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! now supports Google Pay as a payment method for Send Payment Link and GHPP (Generic hosted payment page) transactions.
Enhancements
Additional Info in Batch Settlement Report
Batch settlement reports now include Card Brand, Subtotal, and Fee details.
Customizable Tags for GHPP
The Customer Name tag that appears in the GHPP is now customizable and can be edited to suit the merchant’s needs.
Signature Upload Shown for Declined Transactions
This update ensures that the Signature Upload for declined transactions now includes all relevant information.
Bug Fixes
Signature Upload Fix for Gift Transactions
This update ensures that the customer’s phone number and email address are accurately captured in the Signature Upload for Gift transactions in the Transactions module of the merchant’s CloudPOS.
September 2024 (First Release)
New Features
FEED
iPOSpays is now offering a new feature called ‘Feed,’ which provides real-time transaction tracking for various payment methods, including POS, CloudPOS, Tap to Pay on iPhone, and Tap to Pay on Android.
Tokenization and Run a Sale for Paya ACH
Merchants using Paya ACH can now tokenize their customers’ bank account details for future payments and use the “Run a Sale” feature to autofill those details to perform Paya ACH transactions.
Whitelisting Domain Names Capability for ISOs
Whitelisted domain names can now be managed by ISOs for their merchants.
August 2024
New Features
Fresh Look and Feel
An enhanced user experience with a new color scheme, updated logo, and improved loading animations.
Security Enhancements
Mandatory 12-digit Password for iPOSpays
We have updated the iPOSpays login password minimum requirement from 10 to 12 characters.
OTP Required for TPN Change at POS
To strengthen security, a one-time passcode (OTP) is now required to change the Terminal Profile Number (TPN) on a POS terminal.
MFA for Manual Refunds
To enhance security, manual refunds will now require Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for each new session.
Enhancements
Recurring Payments Module
Previous glitches in the Recurring Payments module been resolved, ensuring a smoother experience for merchants.
Expanded Payment Notification Recipients
Merchants can now add up to 10 email addresses to receive payment notifications, an increase from the previous limit of four.
Decimal Points for Factor 4 Gift Cards
The Factor 4 Gift card system has been updated to support two decimal points for loyalty points.
July 2024 (Second Release)
New Features
Incremental Auth for CloudPOS
Merchants using CloudPOS can utilize Incremental Auth to increase the amount initially authorized in a Pre-Auth.
Pay Now Option for iPOSpays Mobile Users
iPOSpays mobile users will have the “Pay Now” option, allowing them to process a customer present transaction.
Security Enhancements
New Risk Rules
With this update, we have included two new risk rules to the iPOS S.A.F.E module: International card - Refund on CloudPOS and International card - Refund on POS.
MFA For New User Creation and Edit User
With this update, users will be required to enter a 6-digit security code to complete the user creation process and when editing an existing user.
Enhancements
Recurring Payment Time Changed
We’ve adjusted the Recurring Payment processing time to 3:15 AM EST to accommodate merchants in various time zones.
Increased Inventory Limit
The inventory limit on iPOSpays portal and Register Lite on the POS has been increased from 70 to 200 items, allowing merchants to expand their product inventory.
Account Numer in Paya ACH
The Paya ACH account number field on the CloudPOS payments module, Send Payment Link transaction, and GHPP (Generic Hosted Payment Page) has been updated to require a minimum of 6 and a maximum of 17 digits.
July 2024 (First Release)
New Features
Security Enhancements
iPOS S.A.F.E
iPOS S.A.F.E is a risk management tool that is crucial for ensuring transactional security.
New User Email Notification
Automatic email notification sent to iPOSpays Admin users when a new user is created under their account either by a primary or an add-on user.
Enhancements
Additional Lines for Receipt Header
New update allowing 2 additional lines, for a total of 6 header lines on the receipt.
June 2024
New Features
Multi-Factor Authentication
iPOSpays now requires one-time codes for login
New Parameters in Dual Pricing
ISOs can now offer merchants control over Dual Pricing: set it to apply to tips and even tax the fee itself.
Additional Commodity Codes
Added more commodity codes to Level 2 & 3 Autofilling, giving ISOs a wider selection when setting up merchants.
Change User Roles for Merchant Admin
Default merchant admins can now adjust the roles of all non-default users
Enhancements
Invoice Tab Gets a New Filter
The invoice section on the iPOSpays portal now includes a ‘Store’ filter.
UI Enhancements for Mobile Users
Enhancements to ensure the UI is fluid and responsive on all smartphones despite their size.
Skip Breakup Screen for SPIn
A new option that allows ISOs to enable/disable the breakup screen based on their merchant’s preference from the iPOSpays portal.
Simplified User Choice Response
This update removes the extra step of having to click on the “OK’ button. A simple click on the payment type would ensure that the action is reflected on the payment terminal.
Settle Batch for Alternate Transactions
CloudPOS merchants can now settle batches even in the absence of any card transactions.
Bug Fixes
Void Transactions Visible in Batches
addresses a minor issue where, in certain instances, a void transaction declined by the processor did not appear in the Open & Closed Batches.
April 2024
Enhancements
GHPP Customizable Tag
Include a customizable tag when creating a Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP)
Accurate Store Name & Address
Replaces the merchant’s email and address with the store name and address on the email containing the payment link and invoice
Updated Customer Support Contact
Changed the customer support details found in the terms and conditions of Paya ACH in Send Payment Link transaction.
Surcharge Limit
Surcharge fee percentage will no longer round and is capped at 3% for all payment terminals and CloudPOS transactions.
Custom Fields in Paya ACH Transactions
Paya ACH transactions now allow for custom alphanumeric fields on the Pay Now screen, Send Payment Link.
Enhanced Invoice Retrieval for QuickBooks Integration
Lets merchants using QuickBooks integration find invoices by searching customer names, alongside invoice numbers.
Transaction Summary in Batch Report
Batch reports now include a new “Transaction Summary” providing a detailed breakdown of each transaction from each store, including transaction type, ID, and more.
Bug Fixes
Invoice Pending Icon
invoice status now accurately reflects ACH transaction completion, eliminating previous discrepancies.
Delete Cards for Store Manager
iPOSpays portal’s ""Delete card"" button now functions reliably for store manager logins, addressing previous inconsistencies.
March 2024
New Features
Whitelisting Domain Names
Allows merchants to specify the website URL they wish to integrate with Freedom to Design
Tax Removal on CloudPOS
ISOs can now grant their CloudPOS merchants the ability to remove tax from transactions, irrespective of the fee type used.
Treat Debit Card as Cash for CloudPOS
Dual Pricing now supports “Treat Debit Cards as Cash” on CloudPOS
Fortellis Invoice Number Filter
Transactions tab now has Fortellis invoice number filter.
Enhancements
DBA & Reference ID Filter in My Customers Module
My Customers module now has DBA and external reference ID filters for easier search.
Recurring Payment Notifications for Transaction Failures
Recurring payment notifications now includes both successful and failed payments.
Negative Sign & Settlement Status Shown in Transaction Report
Transaction Report now shows refunds/voids with a negative sign and adds a “Settlement Status” column for clarity.
February 2024 (Second Release)
New Features
Adjust Price in Dual Pricing
Dual Pricing now supports “Adjust Price” on CloudPOS
Purchase Order Number
Merchants now can input the purchase order (PO) number while conducting manual card entry transactions.
Enhancements
Four Decimal Places in L2/L3 Tax Rate
Define tax rates with up to four decimal places for Level 2 & Level 3 transactions.
February 2024 (First Release)
Enhancements
Renamed SMS Button to TEXT
The word SMS has been renamed to TEXT for better clarity.
State Surcharge Tax Compliance
Ensures tax compliance with state laws by offering a new “State Surcharge Tax Compliance” option for calculating tax on base amount, tip (if enabled), and fees.
Dual Pricing Fixed Fee
Merchants can now set a fixed Dual Pricing fee amount in addition to the existing percentage option.
Added Filters to the Invoice Tab
Invoice tab equipped with new filters, allowing merchants to narrow down search results when seeking a specific invoice.
Bug Fixes
Capital Letter Error in Email Field
Fixes an issue in the merchant login where capital letters in email addresses caused errors in the send receipt section.
CloudPOS + PIN pad Transactions Updated to QuickBooks
CloudPOS + PIN pad transactions now automatically updated to QuickBooks.
Worldpay
iPOSpays gateway is now certified on Worldpay’s processing platform.
January 2024
New Features
Introducing L2 & L3
L2 and L3 enhancements across all transactions on the iPOSpays portal (CloudPOS) and Gateway-powered payment terminals.
Shortened URL

New Features and Their Guides
Customized Checkout Page
The Customized Checkout Page on iPOSpays let merchants personalize checkout design, match branding, and deliver a seamless payment experience.
Apple Pay Integration
Apple Pay enables fast secure contactless payments using biometric authentication, simplifying checkout and enhancing convenience for customers and merchants.
Simplify Billing With Merchant Invoicing
Merchant Invoicing on iPOSpays lets you bill merchants, set selling prices, track payments, and get paid faster via card, ACH, or digital wallets.
Command and Communication Center
Enables ISOs to remotely manage terminals with app updates, reboots, time settings, and instant messages or images for fast control and communication.
Multi MID
Handle multiple stores or TPNs with ease—Multi MID lets you run transactions for different merchant IDs on one terminal. Check the guide to enable it on your POS.
Digital Ads on Screen
Make the most of your terminal's idle time—display ads or promos with Digital Ad on Screen. Boost brand presence at checkout. See the guide for setup steps.
DVStore – Marketplace Solution
DVStore centralizes payment apps on Dejavoo terminals, enabling ISOs, ISVs, and merchants to easily access, install, and manage solutions.
3D Secure on iPOSpays
Boost security with 3D Secure (3DS) in Send Payment Link, HPP & GHPP. Authenticate cardholders, cut fraud & chargebacks. Check the guide to set it up quickly.

Payment Terminal Integrations
Developer Integration Kits for Dejavoo Payment Terminals
DvStore
DvStore is a powerful marketplace application designed to centralize and streamline payment solutions for merchants. Independent Software Vendors (ISVs) can list their applications on DvStore, giving merchants access to a diverse ecosystem of apps directly on their Dejavoo payment terminal. This eliminates the need for merchants to invest in multiple hardware devices, ensuring both convenience and cost efficiency.
List Now
For more information about how DVStore works and how to manage applications, refer to the below documentation.
DVStore
Deeplinking Integration
Third-party applications listed on DvStore can integrate seamlessly with the Dejavoo payment application, DvPayLite, for payment processing, providing a unified and efficient payment experience for merchants.
We offer two modes of Deeplinking integration:
Deep Linking in Intent Mode
What it is
:
This mode is tailored for Android applications, using Android Intents to navigate to specific screens or perform actions.
How it works
:
An Intent is sent to the app, specifying the desired activity or action.
The app processes the Intent and included parameters to determine its behavior.
Through Intent Mode
Deep Linking in URI Mode
What it is
: A cross-platform mode leveraging Uniform Resource Identifiers (URIs) that works seamlessly on Android, iOS, and web environments.
How it works
:
A URI acts as a direct link to content or functionality within the app.
The URI is mapped to the app’s routing system during development to determine navigation or actions.
Through URI Mode
Peripheral/Hardware SDKs
Third-party applications listed on DvStore can also utilize Dejavoo terminal peripherals to extend their functionality. Seamlessly integrate with our SDKs to access powerful hardware features:
Printer SDK
Swipe Reader SDK
All of our payment applications included in this page comply with
PCI DSS
(Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard). PCI DSS is a set of security standards designed to ensure that any company accepting, processing, storing, or transmitting credit card information maintains a secure environment. By adhering to these guidelines, we ensure that all payment data is handled with the highest level of security, protecting sensitive information and preventing data breaches.

pages.title: POS Terminals - Release Notes | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
POS Terminals - Release Notes | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
POS Terminal
POS Terminals - Release Notes
January 2026
New Features
Bringing VISA CEDP Support on iPOSpays
Unlock better interchange opportunities with Visa CEDP support on iPOSpays. Send level 3 invoice-level transaction data—including line items, taxes, and purchase details—using flexible solutions built for ISOs, ISVs, and merchants of all sizes.
Introducing Enroll & Pay on iPOSpays
iPOSpays now integrates with Enroll & Pay, letting merchants offer a card-based loyalty program directly on Dejavoo Gateway terminals. Customers can enroll instantly, earn rewards, and receive discounts—no separate app or account needed.
Configurable Logo Display on SPIn Terminals
Clean, professional, consistent. SPIn terminals now display one logo at a time—ISO or Merchant—no clutter, just clarity.
Enhancements
Extended Character Limit for Custom Tags
More room, more clarity. Custom Tags now support up to 24 characters, so your labels are clear and descriptive everywhere.
Reduced Wait Time in PIN pad Mode
Speed matters. PIN pad Mode now proceeds faster, cutting unnecessary wait time and keeping checkout moving.
Fee Report Support for Custom Fees
Accuracy meets flexibility. Custom Fees now follow standard reporting logic, making fee tracking consistent and reliable.
Improved PIN pad Transaction Handling
No more interruptions. PIN pad transactions now flow smoothly, with smarter guidance only when needed.
Cardholder Name Display on Receipts
Receipts that make sense. Only the real cardholder name prints, removing placeholders for a cleaner, professional look.
Bug Fixes
Last 4 Digit Entry Causing Transaction Cancellation in PIN pad Mode
Fixed an issue in PIN pad Mode where transactions were being cancelled after entering the last four digits of the card number using the physical keypad.
UnionPay EMV Transactions Fixed
An issue affecting UnionPay EMV PIN transactions has been resolved.
POS Settlement Report Total Accuracy
Fixed an issue in POS-initiated settlement reports where voided transactions were incorrectly included in the total.
Reduced Customer Copy Confirmation Prompt Duration
The Customer Copy Print Confirmation prompt in PIN pad Mode has been updated to improve transaction flow.
Partial Gift Card Redemption Tips Fixed
Fixed an issue where tips were not consistently applied during partial gift card redemptions.
September 2025
New Features
New Pockyt Integration on Dejavoo Terminals
Merchants can now accept PayPal and Venmo through Pockyt on CloudPOS and Dejavoo standalone POS devices.
Introducing Command and Communication Center on iPOSpays
ISOs can now remotely manage and message merchant payment terminals through the Command and Communication Center.
Gift and Loyalty Support for Deep Linking Users
Merchants using Deep Linking on Dejavoo iPOSpays terminals can now accept gift cards and manage loyalty programs for a faster, flexible checkout.
Enhancements
Categories for Batch Settlement Receipt
Merchants can now sort batch receipts by transaction or card type on Dejavoo terminals for easier settlements.
Apply TBP Fee on Cash Transactions
Merchants can now apply TBP fees to cash payments.
Bug Fixes
Gift Card Receipt Balance Display
Receipts now show $0.00 for fully redeemed gift cards, with the fix live across all gift hosts.
August 2025
New Features
Introducing ‘BLINK’ on iPOSpays
Discover BLINK—our latest upgrade to the iPOSpays portal that delivers faster, smarter access to transaction data with role-specific filters and instant downloads.
Expanded Multi MID Support for CloudPOS with PIN pad
CloudPOS merchants using an external PIN pad can now take advantage of Multi MID support—no hardware changes needed. Route transactions through the right MID effortlessly, streamline reconciliation, and maintain secure access across stores.
Enhanced EBT Capabilities: Contactless Payments
Merchants can now accept EBT payments via EMV chip—just like any other card. No new hardware needed; if your device supports chip and contactless, you’re ready.
SPIn-Enabled Terminals Now Support Dynamic Cart Updates
Customers can now view their cart being updated in real time right on the terminal. With support for dynamic updates through SPIn, terminals stay in sync with every change from the POS, helping merchants deliver a smoother, more transparent checkout experience.
EVS Age Verification Now Available via DvStore
The new EVS Age Verification app—now available in DvStore—enables real-time ID scanning and compliance checks right from your Dejavoo terminal powered by iPOSpays. Whether it’s liquor, vaping, or gaming, merchants can now verify age with speed and accuracy at the point of sale.
Recurring Payments – Now With “Custom” Frequency Option
Bug Fixes
Status Check Timing on P3 Terminals
P3 terminals now automatically perform status checks immediately after network reconnection, eliminating delays seen in prior builds.
Declined Receipts Now Printing on Kozen & WizarPOS PIN pads
Declined transaction receipts now print on Kozen and WizarPOS PIN pads, ensuring complete and consistent records across all outcomes.
June 2025
New Features
Multi MID Support  for SPIn-Enabled Terminals
Merchants using SPIn-enabled Dejavoo terminals can now process transactions using multiple Merchant IDs (MIDs).
Check as a Payment Type
With this update, merchants can now log checks as a valid payment type directly on their POS terminals.
Enhancements
Optional Breakup Screen for POS Terminals
With this update, the breakup screen can be enabled/disabled for all payment terminals being used as standalone, PIN pad, or through Deep Linking.
SPIn Intermediate Status Updates
We’ve implemented intermediate status messages during transactions initiated from third-party cash registers and sent to Dejavoo gateway-powered payment terminals
Deep Linking: Optional Screens Support
We’ve added flexibility to the deep-linking flow on Dejavoo gateway-powered terminals by making the transaction Approval/Decline screens optional.
May 2025
Enhancements
Multi-MID Across All Devices
Multi-MID functionality is now available across all Dejavoo gateway-powered payment terminals.
Bug Fixes
Fix for Missing RKL Device Alerts
Resolved an issue where RKL-related device alerts were missing after transactions or parameter downloads on QD4 devices.
Consistent Batch Close Time Display
Fixed a mismatch in batch close times between POS terminals and the Closed Batch tab on the iPOSpays portal.
Improved SPIn Response Data
Ensured Transaction ID and iPOS Token consistently appear in SPIn status responses, even under unstable network conditions.
EBT Cash Transaction Receipt Labeling
Fixed incorrect benefit type labeling on physical receipts for EBT cash transactions to match portal and SPIn logs.
Increased Offline Mode Transaction Limit
Raised the offline transaction threshold from 30 to 150, enabling greater flexibility during connectivity outages.
April 2025
New Features
Introducing Settle By Store
Settle By Store enables merchants with multiple TPNs under a single store to settle batches instantly for all TPNs at once or select specific ones to settle.
Introducing Digital Ad on Screen
The Digital Ad on Screen feature allows merchants to display advertisements on their terminals when the screen is idle.
Enhancements
Elavon Supports Offline Mode Transactions
Elavon now supports offline mode for all transaction types, enabling merchants to accept payments without interruption during network outages.
Firmware Upgrades Now Handled Automatically
Firmware upgrades are now applied automatically after parameter updates, eliminating the need for manual intervention via the iPOSpays portal.
February 2025
New Feautures
Introducing Offline Mode
Offline Mode allows merchants using Dejavoo gateway-powered P-series terminals to process sale transactions even without a network connection.
Bug Fixes
Device Alerts Now Available for Deep Linking Transactions
Device alerts are now properly handled, ensuring that success and failure logs are recorded.
Fixed: “FEE N/A PROFILE” Error in Deep Linking with Fee Profiles
Fixed an issue where the “FEE N/A PROFILE” error appeared on the first transaction after a terminal reboot when using deep linking with a fee profile.
December 2024
New Features
Want to Simplify Tip Management for Your Merchants?
This update enables merchants to view untipped and server reports of other TPNs within the same store from a single terminal.
Help Your Merchants Save Big With L2/L3 Data in SPIn Requests
Merchants using SPIn can send Level 2 and Level 3 data directly from their POS application to the integrated Dejavoo payment terminal.
Bug Fixes
Accurate Values Displayed in Display Cart Items Screen
This update ensures that item values sent from the merchant’s cash register to the integrated PIN pad terminal during SPIn transactions are displayed accurately.
Fixed “Spring framework” Error During Incremental Auth
This update resolves an issue where a “Spring Framework” error would appear on the merchant’s Dejavoo payment terminal
November 2024 (First Release)
New Features
Tip Adjust by Store
This update allows merchants using POS or CloudPOS terminals to adjust the tip for multiple TPNs from a single device or batch, as long as all TPNs are within the same store.
Get Customer Signature for CloudPOS + PIN pad
A new checkbox in CloudPOS allows merchants to enable or disable the signature capture screen that appears on the connected POS terminal after completing a transaction.
Display Cart Items on Device for SPIn
With this update, merchants using SPIn integration can now include cart items in the request sent from their cash register application to their Dejavoo payment terminals, enabling the terminals to display the cart items along with the card and cash prices of the transaction.
October 2024
New features
Asset Protection
This feature safeguards Dejavoo gateway-powered terminals by registering the terminal’s serial numbers to an ISO Office, preventing other unauthorized ISO Offices from downloading their Terminal Profile Numbers (TPN) into the protected asset.
Card Validation Risk Rule
iPOS S.A.F.E has a new risk rule that checks for incorrect CVV and/or ZIP codes. If a transaction is approved but fails either check, it will be automatically voided, and the funds will be released back to the customer’s account.
Display Merchant and Store Logos
ISOs can now display either the merchant or store logo on Dejavoo gateway-powered terminals’ home screens, not just the ISO logo.
September 2024 (Second Release)
New Features
Fee Report for Backward Compatibility
This update separates the base amount and fee for Dual Pricing SPIn transactions on Gateway-Powered Dejavoo terminals, instead of combining them. When the Fee Report parameter is enabled, both values are now reported individually.
Print Receipt for DeepLinking Integration
Merchants using Deep Linking with Dejavoo Gateway terminals can now print both merchant and customer transaction receipts.
Enhancements
Enhanced Tip Screen on POS
The Tip suggestions screen on Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminals has been updated so that when a user taps on any of the tip choices, the app will take them to the next screen.
Customer Copy Print Confirmation for SPIn
Merchants are now prompted to confirm printing the customer copy during PIN pad transactions when the Customer Copy Print option is enabled.
Updated Visibility of “Make Card Price Same as Cash Price
Make Card Price Same as Cash Price’ now appears on the Register Lite break-up screen only if fee adjustment options are enabled and the fee is active for the TPN.
Bug Fixes
Get Signature Response Code in SPIn
This update ensures that customer signatures on SPIn-integrated PIN pad terminals are correctly reflected in the Get Signature response code.
iPOS Token in Deep Linking Response Code
For merchants using Deep Linking with Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminals, the response code will now include an ‘iPOS Token.’
Pre-Auth Transaction Retrieval
This update fixes the ‘Transaction data not found’ error for merchants using Deep Linking with Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminals, allowing successful retrieval of Pre Auth transactions after batch settlement.
Fixed: Discover Card Compatibility with Apple Pay
This update resolves the issue preventing Discover cards saved in Apple Wallet from completing transactions on our Gateway-powered terminals, ensuring full support for these transactions.
August 2024
New Features
Incremental Auth for POS
Merchants using Gateway-powered terminals can utilize Incremental Auth to increase the amount initially authorized in a Pre-Auth.
Security Enhancements
OTP Required for TPN Change at POS
To strengthen security, a one-time passcode (OTP) is now required to change the Terminal Profile Number (TPN) on a POS terminal.
July 2024 (Second Release)
Enhancements
Account Numer in Paya ACH
The Paya ACH account number field on the CloudPOS payments module, Send Payment Link transaction, and GHPP (Generic Hosted Payment Page) has been updated to require a minimum of 6 and a maximum of 17 digits.
July 2024 (First Release)
New Features
DvStore
A new marketplace application designed to simplify payment processing for merchants using our P8 gateway-powered payment terminals.
Incremental Auth Through Transact API
With this feature, merchants using Transact API can add up to five incremental auth, where each increment will be added to the original pre-auth amount.
Social media/like/share/review prompt
This feature allows merchants to display a QR code to link their social media profiles, review forms, or a website.
Disclaimer Page
Enables merchants using the SPIn Cloud integration to display a disclaimer page of their choosing on the integrated PIN pad terminal.
Enhancements
Additional Lines for Receipt Header
New update allowing 2 additional lines, for a total of 6 header lines on the receipt.
July 2024 (First Release)
New Features
DvStore
A new marketplace application designed to simplify payment processing for merchants using our P8 gateway-powered payment terminals.
Incremental Auth Through Transact API
With this feature, merchants using Transact API can add up to five incremental auth, where each increment will be added to the original pre-auth amount.
Fiserv Omaha for Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! (Apple Pay for iPhone)
ISOs can now board merchants processing on the Fiserv Omaha platform with our IPOSgo! Tap to Pay solution on iPhone.
Social media/like/share/review prompt
This feature allows merchants to display a QR code to link their social media profiles, review forms, or a website.
Disclaimer Page
Enables merchants using the SPIn Cloud integration to display a disclaimer page of their choosing on the integrated PIN pad terminal.
Security Enhancements
iPOS S.A.F.E
iPOS S.A.F.E is a risk management tool that is crucial for ensuring transactional security.
New User Email Notification
Automatic email notification sent to iPOSpays Admin users when a new user is created under their account either by a primary or an add-on user.
Enhancements
Additional Lines for Receipt Header
New update allowing 2 additional lines, for a total of 6 header lines on the receipt.
Bug Fixes
Fixed Tip Adjust for Deep Linking
This update improves the Tip Adjust functionality for merchants using Deep Linking Integration.
June 2024
New Features
Mastercard’s Requirements for PIN Bypass Using Contactless Cards
PIN Bypad not supported for contactless card transactions
Enhancements
Skip Breakup Screen for SPIn
A new option that allows ISOs to enable/disable the breakup screen based on their merchant’s preference from the iPOSpays portal.
Simplified User Choice Response
This update removes the extra step of having to click on the “OK’ button. A simple click on the payment type would ensure that the action is reflected on the payment terminal.
April 2024
New Features
Register Lite
Register Lite replaces the need for a separate cash register by offering similar features directly on the payment terminal.
Server Report
Effortlessly retrieve transactions using the server ID
February 2024 (Second Release)
New Features
Treat Debit Card as Cash in Dual Pricing
Dual Pricing now supports “Treat Debit Cards as Cash” on Gateway-powered payment terminals
Print Tax Reported
Merchants can now choose to print the tax reported amount on the merchant copy for transactions processed as Level 2 & Level 3
Purchase Order Number
Merchants now can input the purchase order (PO) number while conducting manual card entry transactions.
S.P.I.n
Service Busy After Batch Settlement
During SPIn transactions, the POS no longer displays ‘Service is Busy’ message after batch settlement.
Bug Fixes
Logo Displayed on PIN pad
Master+PIN pad transactions now display logos correctly
Batch Error in Standalone Payment Terminals
Transactions now appear correctly after password entry for Show/Settle Batch.
February 2024 (First Release)
Enhancements
Renamed SMS Button to TEXT
The word SMS has been renamed to TEXT for better clarity.
S.P.I.n
Signature Compatibility
encodedSign fields inside
<Sign>
and
<ExtData>
sent as base64 image data, ensuring compatibility with the SPIn register.
Restricted Mode
Ensure the integrated PIN pad device remainz in listening mode to prevent users from accessing the menu and SPIn Bypass.
Bug Fixes
P1 Threshold Limit
Fixes the transaction threshold limit issue eliminating the need to reboot.
CloudPOS + PIN pad Transactions Updated to QuickBooks
CloudPOS + PIN pad transactions now automatically updated to QuickBooks.
Social Media Share
Brings the option to add a QR code or a message to the transaction receipt or the payment terminal.
January 2024
New Features
Introducing L2 & L3
L2 and L3 enhancements across all transactions on the iPOSpays portal (CloudPOS) and Gateway-powered payment terminals.
Last Few Batches
New filter to make it easier to view batches on all Dejavoo Gateway Powered terminals and Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!
Mini Receipt for QD Series
Mini Receipt for Gateway-powered QD devices.
Enhancements
Tag Values
Tag values entered against transactions are now displayed in the receipt and the email notification for the merchant.
Change Due Shown After Processing
The balance amount is now displayed after entering the cash amount on the payment processing screen.
CloudPOS
Retain Customer Contacts in Run a Sale
Retain customer contacs from QuickBooks during “Run a Sale.”
SPIn
< IMG > Tag in SPIn Request Code
Gateway-powered devices with SPIn can now print images and signatures on receipts
Reference ID Fix
Service busy code now accurately reflects device status on the SPIn register.
Bug Fixes
Mastercard Integrity Issue
Addressed a concern where restaurant merchants experienced tip money loss with Mastercard transactions.

pages.title: iPOSgo - Release Notes | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
iPOSgo - Release Notes | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
iPOSgo!
iPOSgo - Release Notes
January 2026
New Features
Redesigned Hosted Payment Page for Send Link and QR on iPOSgo!
A checkout refresh you’ll love! Send Link and QR payments now feature a sleek, branded Hosted Payment Page for iOS & Android.
September 2025
New Features
External Payment Options in the iPOSgo In-App SDK (iOS)
Merchants using the iPOSgo iOS SDK now support payments via card entry, QR scans, or payment links, offering a seamless checkout experience.
August 2025
New Features
In App SDK for iPOSgo! (iOS)
Bring payment processing directly into your iOS app—no deep links, no external dependencies, no iPOSgo! install required. The new In App SDK for iOS empowers developers to create seamless, embedded checkout experiences with full in-app control.
iPOSgo! Deep Linking SDK V2
The iPOSgo! Deep Linking SDK for iOS just got more powerful. Version 2 introduces new parameters that give integrators granular control over every step of the in-app payment flow—including tips, fees, and screen visibility—delivering a smoother, more customizable Tap to Pay experience.
June 2025
Enhancements
Merchant Code Support Extended to iOS TPNs
We’ve expanded merchant code support under Merchant Settings → Generate ECOM/TOP Merchant Keys to now include iOS TPNs in addition to TOP TPNs.
September 2024 (Second Release)
New Features
Register Lite for Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! Gets Additional Fee Options
Register Lite for Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! now supports a wider range of fee programs, including Custom Fee, Surcharge, and Tender-Based Pricing.
Send E-Receipt for Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!
Merchants using Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! can now resend receipts for transactions directly within the app, before batch settlement, through the new ‘Send E-Receipt’ section.
additional Commodity Codes for iPOSgo!
With this update, we have included more commodity codes for Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! users when setting up Level 2 & Level 3 Autofilling on the iPOSpays portal.

Transaction Status Check
Transactions will now display their status if the network reconnects within 5 minutes, with approved transactions no longer voided, and can be checked in the new Send E-Receipt section.

No Receipt Option for iPOSgo!
ISOs can now customize receipt options for merchants using the iPOSgo! app by enabling or disabling the ‘No Receipt’ option.

Introduction
iPOSpays

Integrations - Release Notes
January 2026
New Features
Bringing VISA CEDP Support on iPOSpays
Unlock better interchange opportunities with Visa CEDP support on iPOSpays. Send level 3 invoice-level transaction data—including line items, taxes, and purchase details—using flexible solutions built for ISOs, ISVs, and merchants of all sizes.
Introducing Enroll & Pay on iPOSpays
iPOSpays now integrates with Enroll & Pay, letting merchants offer a card-based loyalty program directly on Dejavoo Gateway terminals. Customers can enroll instantly, earn rewards, and receive discounts—no separate app or account needed.
December 2025
New features
Automated Billing Made Easy with the New Recurring Payments API
The Recurring Payments API allows ISVs to automate subscription billing, refunds, and voids directly from their own platform without logging in to iPOSpays.
November 2025
New Features
Apple Pay Support for iPOSpays WooCommerce Plugin (Embedded Page)
Merchants using the WooCommerce Plugin can now accept Apple Pay, enabling faster, one-tap, secure checkouts after domain verification.
Dual Pricing Support for iPOSpays WooCommerce Plugin (Embedded Page)
Merchants can now accept Bank (ACH) payments alongside cards, giving customers a secure, card-free payment option and expanding checkout flexibility.
Enhancements
ISV ID and Recon ID Support for SPIn API
ISVs can now use ISV ID and Recon ID tags in SPIn API transactions, providing clearer visibility into transactions and simplifying reconciliation and reporting.
September 2025
New Features
Gift and Loyalty Support for Deep Linking Users
Merchants using Deep Linking on Dejavoo iPOSpays terminals can now accept gift cards and manage loyalty programs for a faster, flexible checkout.
Apple Pay Support in Freedom to Design
Merchants using Freedom to Design with embedded checkout can now accept Apple Pay for faster, secure payments.
New JavaScript SDK for Google Pay in Freedom to Design
With the latest JavaScript SDK for Freedom to Design, merchants and integrators can now accept Google Pay directly on fully customized checkout pages.
External Payment Options in the iPOSgo In-App SDK (iOS)
Merchants using the iPOSgo iOS SDK now support payments via card entry, QR scans, or payment links, offering a seamless checkout experience.
Accept Gift Cards via iPOS Transact API
Merchants can now easily process gift card payments with seamless integration through the iPOS Transact API.
Payment Link Expiry for Hosted Payment Page API
Merchants can now set payment links in the HPP API to expire automatically between 1 and 31 days.
Get Card Request API in SPIn API
The new GetCard Request API in SPIn lets POS systems securely capture card data on the terminal via swipe, manual entry, or other methods for faster, error-free transactions.
Enhancements
New Theme for HPP API
Merchants can now enjoy a refreshed HPP API with updated Dejavoo branding for a modern, seamless checkout experience.
August 2025
New Features
SPIn-Enabled Terminals Now Support Dynamic Cart Updates
Customers can now view their cart being updated in real time right on the terminal. With support for dynamic updates through SPIn, terminals stay in sync with every change from the POS, helping merchants deliver a smoother, more transparent checkout experience.
New APIs Now Available for iPOSpays Integrators
Four new iPOSpays APIs—Authentication Token, Transaction Status Check, Batch Report, and Clear Card Encryption—expand integration flexibility, automation, and PCI compliance.
Updated Authentication Method and Encrypted Card Data Support for iPOS Transact API
iPOS Transact API v3 now supports token-based authentication and encrypted card data, enhancing PCI compliance and security for card-not-present transactions.
June 2025
New Features
Google Pay Now Available in WooCommerce Embedded Checkout
We’ve integrated Google Pay into the WooCommerce iPOSpays Payment Gateway plugin’s embedded express checkout, giving merchants a faster, more streamlined payment option for their customers.
3D Secure Management Moved to Add-On Features
To simplify configuration, ISOs can now enable or disable 3D Secure (3DS) for merchant stores directly from the Add-On Features section.
Enhancements
Deep Linking: Optional Screens Support
We’ve added flexibility to the deep-linking flow on Dejavoo gateway-powered terminals by making the transaction Approval/Decline screens optional.
HSA / FSA Card Acceptance Now Supported on iPOS Transact API
iPOS transact now supports HSA and FSA card processing for Sale, PreAuth, and Ticket transactions.
Better Handling of Address Info With iPOS Transact
We’ve added a smart fallback for situations where street and ZIP code details aren’t provided in the transaction request.
New Minor Channels Support for WooCommerce
We’ve added minor channel support for WooCommerce-Redirect and WooCommerce-Embedded. It’s behind-the-scenes goodness that improves transaction mapping, reporting accuracy, and platform tracking. Clean data = happy integrations.
FEED Enhancements for Settlement and Transaction Data
We added a new baseAmount field for cleaner settlement insights. And for those on the latest version, we’ve removed the negative signs on refund and void transactions.
February 2025
New Feautures
Enable Google Pay with Freedom to Design API
Google Pay is now supported on the Freedom to Design API checkout page, making transactions quicker and easier for merchants and customers alike.
November 2024 (First Release)
New Features
FEED Activation From Portal
With this update, we’ve introduced the ability to activate FEED directly from the portal, enabling live tracking via webhook for platforms like POS, CloudPOS, Tap to Pay on iPhone, and Tap to Pay on Android for seamless monitoring and control.
Display Cart Items on Device for SPIn
With this update, merchants using SPIn integration can now include cart items in the request sent from their cash register application to their Dejavoo payment terminals, enabling the terminals to display the cart items along with the card and cash prices of the transaction.
Enhancements
New Keys in iPOS Transact API V2 Response
The “View By Device” filter in the “Merchants Boarding” card for ISOs on the iPOSpays Dashboard can be displayed as a pie chart.
Bug Fixes
Freedom To Design Null Issue
This update improves the Freedom to Design (FTD) API by ensuring the Credit Card (CC) number field correctly displays the CC number, resolving the issue where “null” appeared for Amex card transactions.
November 2024 (First Release)
New Features
White Label iPOSpays
This update introduces iPOSpays’ white-labeling feature, enabling ISOs to customize the portal, POS terminals, and iPOSgo! for a fully branded, tailored customer experience.
FEED Activation From Portal
With this update, we’ve introduced the ability to activate FEED directly from the portal, enabling live tracking via webhook for platforms like POS, CloudPOS, Tap to Pay on iPhone, and Tap to Pay on Android for seamless monitoring and control.
Tip Adjust by Store
This update allows merchants using POS or CloudPOS terminals to adjust the tip for multiple TPNs from a single device or batch, as long as all TPNs are within the same store.
Dashboard as a Privilege
With this update, ISOs and Merchant admins can now control access to the iPOSpays Dashboard by enabling or disabling it as a privilege when creating new users.
CloudPOS Customization
With this update, merchants can now customize the requirement for phone number or email address entry on the CloudPOS - Pay Now screen, making both fields optional or mandatory based on their needs.
Get Customer Signature for CloudPOS + PIN pad
A new checkbox in CloudPOS allows merchants to enable or disable the signature capture screen that appears on the connected POS terminal after completing a transaction.
Display Cart Items on Device for SPIn
With this update, merchants using SPIn integration can now include cart items in the request sent from their cash register application to their Dejavoo payment terminals, enabling the terminals to display the cart items along with the card and cash prices of the transaction.
ECard Added as a Gift Host
With this update, the ECard has been added as a gift host to the iPOSpays portal.
Enhancements
View By Device
The “View By Device” filter in the “Merchants Boarding” card for ISOs on the iPOSpays Dashboard can be displayed as a pie chart.
New Keys in iPOS Transact API V2 Response
The “View By Device” filter in the “Merchants Boarding” card for ISOs on the iPOSpays Dashboard can be displayed as a pie chart.
Bug Fixes
White Label iPOSpays Bug Fixes
Logo Display Issue: Fixed a bug where the iPOSpays logo appeared on the Hosted Payment Page (HPP) and Generic HPP when no logo was uploaded. Fav Icon Disappearance: Resolved an issue where the fav icon on the browser’s tab disappeared after completing a transaction on the HPP or GHPP. Incorrect Recipient Name: Corrected the MFA email to display the correct recipient name selected by the ISO.
Store DBA Name Fixed in Invoice
With this update, the store DBA name will now appear correctly in the E-invoice email, even with special characters on the store DBA name.
Updated Password Reset Box Messages
With this update, the message on the password reset box on the iPOSpays portal will now provide specific feedback based on the user’s input to prevent generic “Invalid password” error messages from being displayed for incorrect entries.
iPOSpays Password Reset Glitch Fixed
This fix ensures the password reset link now properly directs to the reset page, even if other portal tabs are open.
Duplicate Settlement Report Issue Fixed
This update addresses an issue where, in some instances, merchants received duplicate settlement reports via email after settling a batch.
Google Pay Issue Resolved
This update addresses functionality issues with the Google Pay feature.
Freedom To Design Null Issue
This update improves the Freedom to Design (FTD) API by ensuring the Credit Card (CC) number field correctly displays the CC number, resolving the issue where “null” appeared for Amex card transactions.
September 2024 (Second Release)
New Features
Print Receipt for DeepLinking Integration
Merchants using Deep Linking with Dejavoo Gateway terminals can now print both merchant and customer transaction receipts.
Bug Fixes
iPOS Token in Deep Linking Response Code
For merchants using Deep Linking with Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminals, the response code will now include an ‘iPOS Token.’
Pre-Auth Transaction Retrieval
This update fixes the ‘Transaction data not found’ error for merchants using Deep Linking with Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminals, allowing successful retrieval of Pre Auth transactions after batch settlement.
September 2024 (First Release)
New Features
FEED
iPOSpays is now offering a new feature called ‘Feed,’ which provides real-time transaction tracking for various payment methods, including POS, CloudPOS, Tap to Pay on iPhone, and Tap to Pay on Android.
July 2024 (First Release)
New Features
Incremental Auth Through Transact API
With this feature, merchants using Transact API can add up to five incremental auth, where each increment will be added to the original pre-auth amount.
Bug Fixes
Fixed Tip Adjust for Deep Linking
This update improves the Tip Adjust functionality for merchants using Deep Linking Integration.
April 2024
New Features
Deep Linking on iPOSgo!
An API called Deep Linking Integration that lets the Invoking app use iPOSgo! for accepting payments.

Deep Linking Integration

New Batch Reports API: Simplified Access to Current Batch Summary from POS Application
New API Feature: POS Application Prompting and Transaction Triggering
