pages.title: Bringing VISA CEDP Support on iPOSpays | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Bringing VISA CEDP Support on iPOSpays | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Bringing VISA CEDP Support on iPOSpays
Bringing VISA CEDP Support on iPOSpays
The Visa
Commercial Enhanced Data Program (CEDP)
enables merchants to submit
invoice-quality, Level 3 line-item transaction data
along with the payment authorization for commercial card transactions. This data includes line items, taxes, quantities, invoice or purchase order numbers, and other purchase-level details that are
validated under Visa CEDP requirements
.
Dejavoo provides
multiple CEDP-enabled solutions
to help ISOs, ISVs, and merchants meet
Visa CEDP data requirements
. This allows those who submit complete and accurate enhanced data to qualify for preferred commercial interchange rates. Conversely, incomplete or inaccurate data may result in
downgrades to interchange
.
Key Rules & Requirements – CEDP Opt-In & Eligibility
ISOs
must coordinate with their
processor
to enroll merchants in the
Visa Commercial Enhanced Data Program (CEDP)
, typically at an acquirer cost of approximately
5 basis points (bps)
.
CEDP
applies only to
Visa commercial card
transactions.
For Mastercard
, simply submitting the data is sufficient; opting it at the processor level is not required.
Visa will validate the data for
500 consecutive transactions or 20 calendar days
, whichever occurs first. Based on this validation, a merchant may be marked as Verified. Even after a merchant is verified, Visa will continue to monitor data quality on an ongoing basis. Merchants should check with their processor after the evaluation period to confirm whether the merchant has achieved
Verified
status, as interchange benefits will begin only after verification is confirmed.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
: 10157 and above
Supported Processors
:
TSYS
Fiserv North
WorldPay
Supported Fee Types
: NA
Supported Solutions
:
CEDP Basic
Register Lite
SPIn API
iPOS Transact API
DvPayLite Deep Linking Integration - Intent Mode
Hosted Payment Page (HPP) API
Freedom to Design
Our Solutions
1. CEDP Basic
CEDP Basic is the entry-level version of the
Visa CEDP program
. It allows ISOs to
pre-select and report one level 3 line item and one commodity code per transaction
, making it ideal for simple businesses that sell a single product or service category. This can be preselected and is applicable to all types of transactions on iPOSpays, including payment terminals, virtual terminals, Tap to Pay, API transactions, and more.
2. Register Lite
Register Lite
offers a built-in register experience, enabling merchants to
manage inventory, pricing, and taxes
directly from the terminal. It helps capture
itemized level 3 transaction data automatically
for
Visa CEDP reporting
, simplifying
checkout management
.
3. APIs & SDKs
We support ISVs and third-party software to send Level 3 / Visa CEDP data. For eligible cards, iPOSpays transmits this enhanced data to the processor. This capability is supported across all APIs listed below:
SPIn
- Our semi-integration solution
iPOS Transact API
- Our transaction API using tokenized cards
DvPayLite Deep Linking Integration
– Deep Linking via Android Intents
Hosted Payment Page (HPP) API
– Secure, ready-to-use checkout page
Freedom to Design
– JavaScript framework for fully customizable, PCI-compliant checkout
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Offer Loyalty Programs with Enroll & Pay on iPOSpays | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Offer Loyalty Programs with Enroll & Pay on iPOSpays | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Introducing Enroll & Pay on iPOSpays
Offer Loyalty Programs with Enroll & Pay on iPOSpays
iPOSpays has partnered with
Enroll & Pay
to enable ISOs and merchants to offer an easy-to-use, card-based loyalty program directly on Dejavoo Gateway-powered payment terminals. This integration allows customers to enroll, earn rewards, and receive discounts during checkout—without requiring a separate app or account.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
: 10156
Supported Terminals
: Dejavoo Gateway-powered P Series Payment Terminals
Supported Payment Processors
: Supported by all processors
Supported Fee Types
: Supports all fee types
Requires Registration With Enroll & Pay
How It Works
Merchant Registration
Merchants register for Enroll & Pay using the registration link provided by their ISO or Dejavoo representative. They must also add the TPNs on which they wish to use the loyalty programs
ISO Enablement
After registration, the ISO enables Enroll & Pay for the merchant:
Navigate to the
Add-On Features
section in the iPOSpays portal.
Enable Enroll & Pay for the desired store.
Once enabled, the feature automatically applies to
all TPNs (terminals)
associated with that store.
Merchant Dashboard
After setup, merchants can access the Enroll & Pay Dashboard to:
View and manage loyalty transactions
Add or remove supported terminals (TPNs)
Configure reward percentages or discount offers
Track enrolled customers and loyalty performance
Customer Enrollment Experience
When a customer uses their card for the first time, the terminal prompts them to enter their phone number.
The phone number and card are securely linked to the loyalty program.
Customers receive a welcome reward or discount upon enrollment.
Future purchases using the same phone number automatically earn rewards.
How to Enable Enroll & Pay
Enroll & Pay must be activated at the ISO level first. Contact your Dejavoo representative to request access.
Step 1: Enable the Add-On Feature
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using your ISO account.
Go to
Merchants → Search and select the merchant → Store DBA → Add-On Features.
Under
Enable Loyalty
→
Provider
, select
Enroll & Pay.
Click
Save.
Confirm the prompt acknowledging that the feature will apply to all terminals (TPNs) for the store.
Step 2: Terminal Setup
From your ISO account, go to
S.T.E.A.M → Edit Parameters → Locate the desired TPN.
If needed, assign the compatible build (10157 and above) number to enable the Enroll & Pay configurations.
Click
Edit Parameter.
Navigate to
Value Add
→
Loyalty
and enable
Enroll & Pay.
(Optional) Enable or disable the reward confirmation prompt shown after a successful transaction.
Click
Save
and perform a
parameter update
on the terminal.
Card Enrollment Behavior
Multiple cards can be linked to a single loyalty account using the same phone number.
Tap and Manual Entry transactions:
Customers must enter their phone number each time to enroll or earn rewards.
Chip and Swipe transactions:
Customers only need to enroll once; the same enrollment applies to both methods.
How to Use Enroll & Pay on the Terminal
Open the
DvPayLite app
on the payment terminal.
Enter the enrolled
TPN
and download parameters.
Once the app reloads, enter the transaction amount and tap
OK.
On the Sale screen, the customer taps, swipes, inserts, or manually enters their card.
When a new card is detected, the loyalty enrollment screen appears automatically.
Enter the customer’s phone number and tap
Enroll
to join the loyalty program, or
Skip
to proceed without enrolling.
For new customers, the enrollment discount is applied automatically.
For returning customers, the configured loyalty discount is applied whenever the same card is used.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Automatic Risk Alert Notifications | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Automatic Risk Alert Notifications | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Automatic Risk Alert Notifications
Automatic Risk Alert Notifications
We’re excited to announce the launch of Automatic Risk Alert Notifications, a new enhancement designed to keep ISO administrators informed and proactive—without unnecessary noise.
What’s new?
When a risk rule is triggered, the system will now
automatically notify the ISO Admin
user associated with the affected TPN. This ensures faster visibility into potential risk events and supports timely action.
How Notifications Work:
You will receive one alert per TPN, per risk rule, within a 24-hour period.
This prevents duplicate or excessive notifications for the same issue.
If multiple different risk rules are triggered on the same TPN, you will receive a separate notification for each rule.
Why This Matters:
Clear, timely visibility into risk activity
Reduced alert fatigue with smart notification limits
Better control and faster response to potential issues
This update is fully automated and requires no action to enable.
We’re committed to making risk management clearer, smarter, and easier for our partners.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Automatic New User Verification Alerts | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Automatic New User Verification Alerts | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Automatic New User Verification Alerts
Automatic New User Verification Alerts
We’re introducing
Automatic New User Verification
, an important security enhancement designed to ensure
greater oversight and accountability
when new users are added to the system.
What’s new?
When a new add-on user is created, and their
email domain does not match
the Admin user’s email domain, the system will automatically generate a
verification required email
.
Who is Notified?
The
ISO Admin
, or
The
Agent Office Admin
, when applicable
Your Responsibility
These notifications require
timely review and response
. Admins must
validate the legitimacy
of each newly created user and
confirm approval or take corrective action
by replying directly to the automated email notification if access is unauthorized or inappropriate.
How Notifications Work
All applicable user creation events are
grouped and sent within a 30-minute time frame
.
This ensures timely awareness while minimizing notification fatigue.
Why This Matters
Protects the platform from unauthorized access
Reinforces accountability in user management
Helps prevent potential security and compliance risks before they escalate
This feature is enabled by default and requires no action to activate. Your diligence in reviewing and validating new users is essential to maintaining a secure and trusted environment.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Control Agent Ability to Edit Merchant Fees | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Control Agent Ability to Edit Merchant Fees | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Control Agent Ability to Edit Merchant Fees
Control Agent Ability to Edit Merchant Fees
A new
Edit Fee
privilege has been introduced to give better control over merchant fee management. This option allows administrators to define whether an
Agent Office
can modify merchant fees.
By default, the privilege is enabled, allowing agents to edit fees as they do today. When the
Edit Fee
option is disabled, agents can no longer modify merchant fees, and fee fields appear as read-only in the portal.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
: Not applicable
Available on
: iPOSpays ISO Admin Login
Supported Payment Methods
: Not applicable
Supported Processors
: Not applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not applicable
This enhancement helps prevent unauthorized fee changes while maintaining flexibility through role-based access control.
If the ISO disables this privilege for the default Agent Office user, any non-default Agent Office users created or managed by that default Agent Office user will also automatically lose this privilege.
How to use:
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using ISO admin credentials.
Go to
User Management
→ Find and select the user using the search box →
Edit
.
Click
Modules and Privileges → Steam → Edit Param
→ Check or Uncheck the
Edit Fee
box.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: New Commodity Code Added | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
New Commodity Code Added | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
New Commodity Code Added
New Commodity Code Added
A new
Commodity Code
has been added to
support Automotive and Light Truck Maintenance and Repair
transactions:
78181507 – Automotive and Light Truck Maintenance and Repair
This code is applicable across all templates, allowing merchants to accurately categorize relevant transactions.
How to use:
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using an ISO admin account.
Navigate to
S.T.E.A.M → Edit Parameters
.
Use the search box to find and select the TPN you wish to edit.
Click
Edit Parameter → Value Add
.
Under
L2/L3 Autofilling → Other
, click on the link inside the text box (“here”) to open the
Commodity Codes
page.
Use
Command + F / Ctrl + F
to search for the new commodity code.
Copy the code and paste it into the “
Please enter Commodity code under L2/L3 Autofilling
” field.
Click
Save
and perform a
parameter update
on the payment terminal for the changes to take effect.

Auto-Enable Store-Wise Settlement Reports for New Merchants
Auto-Enable Store-Wise Settlement Reports for New Merchants
When a new merchant is created, settlement report emails are now automatically enabled—making it easier for merchants to start receiving their daily reports without additional configuration.
What’s New

Store-Wise Consolidated Report

Pre-Populated Merchant Email

You’ll now see:
Settlement Enabled

Send a consolidated end-of-the-day report

Incorrect Expires On Date Displayed in Merchant Invoicing
Incorrect “Expires On” Date Displayed in Merchant Invoicing

Expires On
date for Merchant Invoicing did not display correctly within the Invoices module. In certain cases, the expiry date appeared earlier than the creation date or showed an unexpected value. Bug Fixes
Incorrect Fee Applied on Debit Pre Auth Transactions When TCD Was Disabled
Incorrect Fee Applied on Debit Pre Auth Transactions When TCD Was Disabled
Resolved an issue where debit card PreAuth transactions incorrectly included a Dual Pricing fee even when
True Cash Discount (TCD)
was disabled. The transaction should have been completed at the original amount, but an additional fee was being added. This behavior has now been corrected so that debit transactions process at the exact authorized amount when TCD is turned off.

pages.title: Introducing Customized Checkout Page and Payment Links | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Introducing Customized Checkout Page and Payment Links | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Introducing Customized Checkout Page & Payment Links!
Introducing Customized Checkout Page & Payment Links!
Help your merchants stand out with the new
Customized Checkout Page & Payment Links
feature on iPOSpays. Now, they can design branded payment pages right from the iPOSpays Portal—complete with their own colors, logos, button labels, and custom messages for a seamless, on-brand checkout experience.
Watch the video for a visual walkthrough of how you can customize your checkout page
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
: Not Applicable
Supported Payment Pages
: Send Payment Link, Hosted Payment Page (HPP), Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP)
Supported Terminals
: POS, CloudPOS, iPOSgo!
Supported Processors
: Not Applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not Applicable
Key Features
Customizable Templates
: Personalize checkout pages with your
brand colors, fonts, button labels, and logos
.
Advanced Custom Fields
: Add up to
five custom tags
to capture additional data, configurable as optional or mandatory.
After Payment Message
: Display a personalized confirmation message (e.g., “Thank you for your payment!”) after each successful transaction.
Multi-Store Assignment
: Assign and manage different templates for multiple stores, ensuring consistent branding across locations.
Real-Time Preview
: Instantly view and edit design changes before saving, ensuring the checkout page looks exactly as intended.
How to Customize the Checkout Page
The Customized Checkout Page & Payment Links feature in iPOSpays allows merchants to design branded payment pages and links. You can modify colors, fonts, button labels, and confirmation messages to match your brand identity and enhance your customers’ payment experience.
Step 1: Log In to Your Merchant Account
Log in to your
iPOSpays Merchant Portal
using your merchant credentials.
Step 2: Access Checkout Customization Settings
Go to
Settings → Customize Checkout Page & Send Payment Link
.
Click
Add Template
to create a new checkout page design.
Step 3: Customize the Payment Page
After clicking the
Add Template
button, you’ll enter the customization module, where you can personalize the look and feel of your checkout interface.
Live Preview
The right panel displays a
real-time preview
of your checkout page.
You can see all color, font, and text updates instantly.
In the preview top right corner, you can switch between
Desktop
:
And
Mobile
views to verify how the page appears across devices.
Some UI elements are interactive, allowing you to test how customers will experience the page.
Customize Colors
Under
Customize Colors
, you have three color options to customize the checkout page.
Brand
Accent
Secondary
Brand
Defines your primary color, applied across key UI elements that reflect your brand identity.
You can click on the brand color tile to:
— Choose a shade using the color
picker
or
slider
,
(or)
— Enter exact RGB, HSL, or HEX codes for precise color matching.
Accent and Secondary
—
Accent Color
: Controls the color of the
Pay
button.
—
Secondary Color
: Adjusts the color of supporting text and disclaimers.
— You can click on the primary and secondary color tile to:
Choose a shade using the color picker or slider,
(or)
Enter exact RGB, HSL, or HEX codes for precise color matching.
Button Label
Customize the text displayed on the payment button (e.g., “Pay Now,” “Complete Payment,” “Proceed to Checkout”).
Font Style
Select a preferred font (e.g., Mulish) for your payment page from the drop-down menu.
Logo Upload
Click on
Choose File
to upload your
store logo
for consistent branding.
Ensure the file format matches the supported type listed in the tooltip. (Upload a logo image in PNG format, maximum 100KB, dimensions between 100-120px width and 80-100px height )
Step 4: Add Custom Tags (Advanced Customization Options)
If you need to collect additional information from customers during checkout:
Click on
Advanced Customization Options.
Toggle on
Add Custom Tag.
Enter a
Tag Name
(e.g., Order ID, Invoice No., Customer Note).
Set each tag as either:
Mandatory
– Required for transaction completion, or
Optional
– Customer can skip it.
You can add up to
five custom tags
.
Use the
⊕ icon
to add new tags, the
⊖ icon
to remove them, and the
trash icon
to delete a tag completely.
Step 5: Configure After Payment Message
Click on the
After Payment
tab.
Edit the
confirmation message
that appears after a successful transaction.
Example
: “Thank you for your payment! Your transaction has been successfully processed.”
This message helps confirm successful payments and enhances customer communication.
Step 6: Save and Apply the Template
Click
Save Template
once all customization is complete.
Enter a
Template Name
to identify your design.
Choose whether to apply it to:
All Stores under your merchant account,
(or)
Selected Stores only.
Click
Save
to apply and activate your checkout page template.
Step 7: Manage Templates
After saving, you’ll be redirected to
Settings → Customize Checkout Page & Send Payment Link.
After creating the template, you can:
Edit
an existing template to make design changes.
Delete
templates that are no longer needed.
Manage store assignments
to reassign templates to different stores.
Step 8: Review and Test
Use the
Live Preview
feature to ensure your checkout design looks correct.
Send yourself a
test payment link
to verify colors, logo, and message placement.
Make final adjustments if needed and save again.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Bank Standalone Refund for Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Bank Standalone Refund for Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Bank Standalone Refund for CloudPOS
Bank (ACH) Standalone Refund for CloudPOS
Merchants can now process
Bank (ACH) Standalone Refunds
directly from their iPOSpays portal without needing an associated original transaction. This enhancement gives merchants more flexibility while maintaining the same secure ACH processing flow already used for regular ACH refunds.
Feature Compatibility
Required Build
: Not Applicable
Supported ACH Processor
: Paya ACH (Standalone Refund available only for Paya ACH)
Supported Platforms
: CloudPOS → Pay Now
What’s New
Bank (ACH) is now available as a
refund option
when issuing a standalone refund from the portal.
Refunds initiated through the Transaction module and Standalone Refunds now behave consistently.
Standalone Bank (ACH) refund transactions are now saved under the
My Customers
module for easy reference.
Prerequisites
Refund must be enabled for the merchant, and the logged-in user must have the privilege to perform Refunds.
ACH refunds
cannot be voided
.
Login As users cannot perform refund actions.
Only Paya ACH is supported.
How It Works
Standalone Bank (ACH) Refund Flow (No Original Transaction Required)
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using your merchant admin credentials.
Navigate to CloudPOS → Payments.
Select the appropriate TPN from the drop-down.
Configure the standalone refund:
Action
: Quick Pay
Payment Type
: Pay now
Transaction Type
: Refund
Payment Options
: Bank (ACH)
A popup will appear asking you to choose the customer authorization method required for Paya ACH.
Enter the refund amount.
Fill in the required customer and authorization fields.
Click
Process
to complete the transaction.
Where to Find the Refund
The transaction will appear under:
Transactions → Filter → Alternative Payments
Transaction signatures will indicate
CHECK
and
Paya ACH
.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Cash Refund Support for Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Cash Refund Support for Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Cash Refund Support for CloudPOS
Cash Refund Support for CloudPOS
Merchants can now process
Cash Refunds
from their iPOSpays portal in the CloudPOS → Pay Now screen. This enhancement ensures cash transactions follow the same consistent flow as other payment methods while supporting downloadable reports and batch handling.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
: Not Applicable
Supported Platforms
: CloudPOS → Pay Now
Supported Payment Processor
: Not Applicable
What’s New
Cash transactions can now be
refunded
on the CloudPOS → Pay Now screen and from the Transactions module
Void is
not allowed
for cash transactions — only a
fund
is shown.
Prerequisites
User must have the
REFUND
permission enabled.
Cash refunds do not support voiding at any point.
Screens have been updated to display
Refund
or
Cash Refund
only, based on transaction type.
How to Make a Standalone Cash Refund
You can perform standalone cash refunds on the CloudPOS → Pay Now screen.
To do this:
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using merchant credentials.
Click on
CloudPOS → Payments
.
Select the desired TPN from the drop-down menu.
Configure the transaction:
Action
: Quick Pay
Payment Type
: Pay now
Transaction Type
: Refund
Payment Options
: Cash
Enter the cash amount of the product/service in the
Amount
field.
Enter phone number and email in their respective fields.
Enter the amount given by the customer in the ‘
Cash In
’ field. The change is calculated automatically and displayed in the ‘
Change
’ field.
Click
Process
to complete the transaction.
The transaction appears in the
Transactions
module → Filter icon →
Alternative Payments
.
Transaction signatures will indicate
CASH
.
How to Refund an Existing Cash Transaction
Transactions
module → Filter icon →
Alternative Payments
.
Enter the specific CloudPOS TPN used to perform the cash transaction.
Select the transaction →
Refund
.
A prompt will appear asking you to confirm the refund.
Once the refund is successfully completed, the transaction label will change from
CASH
to
REFUND
.
Bug Fixes

What’s New

pages.title: Merchant Invoicing on Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Merchant Invoicing on Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Merchant Invoicing on iPOSpays
Introducing Merchant Invoicing on iPOSpays
Introduction
Merchant Invoicing on iPOSpays is a module accessible from the ISO account in the iPOSpays portal. It allows ISOs to bill their merchants for the services they use on iPOSpays, including POS, CloudPOS, QuickBooks, and more.
ISOs have complete flexibility—they can set billing amounts as needed, without restrictions from iPOSpays.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
: Not Applicable
Supported Processors
: Supported by all processors
Supported Fee Types
: Supported by all fee types
Supported Terminals
: CloudPOS (Virtual Terminal)
How Does It Work?
Merchant Invoicing uses a merchant account repurposed as a Collection Account.
This account handles and records all transactions whenever a merchant pays an invoice.
All invoice payments are processed through this Collection Account.
Payment Methods Accepted
The Collection Account uses the
CloudPOS device model by default
(this cannot be changed). Payments for invoices are accepted via two delivery methods:
1. Send Payment Link
Merchants can pay using:
Google Pay
Apple Pay
Card details
ACH
If the merchant enters their card details during a Send Payment Link transaction, those card details will automatically be saved and used as the default payment method for the next invoice.
2. ACH (Add Bank)
Set up recurring invoice payments by entering the merchant’s bank account details.
(If you wish to charge merchants directly through their bank account, they can either enter their bank details via the payment link or provide them to you, and you can enter the details manually here.)
How to Set Up Merchant Invoicing
Before you begin: If you do not see Merchant Invoicing under Invoices in your ISO account sidebar, it means this feature has not been activated for your account. Contact your Dejavoo representative to get it enabled.
Once activated, follow the steps below:
Step 1: Add Merchant Info (Collection Account)
Log in to your iPOSpays account using ISO credentials.
Navigate to
Invoices
→ Merchant Invoicing.
Click
Add Collection Account
.
You’ll be taken to the
Merchant Info
section. The details from your ISO account setup will be prefilled.
In the
Email
field, enter a different email (the original one cannot be reused).
Verify all required details, then click
Save
to continue.
Step 2: Add Store Info
Enter all required fields marked with (*).
To directly bill merchants using their bank account details, enable and configure ACH as a payment method for invoice delivery. Registration with Paya ACH is required to complete the necessary fields. Please contact your Dejavoo Sales Executive for guidance on this process.
After completing the fields, click
Add Device
.
Step 3: Add Device
The device is automatically set to
CloudPOS
and
cannot be changed
.
Select your supported processor from the dropdown menu.
Configure the
With Fee
or
Without Fee
profile based on your merchant’s account configuration, using the VAR Sheet.
Click
Save
to finalize.
The Collection Account is now created.
When the Add-on Features prompt appears, click Skip. This prompt is designed for merchants—you only need the Collection Account to manage and track invoices.
Setting Up Merchant Invoicing Preferences
After creating the Collection Account, you’ll be redirected to the Merchant Invoices Preferences section. Here you can configure:
1. Collection Information
Merchant DBA
→ Edit merchant account information.
Store DBA
→ Edit store information and Add-on Features.
TPN (Device Label)
→ View device details, update TPN parameters, and processor information.
2. Customize Invoice & Payment Link
Send Payment Link Description
→ Add a customer-facing description for invoices sent via payment link.
Send Payment Link Disclaimer
→ Add a disclaimer that will appear on the payment link page.
Logo
→ Upload a custom logo via Collection Account → Settings → Upload Logo.
When finished, click
Next
in the bottom-right corner of the screen.
3. Setting the Selling Price
Define the selling price for each service. The prices set for your ISO account and iPOSpays services are automatically carried over and prefilled here. You can adjust these prices and charge your customers as you prefer.
Click
Save
.
When you click
Save
, you will see two prompts:
Prompt 1
: Confirm that the prices you’ve set will be used as the default prices for all merchants.
If you want
merchant-specific pricing
, you’ll need to configure it separately in the Merchants module.
Prompt 2
: Reminder that merchants won’t be invoiced unless Merchant Invoicing is enabled.
Do Later
→ Skip bulk enabling (you can enable individually or in bulk later).
Bulk Enable
→
a.
Function
: Applies the selling price for all merchants immediately.
b.
Use Case
: Use this when you want to apply the default pricing template across all merchants at once.
c.
Note
: If you only want to update specific prices while leaving others unchanged, use Bulk Update instead.
Enter your email address when prompted to confirm your action, then
click
Confirm.
Editing Merchant Invoicing
After setting up merchant invoicing, you’ll land on the main page of the Merchant Invoices module. In this module, you can edit the preferences, selling price, and bulk enable Merchant Invoicing for all your merchants. To do this,
Click on the menu button in the top left corner of the module.
Edit Preferences & Selling Price
: Edit the preferences and selling prices you configured earlier.
Login to Collection Account
: Use this option to access the collection account.
Bulk Update
:
a.
Function
: Updates only the modified selling prices for all merchants, while leaving the rest unchanged.
b.
Example
: If you update the price of
Primary TPNs
, only that price changes for all merchants while all other prices remain unchanged.
c.
Additional Option
: You can choose to set the updated prices as the new
default pricing
.
If you onboard new merchants in the future, a message will appear at the top of the Merchant Invoicing module to remind you that merchant invoicing is not enabled for them. Click on the message to enable merchant invoicing for the new merchants.
Click,
Bulk Update
to enable Merchant Invoicing for the newly onboarded merchants
Enable/Disable Merchant Invoicing for Merchants Individually
You can enable/disable merchant invoicing for some of your merchants by following the steps below.
Go to the
Merchants
module.
Use the search box to find and select the desired merchant.
Click on their DBA → Edit Invoicing. You’ll land in the edit invoicing tab for this specific merchant.
SELL RATE
In this section, you can:
Enable/Disable Merchant Invoicing
Adjust the selling rate for all the services you are charging this specific merchant
Click
Next
at the bottom of the screen to proceed.
DELIVERY METHOD & CONTACT INFO
In this section, you can configure the
payment delivery method
and update the
merchant’s contact information
.
Delivery Methods
By default, the payment delivery method is set to Send Payment Link. You can change this by selecting an option from the drop-down menu.
1. Send Payment Link
The merchant will receive a payment link along with the invoice via the provided email or phone number. They can open the link and pay using their preferred method (e.g.,
Card, ACH, Google Pay, or Apple Pay
) when the invoice is generated.
A. Card / ACH
If you do not already have the merchant’s details:
The merchant can enter their
card
or
bank (ACH)
details themselves when paying through the payment link.
If you already have the merchant’s details:
You can enter them manually:
a. Log in to the iPOSpays portal using your
Collection Account
.
b. Go to
My Customers
.
c. Use the search box to find and select the specific merchant.
d. Click
Add Payment Method
→
Add Card
.
e. Enter the merchant’s card details →
Validate Card
.
f. The card details will now be saved to that merchant.
g. Return to the
Invoice Delivery Method
in the ISO account. The
Card
option will now appear, and you can set it as the payment method.
Default Behavior:
a. If the merchant enters card or ACH details through the payment link, the delivery method will automatically update to that option.
b. The next invoice—and all subsequent ones—will be automatically charged to the saved
card or bank account
.
c. Merchants only need to complete the
first payment manually
; after that, payments are automated and billed monthly.
B. Google Pay / Apple Pay
If the merchant chooses Google Pay or Apple Pay, they must manually complete each payment whenever a new invoice is generated.
2. Add Bank
If you already have the merchant’s bank account details, select
Add Bank
and enter them in the respective fields. If not, you can use
Send Payment Link
to send the merchant a payment link, and they can enter the details themselves.
Once configured, when an invoice is generated and committed, the amount will be automatically deducted from the specified account.
All subsequent invoices will continue to be charged to this account with no action required from the merchant.
Contact Information
If you select
Send Payment Link
as the delivery method:
Enter the merchant’s email address and phone number.
The payment link will be sent to these contact details.
Click
Finish
to apply the changes.
Using Filters to Find Merchant Invoices
The Merchant Invoicing module has filters and additional information about the invoices generated. To do this,
Go to
Invoices
→ Merchant Invoicing
At the top of the module, you’ll find three filters:
Search by Invoice
: To find invoices by their invoice number
Month Selection
: To find all invoices generated in a specific month
Year Selection
: To find all invoices generated in a specific year
Click on the
More
button next to any of the invoices you’ll get a more detailed view of all invoices for that specific month.
The filters at the top of the screen also get two additional filters:
Search by Merchant
: To find the invoice belonging to a specific merchant
Invoice Status
: To know all invoices based on their status, completed or pending.
Using “Login as” to Log in to Your Collection Account
In the Merchant Invoicing module, you have the option to log in as your merchant into your collection account. If this feature is not enabled for you, contact your Dejavooo representative to enable it for you. If not, this feature will remain inaccessible.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: New Pockyt Integration on Dejavoo Terminals | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
New Pockyt Integration on Dejavoo Terminals | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
New Pockyt Integration on Dejavoo Terminals
New Pockyt Integration on Dejavoo Terminals
In this release, we are introducing
Pockyt integration
for merchants using CloudPOS and Dejavoo payment terminals powered by iPOSpays.
Pockyt
is a third-party provider that allows merchants to accept payments through:
PayPal (Digital Wallet)
Venmo (Digital Wallet)
This gives merchants additional ways to accept payments, including digital wallets and alternative payment methods.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build: 10145
Supported Processors: Not applicable
Supported Fee Types: Not Applicable
Supported Terminals: Dejavoo P series
Where is it Available?
CloudPOS and POS terminals
Send Payment Link and Generic Hosted Payment Page
Supported Transaction Types
Sale
Refund
Void
Prerequisites & Requirements
The merchant must be
registered with Pockyt
to obtain credentials. Contact your Dejavoo sales executive for assistance.
The merchant must have a valid
CloudPOS or Standalone P Series TPN
and be onboarded as a
merchant admin
on iPOSpays.
How to Enable This Feature on iPOSpays
Step 1 – Enable Pockyt for Merchant
Log in to the iPOSpays portal with an
ISO account
.
Go to the
Merchants module
and search for the target merchant.
Click on the
DBA → Edit Merchant → Integrations → Pockyt → Save
.
Provide the merchant with their
Pockyt credentials
(Merchant ID, Store ID, Store Token).
Step 2 – Merchant Configuration
Log in to the
merchant portal
.
Go to
Settings → Pockyt Integration
.
Enter the required credentials.
Optionally, click ⊕ to add multiple stores and assign unique credentials.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
Step 3 – Enable in Terminal Parameters
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using
ISO credentials
.
Go to
S.T.E.A.M → Edit Parameter
.
Search and select the target TPN →
Edit Parameters
.
Under
Integrations
, enable
Pockyt
and select payment methods (
PayPal or Venmo
).
Click
Save
and perform a
parameter update
on the terminal.
How to Use
On Payment Terminals
Enter the
transaction amount
on the idle screen and tap
OK
.
If tipping is enabled, the customer will be prompted to add a
tip
.
Transaction breakdown appears; note that
PayPal and Venmo do not incur fees
.
On the Sale screen, select
Wallet
→ choose
PayPal
or
Venmo
.
Terminal displays a
QR code
or
webview
.
Customer completes payment:
Scan QR code → open browser → choose wallet → complete payment
Or pay directly in the webview
Merchants can view transaction details in
Transactions → Filter → Payments → Alternative Payments
and perform
void or refund
if needed.
On CloudPOS (Virtual Terminal)
Log in to
ipospays.com
with a merchant account.
Navigate to
CloudPOS → Payments
.
Select the
target TPN
from the dropdown.
Configure the transaction:
Payment Type
: Send Payment Link
Transaction Type
: Sale
Amount
: Enter transaction amount
Customer Info
: Enter phone number and email
Description
: Optional
Click Send Payment Link.
Benefits
Merchants can
expand payment options
to attract more customers.
Customers can pay directly using their preferred wallets (
PayPal or Venmo
).
ISOs can offer merchants
flexible and modern payment options
, helping them remain competitive.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Introducing Command and Communication Center on Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Introducing Command and Communication Center on Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Introducing Command and Communication Center on iPOSpays
Command and Communication Center
The
Command and Communication Center
is a feature on the iPOSpays portal that enables
ISOs (Independent Sales Organizations)
to remotely control merchant payment terminals and communicate with them by sending commands or messages.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
: 10145
Supported Processors
: Not applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not Applicable
Supported Terminals
: Dejavoo P8, P12, P17, P18, P1& P3 (With Android 11)
OS Requirement
: Android 8 and above
How It Works
Enable Control Center and set up the Terminal
Log in to
ipospays.com
using an ISO account.
Go to S.T.E.A.M →
Edit Parameters
.
Use the search box to find and select the target POS TPN → Edit Parameter.
Go to
Value Add → Control Center.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
Ensure the TPN has a build number of
10145
or higher.
Enter the TPN on the merchant’s payment terminal and perform a
Parameter Update
.
A message will appear on the status bar:
“
NATS JetStream
Listening for JetStream messages…”
This confirms the device is ready to receive remote commands and messages.
Access From the Portal
Log in to
ipospays.com
using an ISO account.
Select
Control Center
from the side menu. (If it doesn’t appear on your screen, contact your Dejavoo sales executive to enable it for you.)
Use the
search box
to find terminals by:
a.
TPN/Device Label
b.
Merchant DBA
Select one or multiple TPNs.
Under the
Command and Communication Center
, choose an action.
Available Actions
A. Commands
Choose a command from the drop-down menu:
Command
Description
APP UPDATE
Remotely update the DvPayLite app
PARAM UPDATE
Perform a remote parameter update on the terminal
REBOOT
Restart the merchant’s terminal remotely
GET TIME
Retrieve the current time set on the terminal
SET TIME
Set the time on the terminal remotely
APP UPDATE:
Choose the
APP UPDATE
command.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
A banner will be displayed on the terminal before the command is executed.
If you leave the banner untouched, the terminal will automatically execute the command once the timer runs. You can also tap
UPDATE
to immediately execute the command or click
LATER
to ignore it.
The result will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
PARAM UPDATE:
Choose the
PARAM UPDATE
command.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
A banner will be displayed on the terminal before the command is executed.
If you leave the banner untouched, the terminal will automatically execute the command once the timer runs. You can also tap
UPDATE
to immediately execute the command or click
LATER
to ignore it.
The result will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
REBOOT:
Choose the
REBOOT
command.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
A banner will be displayed on the terminal before the command is executed.
If you leave the banner untouched, the terminal will automatically execute the command once the timer runs. You can also tap
UPDATE
to immediately execute the command or click
LATER
to ignore it.
The result will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
GET TIME:
Choose the
GET TIME
command.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
The time currently set on the terminal will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
SET TIME:
Choose the
SET TIME command
.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Choose the time zone you wish to set on the terminal
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
A banner will be displayed on the terminal before the command is executed.
If you leave the banner untouched, the terminal will automatically execute the command once the timer runs. You can also tap
UPDATE
to immediately execute the command or click
LATER
to ignore it.
The result will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
B. Communication
You can send instant messages or images to the merchant’s terminal.
1. Instant Message
Enter the desired message in the text field.
“
Display until user acknowledges
” is enabled by default, so the message stays on the terminal screen until the merchant interacts with it. You can check or uncheck this option to change that behavior.
2. Image Upload
Upload an image.
Ensure the image matches the
resolution
and
file size requirements
displayed for each device model.
“
Keep showing until user interacts
” is enabled by default, so the message or image stays on the terminal screen until the merchant interacts with it. You can check or uncheck this option to change that behavior.
Steps to Execute:
Choose
Instant Message
or
Image Upload
.
Enter the required details or upload the file.
(Optional) Enable/disable “
Keep showing until user interacts.
”
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Limitations
TPN Limit
: Maximum of
10 actions per TPN per day
.
User TPN Limit
: Maximum of
30 actions across all TPNs per user per day
Action (Command / Communication)
Limit per TPN (per day)
APP UPDATE
2
PARAM UPDATE
2
REBOOT
2
GET TIME
2
SET TIME
2
Instant Message
2
Image Upload
2
Bug Fixes

pages.text:
Apple Pay Support in Freedom to Design | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays

Apple Pay Support in Freedom to Design
Apple Pay Support in Freedom to Design
Merchants using
Freedom to Design
with the embedded checkout option can now accept payments through
Apple Pay
.
Prerequisites
Merchant must have an
Apple Developer Account
to register domains for Apple Pay.
Merchant must have access to their
domain hosting
to upload the verification file.
Only domains or subdomains that can be verified through Apple Pay will be eligible.
How to Enable
Log in to
ipospays.com
using a
merchant admin account
. If you have access to the account, you can do this, or your merchant can do it themselves.
Go to
Settings → Payment Methods → Apple Pay → Manage
.
On the new page, click
Add New Domain
.
Enter the domain where Apple Pay will be enabled:
a. Use the top-level domain (e.g., denovosystem.tech) or
b. A subdomain (e.g., payment.denovosystem.tech).
Download the
verification file
provided.
Host the file at the exact location specified on your domain.
Once the file is placed, click
Verify
.
For full details of the API changes, including request/response structures, please refer to the updated documentation here:
Freedom to Design

.

:

Transaction Type
Card Type
Default

Under List By, choose one of:
Transaction Type
Card Type
Default

Save
.

parameter update
on the payment terminal for the changes to take effect.

Additional Options in Invoice Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays

Additional Options in Invoice Module
Additional Options in Invoice Module
This update introduces new actions for managing invoices for merchant accounts on iPOSpays. Users now have more flexibility when handling
expired
and
pending
invoices.
Expired Invoices – New Options
Resend Link
– Send the payment link again to the customer.
Mark As Paid
– Update the invoice status as paid.
Delete Invoice
– Remove the invoice permanently.
Pending Invoices – New Options
Mark As Paid
– Update the invoice status as paid.
Delete Invoice
– Remove the invoice permanently.

Card Brand Mids Now Mandatory for 3d Secure | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays

Card Brand MIDs Now Mandatory for 3D Secure
Card Brand MIDs Now Mandatory for 3D Secure
This update makes all Card Brand MIDs mandatory when configuring 3D Secure in the Add-On Features module within iPOSpays.
Why
Ensures that all merchants using 3D Secure have valid
Card Brand MIDs
configured.

Merchants
.

pages.title: Expanded Multi Mid Support for Cloudpos with Pin Pad | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Expanded Multi Mid Support for Cloudpos with Pin Pad | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Expanded Multi MID Support for CloudPOS with PIN Pad
Expanded Multi MID Support for CloudPOS with PIN pad
This update extends Multi MID (Merchant ID) support to merchants using CloudPOS with an external PIN pad. Previously available only on SPIn or standalone terminal setups, this enhancement allows merchants to seamlessly switch between multiple MIDs during a transaction initiated from the CloudPOS interface.
Feature Compatibility
Required POS Build
: 10130 and above
Supported Processors
: Not Applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not Applicable
What’s New?
CloudPOS configurations with PIN pad devices can now route transactions to the correct Merchant ID (Linked to the TPN) as selected by the user.
Transactions are processed using the selected MID, while all device communication still flows through the primary TPN (Terminal Profile Number).
No changes required on the hardware side—just configure Multi MID at the portal level.
Works for all supported processors with Multi MID capabilities.
Each store’s transactions remain isolated and accessible only by that store’s merchant credentials. This ensures secure, role-based access even when multiple MIDs are managed in one system.
Key Benefits:
Simplifies transaction routing for merchants operating across multiple business entities.
Eliminates the need to log in and out or swap hardware to change MIDs.
Improves transaction-level reconciliation and reporting accuracy.
How to Set Up Multi MID for CloudPOS Merchants
Follow the same steps as enabling Multi MID for POS terminals, but ensure the TPNs used are CloudPOS.
How to Set Up Multi-MID for Your Merchants
Enable SPIn for CloudPOS TPN
To activate CloudPOS Multi MID, SPIn must be enabled for the master TPN:
Log in to the iPOSpays portal as an ISO.
Go to
S.T.E.A.M
→
Edit Parameter
.
Search for and select the master CloudPOS TPN.
Click
Edit Parameter
→
Integration
.
Enable
SPIn
and select
Cloud
as the SPIn Mode.
Choose your PIN pad device from the drop-down menu that appears and click
add.
Click
Save
, enter the TPN on the connected PIN pad terminal, and perform a parameter update.
How to Use
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using merchant credentials.
Go to
CloudPOS → Payments
.
From the
TPN dropdown
, select the desired TPN linked to the MID you wish to transact with.
Choose
Pinpad
as the payment type and enter the transaction amount.
If fees are enabled, a confirmation prompt will appear. Click
Okay
to proceed.
Click
Process
to send the transaction to the connected PIN pad.
The customer completes the payment on the terminal.
To process a transaction for a different MID, select another TPN and repeat the steps.
The device will show a Profile Loading animation whenever the merchant profile is switched.
All transactions can be viewed under the
Transactions
module.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Street Check in Card Validation Risk Rule | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Street Check in Card Validation Risk Rule | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!

Bug Fixes
Street Check-In Card Validation Risk Rule
Street Check in Card Validation Risk Rule
A new enhancement has been added to the iPOS S.A.F.E
Card Validation risk rule—Street Check
. When enabled, this parameter ensures stricter AVS (Address Verification Service) validation by checking the street address provided during a transaction.
If the street address does not match what’s on file with the card issuer, the transaction will be declined.
This update gives ISOs and merchants greater control over fraud prevention, particularly for
card-not-present
transactions.
Feature Compatibility
Required Build
: Not Applicable
Supported Processors
: Not Applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not Applicable
How to access:
Log in to the
iPOSpays Portal
using your ISO account credentials.
Go to iPOS S.A.F.E → Risk rules → Card Validation → Enable/Disable
Street Check
.

New UI for Generic Hosted Payment Page | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!

New UI for Generic Hosted Payment Page
New UI for Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP)
We’ve updated the UI of the Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP) to provide a more seamless and consistent experience across all iPOSpays platforms. This refreshed design aligns with the new color palette introduced last year and visually matches the Send Payment Link page.
What’s New:
Updated UI
– A cleaner, more intuitive layout for easier navigation and a consistent look across payment tools.
Expanded Customizable Tags
– Now add up to 5 customizable tags (previously 2) to better track and categorize transactions. Each tag can be configured as Mandatory or optional, giving merchants more control.

Required Build

: Not Applicable

Log in to the iPOSpays Portal using your merchant credentials.
Navigate to
Settings
→
Generate Payment Form
→
URL Generation
.
Select the desired
TPN (Terminal Profile Number)
from the drop-down list.
Click
Generate URL
, then
Copy URL

optional
or
mandatory
.

pages.title: Storewise Settlement Email Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Storewise Settlement Email Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Storewise Settlement Email Report
Storewise Settlement Email Report
Merchants can now receive a consolidated settlement report directly via email, right after batch settlement. This enhancement ensures improved visibility and reconciliation across all devices and TPNs under each store.
Feature Compatibility
Required Build
: Not Applicable
Supported Processors
: Not Applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not Applicable
What’s New?
After a batch is settled, the iPOSpays portal automatically sends a consolidated settlement report to the email addresses configured under the merchant’s account. Reports can be scheduled for daily summaries or sent instantly upon settlement.
How to Set It Up
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using the merchant account.
Go to:
Settings
→
Settlement Report via Email
→
Enable
Choose one or both of the following options:
Send a Consolidated End-of-Day Report
: A combined batch report for all TPNs under the store, sent daily at 4:00 a.m. EST.
Send an Instant TPN Report
: Immediately sends a batch settlement report upon settlement.
Merchant-Wide Emails
: Add up to 15 recipient emails for consolidated merchant-level reports.
Enable Store Alerts
: Specify unique recipient emails for individual stores. This allows store managers or location-specific staff to receive reports only relevant to their assigned store.
Store
: Enter the specific store
Email
: Enter the specific email
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
The settlement report will be sent after the batch has been settled to the mentioned email address.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Enhanced Ebt Capabilities Contactless Payments | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Enhanced Ebt Capabilities Contactless Payments | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays

iPOSgo!

Enhanced EBT Capabilities: Contactless Payments
Enhanced EBT Capabilities: Contactless Payments
Until now, EBT transactions on DVPayLite were limited to swipe and manual entry. With this release, merchants can now accept EBT cards using EMV chip (contact & contactless) methods—just like any other modern payment type.
No need to change terminals or hardware—if chip and tap are supported on your current device, you’re ready to go.
Feature Compatibility
Required POS Build
: 10137
Supported Processors
: TSYS, Fiserv North & Elavon
Supported Fee Types
: Not applicable
What’s New?
EBT payments now support EMV chip transactions
PIN entry is mandatory for all EBT transactions
Magstripe fallback is allowed when chip fails
How to Enable
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account
Go to S.T.E.A.M → Edit Parameters
Use the search box to find and select the desired POS TPN
Click on Edit Parameter → Transactions, then set EBT EMV to “Yes”.
Click
Save
and perform a parameter update on the payment terminal for the changes to take effect.

pages.title: Store Level Ebt Number Configuration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Store Level Ebt Number Configuration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal

Store-Level EBT Number Configuration
Store-Level EBT Number Configuration
The EBT Number field has been moved from the
Merchant
level to the

level in the iPOSpays portal.
Why the change?
For businesses operating across multiple states, each store may need its own EBT number. This update makes sure each location is set up correctly—without requiring multiple merchant accounts.
What changes in the portal?

The EBT field is no longer editable at the Merchant level
Where to access
: iPOSpays portal with ISO/Agent Office credentials → Merchants → Search and select the desired merchant using the search box → Stores → DBA → Edit Store.

Store Info
section when onboarding a new merchant.

pages.title: Recurring Payments Now with Custom Frequency Option | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Recurring Payments Now with Custom Frequency Option | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Recurring Payments Now with Custom Frequency Option
Recurring Payments – Now With “Custom” Frequency Option
Merchants can now go beyond standard recurring schedules like weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly. With the new Custom option, they can define a specific interval—say, every 10 days, every 45 days, or any frequency that fits their customers’ billing needs.
This enhancement gives merchants greater control and flexibility when setting up recurring payments, helping them tailor plans to unique customer agreements or non-standard billing cycles.
Feature Compatibility
Required POS Build
: Not applicable
Supported Processors
: Not applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not applicable
How to Set Up a Custom Recurring Schedule
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using your merchant account credentials.
From the dashboard, go to “
My Customers
.”
Use the search bar to find the customer by name, phone number, or email.
Click “
More
” on the right and select “
Add Recurring Payment.
”
Enter the recurring payment amount.
Select the appropriate
Terminal Profile Number (TPN)
.
Choose the customer’s preferred payment method —
Card
or
Bank (ACH)
.
Add an
external reference ID
for easy reconciliation.
From the
Frequency
dropdown, select
Custom
.
“Repeat By” will be set to
Days
by default and cannot be modified for any other value.
In
Repeat Every
, specify the number of days between each payment (e.g., 30 for monthly).
Set the
start and end dates
using the calendar tool.
Click “
Setup Recurring Payment
” to complete the process.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: In App Sdk for Iposgo iOS | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
In App Sdk for Iposgo iOS | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
In-App SDK for iPOSgo iOS
In App SDK for iPOSgo! (iOS)
The iPOSgo! In App SDK for iOS enables third-party applications to initiate and complete payments directly—without requiring the iPOSgo! app to be installed on the device. This provides a more seamless and embedded experience for white-labeled or fully integrated POS solutions.
Feature Compatibility
Supported Devices
: iPhone Xs or later.
Required iOS Version
: iOS 16.9 or later. It’s recommended to use the latest version for optimal performance.
Supported Processors
: The iPOSgo! (iOS) app only supports TSYS and Fiserv Omaha
Supported Fee Types
: Supports all fee types
The SDK is ideal for developers building iOS-native applications and offers the following benefits:
Direct access to payment functionality without app switching
No dependency on Deep Linking or installed apps
Streamlined, embedded checkout flow within the host application
Enhanced user experience through full in-app control
To use the SDK, integrators must obtain an entitlement from Apple using an organization account. The approval process typically takes about two weeks.
Check out the SDK here:
In App SDK Integration
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Iposgo Deep Linking Sdk V2 | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Iposgo Deep Linking Sdk V2 | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
iPOSgo Deep Linking SDK v2
iPOSgo! Deep Linking SDK V2
Version 2 of the iPOSgo! Deep Linking SDK for iOS includes new parameters to give integrators greater control over the in-app transaction flow. These enhancements enable a more tailored and seamless checkout experience when using Tap to Pay on iPhone.
Feature Compatibility
Supported Devices
: iPhone Xs or later.
Required iOS Version
: iOS 16.9 or later. It’s recommended to use the latest version for optimal performance.
Supported Processors
: The iPOSgo! (iOS) app only supports TSYS and Fiserv Omaha
Supported Fee Types
: Supports all fee types
What’s New in V2?
Developers can now configure individual transaction elements for a more seamless and app-aligned payment experience:
tipAmount – Send a custom tip amount per transaction
feeAmount – Send a custom fee amount per transaction
showTipScreen – Choose whether to show the tip selection screen
showBreakUpScreen – Choose whether to show the breakup screen
showApprovalScreen – Choose whether to show the approval screen after transaction completion
Previously, the SDK accepted only a combined tip and fee amount.
Version 2 enables granular control
by allowing each to be passed separately.
Check out the SDK here:
iOS Deep Linking Integration - Version 2.0
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Expanded Worldpay Support 3ds Authentication and Hsa Card Acceptance | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Expanded Worldpay Support 3ds Authentication and Hsa Card Acceptance | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Expanded WorldPay Support: 3DS Authentication & HSA Card Acceptance
Expanded WorldPay Support: 3DS Authentication & HSA Card Acceptance
Merchants using the WorldPay processor now benefit from two major enhancements designed to improve both payment security and customer flexibility:
1. 3D Secure (3DS) Authentication for Online Transactions
WorldPay now supports
3D Secure
(3DS) authentication for online and card-not-present payments. This advanced security protocol prompts cardholders to verify their identity during the transaction process, significantly reducing the risk of fraud and unauthorized activity.
By enabling 3DS, merchants gain:
Enhanced transaction security through real-time cardholder verification
Reduced exposure to chargebacks and fraudulent disputes
Compliance with global eCommerce security standards, especially critical for high-risk or regulated industries
This update is especially valuable for merchants in sectors like digital services, healthcare, and government-related payments, where additional verification is a compliance requirement or best practice.
2. Support for Health Savings Account (HSA) Cards
Merchants can now
accept HSA cards processed via WorldPay
, expanding payment method flexibility for businesses in eligible verticals such as healthcare, wellness, and pharmacy services.
These enhancements mark a significant step forward in making WorldPay a more versatile and secure payment option across various industries.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Check as a Payment Type | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Check as a Payment Type | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Check as a Payment Type
Check as a Payment Type
Minimum Required POS Build: 10133
With this update, merchants can now log checks as a valid payment type directly on their POS terminals. While checks are processed offline and not settled through the payment gateway, every check transaction is still recorded under the merchant’s TPN in the iPOSpays portal, ensuring accurate reporting and cleaner reconciliation.
The flow mirrors a cash sale: merchants simply enter the check amount and tap to record the payment. This helps reduce manual tracking, cuts down reconciliation errors, and keeps the full transaction history intact across all payment types.
This feature is currently available only for merchants on the
Tender-Based Pricing
fee program.
These transactions
will not be batched or funded
, as they’re intended for recordkeeping only.
Batch reports will display check transactions in a
separate section
, clearly labeled for easy visibility.
How to Enable
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using an
ISO
account.
Navigate to
S.T.E.A.M → Edit Parameters.
Use the search box to find and select the desired
TPN
, then click
Edit Parameter
.
Go to
Transactions
→
Card Type
, and enable
Check
.
Click
Save
, then perform a
parameter update
on the merchant’s payment terminal for the changes to take effect.
How to Use
On the terminal’s
idle payment screen
, enter the transaction amount and tap
OK
.
If
fees, taxes, or breakdown parameters
are enabled for the TPN, a detailed breakdown screen will appear. Tap
PROCEED
to continue.
Select
Check
as the payment method.
The transaction will be completed and recorded.
The transaction receipt will also have
Type: Check
printed on it for easy identification.
You can view the transaction record by logging into
iPOSpays
(merchant or ISO account) and navigating to:
Transactions → Filter icon → Alternative Payments
The check transaction will appear here automatically.
Batches → Alternative Payments
All check transactions are also listed here for reconciliation.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Itemized Billing on Send Payment Link | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Itemized Billing on Send Payment Link | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Itemized Billing on Send Payment Link
Itemized Billing on Send Payment Link
Merchants can now offer detailed, transparent billing using the
Send Payment Link
feature with
itemized billing
support.
What’s New?
This enhancement allows merchants to list each product or service individually, along with pricing, discounts, tax, and quantity, ensuring that customers have a clear view of what they’re being charged for.
Key Components in the Payment Link
Item Name / Description
– Identifies each product or service clearly.
Price & Quantity
– Displays unit price and number of units.
Discounts / Adjustments
– Reflects any applied deductions.
Subtotal & Taxes
– Shows pre-tax total and applicable taxes or fees.
Grand Total
– The final amount due.
This feature increases transparency, builds customer trust, and reduces billing-related disputes.
How to Use
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using a merchant account.
Navigate to
CloudPOS → Payments
.
Pre-requisite
: Ensure your inventory is set up correctly.
Enter Customer Details
Choose the following options:
TPN
: Select the CloudPOS TPN to record the transaction.
Action
: Choose
Itemized Billing
.
Payment Type
: Select
Send Payment Link
.
Transaction Type
: Choose
Sale
.
Under
Customer
, enter:
Phone or Email
(At least one must be provided. The other will become optional.)
First Name (mandatory)
Optional: Last Name, DBA, Zip Code, City, State
Use
Reset
to clear all fields or
Save
to continue.
Add Order Details
In the
Product Name
field, search and select the item.
The system auto-fills
Cash/Card Price
and
Description
based on your inventory setup.
Enter the
Quantity
requested.
Optionally, apply a
Discount
by clicking the
% icon
, and choose between a fixed amount or a percentage.
Click
⊕
to add more products to the order.
To remove a product, click the 🗑️
icon
.
You can also add a
Shipping Fee
to the total.
Click
Next
to proceed to the payment details.
Add Payment Details
A full breakdown of the order will appear; click Okay to continue.
Fill in the following:
Phone or Email
(At least one must be provided. The other will become optional.)
CC Emails
(Optional)
Link Expiration Time
: Choose 1 day, 1 week, or 1 month
Reference Number
Order Description
Disclaimer
for the payment page
Option to
Upload a Custom Invoice
Click
Reset
to start over, or
Send Payment Link
to deliver the invoice via email or SMS.
Customer Experience
The customer will receive a payment link and can open it on their mobile or desktop to review the invoice and complete the transaction securely.
Invoice
On the merchant login, you can go to CloudPOS → Invoices to locate the invoice for the transaction that was just completed.
The invoice will look like this 👇

pages.title: New Terms and Conditions Enforcement on Login | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
New Terms and Conditions Enforcement on Login | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!

Bug Fixes
New Terms and Conditions Enforcement on Login
New Terms and Conditions Enforcement on Login
To reinforce legal compliance and improve data protection standards, a new
Terms and Conditions
agreement has been implemented on the iPOSpays login page.
Key Highlights:
Mandatory Acceptance
: All users must agree to the Terms and Conditions via a checkbox to proceed with login.
Visibility
: A direct hyperlink allows users to view the full agreement before acceptance.
Persistent Validation
: Once accepted, the agreement is logged with a timestamp and won’t be prompted again unless the content is updated.
Backend Support
: Acceptance is securely recorded in the database to ensure consistent enforcement.
Exclusion
: This feature is not applicable when using the “Login As” functionality.
Non-bypassable
: The agreement must be accepted to access any iPOSpays modules; it cannot be dismissed otherwise.
This update enhances transparency and ensures all users acknowledge the platform’s usage policies prior to accessing their accounts.
Bug Fixes

pages.text:

Introduction
iPOSpays

3D Secure Management Moved to Add-On Features
3D Secure Management Moved to Add-On Features
To simplify configuration, ISOs can now
enable
or
disable 3D Secure
(3DS) for merchant stores directly from the
Add-On Features
section. Previously, this setting was only accessible via the
Integrations
drop-down menu during merchant onboarding. This change makes it easier to manage 3D Secure settings anytime without needing to revisit onboarding screens.
How to Enable 3D Secure for Your Merchants
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using your ISO account credentials.
Navigate to the
Merchants
module and use the search box to find the merchant you want to update.
Select the merchant, then choose the
desired store
. Click on the store’s
DBA name
to reveal the drop-down menu and choose
Add On Features.
Inside the
Add On Features
section, toggle the
3D Secure
button to enable it for the chosen store.
If you see a message indicating you don’t have access to enable 3D Secure, please contact your Dejavoo representative to request the necessary permissions.
If there’s only one sponsor bank configured for you, it will be selected automatically in the Sponsored 1 drop-down button.
If there are two sponsor banks, you will be able to choose from the Sponsored 1  drop-down button.
Your sponsor bank BINs are displayed here by default for reference on all stores.
You can then enter the merchant’s ID for Visa, Amex, Mastercard, and Discover. Note that if any of these IDs, such as Visa, is not provided, transactions made with that card type will not be protected by 3D Secure.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.

permission, they can now save a new template even after selecting an existing one.

pages.title: Settle by Store | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Settle by Store | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Settle by Store
Settle By Store
Required POS Build: 10127
Settle By Store
is a powerful feature that enables merchants with multiple
TPNs
under a single store to settle batches instantly for all
TPNs
at once or select specific ones to settle. This eliminates the need to manually locate and settle each
TPN/device
individually, saving time and improving efficiency.
Enabling Settle By Store for Merchants
To enable
Settle By Store for the merchant’s payment terminal TPN
, follow these steps:
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using an
ISO account
.
Navigate to
S.T.E.A.M → Edit Parameters
.
Use the
search box
at the top of the screen to find and select the
merchant’s payment terminal TPN.
.
Click on
Edit Parameter → Settlement → Enable/Disable Settle By Store
.
Follow these steps on all the TPNs you wish to enable this feature for within the store.
Using Settle By Store in CloudPOS
This feature is available by default for
CloudPOS TPNs
and does not require ISOs to enable any additional options for it to function.
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using a
merchant account
.
Navigate to
Batches
.
If the merchant has multiple stores, use the
search box
to find and select the specific
merchant store
.
Click
Settle Store
→ A tab will appear displaying the
TPNs
linked to the store.
Check/uncheck the
TPNs
you want to include in the batch settlement.
Click
Settle
to finalize the process.
The batch status will update from
Yet to Start
to
Batch Settled
.
Using Settle By Store on a Payment Terminal
From the
idle payment screen
, tap ☆ to access
Favorites
.
Select
Show/Settle Batch
.
A prompt will appear with two options:
Settle Store
: Settles the batch for all
TPNs
linked to the same store.
Settle TPN
: Settles the batch for the
specific TPN
registered to the payment terminal.
Choose
Settle Store
→ Tap
OK
.
Select
all TPNs
or choose specific ones using the search box, then tap
Settle
to process the batch settlement.
To print the batch report for the selected
TPNs
, tap the
print icon
at the top of the screen.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Digital Ads on Screen | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Digital Ads on Screen | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Digital Ads on Screen
Introducing Digital Ad on Screen
Required POS Build: 10127
The
Digital Ad on Screen
feature allows merchants to display advertisements on their terminals when the screen is idle or display the ads during the transaction process depending on device capabilities.
Requirements:
The device must have at least
50% battery capacity
or be
plugged into a charger
if the battery falls below 50% in order for ads to display.
Accessing Digital Ad on Screen
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using a
merchant account
.
Navigate to
Digital Ads → Digital Ad on Screen
.
To create a campaign:
Click +
New Campaign
if setting it up for the first time. (Or)
Click the
menu button
in the top-left corner and select +
Add Campaign
.
Creating a Campaign
Name the Campaign
Enter a
custom name
in the relevant field.
Set the Campaign Duration
Choose a
start and end date
using the calendar view.
Select
Never
for the end date to run the campaign indefinitely or pick a custom date.
Choose When to Display the Ad
Idle Screen
: Displays the ad only when the terminal is idle.
Always on Screen
: Keeps the ad visible at all times.
Device Compatibility:
Idle Screen
: Supported on
P-Series terminals (P1, P3, P5, P8)
and
QD-Series terminals (QD2, QD4, QD5)
.
Always on Screen
: Currently supported only on P18. (This device is not available for purchase yet)
Upload the Ad File
Ensure the file matches the
resolution and file size
requirements displayed on the screen for each device model.
Supported file formats:
JPEG, JPG, PNG, GIF
.
Click
Save
to apply the changes.
Assigning Stores and TPNs
After clicking
Save
, a prompt will appear asking if you wish to assign
stores and TPNs
to the campaign you just created. Click
Yes
to proceed or
Later
to exit.
Assigning Stores and TPNs Steps:
Click
Yes
to proceed.
Choose a
store
from the drop-down menu.
Assigning the ad to a store will automatically apply it to
all TPNs
under that store.  If you want to prevent certain TPNs from displaying the digital ad, you can manually search for them and exclude them.
Click
Assign
to finalize the assignment.
If multiple campaigns have overlapping dates, the most recently
created/updated campaign
will override older ones and be displayed on the POS.
You can
edit, reassign, or delete campaigns
after the setup is completed.
Perform a
parameter update
on the payment terminal for changes to take effect.
Once the device is idle, the ad will automatically appear on the screen (Ads can be set to always on display for P18 terminals).
Managing Active Campaigns
View all active campaigns in the
Digital Ad on Screen
module.
Edit campaign name, including images, and duration.
Reassign campaigns to different stores and TPNs as needed.
Delete campaigns that are no longer required.
Troubleshooting
If an ad does not appear on the device, ensure the
parameter update
was successfully performed.
Verify that the campaign dates are
correct and active
.
Check that the terminal is
connected to the network
for updates.
Bug Fixes

We’re excited to introduce

on

, allowing merchants to accept Apple Pay as a payment method when
sending payment links
and processing transactions through
checkout pages
. This update enhances the payment experience by providing a faster, more secure, and seamless checkout process for customers.
Where Apple Pay is Supported:
Apple Pay is now available across all
iPOSpays-supported
payment platforms using
TSYS and Elavon processors
, including:
Send Payment Link
– Accept payments via links sent directly to customers.
Hosted Payment Page (HPP)
– Enable Apple Pay on hosted checkout pages.
Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP)
– Provide Apple Pay as an option for a broad range of merchants.
Freedom to Design
– Customize checkout experiences while integrating Apple Pay.
WooCommerce iPOSpays Payment Gateway Plugin
– Seamlessly integrate Apple Pay into WooCommerce-powered stores.
With this update, merchants can offer a
faster checkout experience, reduce cart abandonment
, and
enhance security
with
Apple’s tokenization and encryption technology
.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Template Management on Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Template Management on Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Template Management on iPOSpays
Template Management on iPOSpays
The
Edit Parameter
section of the iPOSpays portal enables ISOs and Agent Offices to enable/disable parameters for a particular TPN and save them as a template and assign the same configurations to other similar TPNs. This update takes that feature a step further, enabling them to create as well as edit, and delete the templates.
This update is available to default ISO admins and Agent Office users, who can enable this feature as a privilege when onboarding an add-on user.
How to Enable for an Add-On User (ISO)
Login to the iPOSpays portal using the default ISO admin account.
Go to
User Management
→ Menu Button → Add User
Enter the add-on user’s name, email address, phone number and choose
ISO Admin
as the “User Role.” (Template privileges are only available for ISO Admin and not for ISO Operator user roles.)
Click on “+Template” from under
Modules and Privileges
→ Choose the privileges for the user:
Create Template, Edit Template and Delete Template
.
Click
Submit
for the changes to take effect.
How to Create, Edit & Delete Templates
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO/Agent Office account with template privileges.
Go to
S.T.E.A.M → Edit Parameters.
Use the search box to find and select the desired TPN →
Edit Parameter
.
After configuring the parameters for the chosen TPN →
Save as Template.
Name the template and
click
* Save to create the template.
If you wish to edit the template, simply click
Save as Template
again, and you will have two options:
a.
Rewrite in Existing Template
: To apply any changes you have made to the existing template.
b.
Save as new one
: Save the updated parameter configurations separately as a new template altogether.
The templates can be accessed from the
Apply Template
drop-down menu at the top right corner of the screen.
Bug Fixes

Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.

Click on

A disclaimer will also appear if the selected TPNs do not have a build that matches their assigned build. Click

The ability to add/manage value added apps from the Edit Parameters section remains unchanged and any changes made from the DvStore module will automatically be reflected here and vice versa.

pages.title: Ipospays Secured by Memcyco | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ipospays Secured by Memcyco | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
