IPOSpays Secured by Memcyco
iPOSpays Secured by Memcyco
We’re excited to announce a significant upgrade to the security of iPOSpays, designed to provide our users with an even safer and more reliable experience. We’ve integrated Memcyco’s cutting-edge phishing protection technology to actively safeguard your valuable data.
What is Memcyco and Why is it Important?
Memcyco
specializes in real-time, proactive threat detection, specifically targeting phishing attacks. These attacks often involve deceptive websites that mimic legitimate platforms like iPOSpays, aiming to steal your login credentials and sensitive information. Memcyco’s advanced AI-powered algorithms continuously monitor the web for these fraudulent sites.
How Does it Benefit iPOSpays Users?
Real-Time Phishing Detection
: Memcyco actively scans for and identifies phishing websites that impersonate iPOSpays.
Proactive User Alerts
: If a potential phishing site is detected, iPOSpays users will receive immediate alerts, warning them of the danger.
Enhanced Data Security
: By preventing users from falling victim to phishing attacks, we’re significantly strengthening the security of your data and transactions.
Increased Peace of Mind
: Knowing that iPOSpays is protected by Memcyco’s robust technology allows you to use our platform with greater confidence.
Continuous Security Updates
: Memcyco’s threat intelligence is constantly updated, ensuring ongoing protection against evolving phishing tactics.
This integration represents our ongoing commitment to providing a secure and trustworthy platform for all iPOSpays users. We believe this added layer of protection will significantly mitigate the risks associated with phishing, allowing you to focus on what matters most.
Key Improvements:
Added Memcyco integration for real-time phishing detection.
Implemented user alerts for potential phishing website threats.
Enhanced overall data security and user protection.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: 3d Secure on Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
3d Secure on Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
Enhance Security With 3D Secure on iPOSpays
3D Secure (3DS) is a global security standard designed to add an extra layer of protection to online card transactions. By requiring additional authentication, 3DS significantly reduces the risk of fraudulent activity.
We’ve integrated this advanced security feature into
Send Payment Link, Hosted Payment Page (HPP),
and the
Generic Hosted Payment Page
(GHPP), ensuring the highest standards of payment security and providing customers with a safer online transaction experience.
Enabling 3D Secure for Merchants
For this feature to work, the ISO must activate the 3DS for their merchants on the iPOSpays portal.
Prerequisites
To enable 3DS for all necessary card brands (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, etc.), the following information is required from your sponsor bank(s).
If details for a specific brand are not submitted, 3DS will not be activated for that brand.
What is the Acquirer BIN?
It is a 6-digit number assigned to the Acquirer, by the card schemes, so the Acquirer can process payments. Each merchant will have a unique acquirer BIN for Visa, Mastercard, AMEX, and Discover.
First digit identifies the scheme
Visa begins with 4xxxxx
Mastercard begins with 5xxxxx and recently added 2xxxxx-series BINs
AMEX begins with 3xxxxx
Discover begins with 6xxxxx
Acquiring banks/processors have a BIN assigned to them from each card network. This identifies the acquirer/processor to the networks and holds the acquirer/processor financially responsible for settlement and risk.
An Acquirer BIN
IS NOT an ICA
. An ICA is a 4 - 6-digit number assigned by MC but is a level (hierarchy) above the Acquirer BIN.
What does the merchant need to provide?
3DS requires the merchant acquirer BIN for each card scheme used for 3DS.
Every merchant with a merchant account will use the ‘Acquiring BIN’ for each card scheme in order to transaction/process payments.
The merchant processor/gateway/ISO/Acquirer will assign the Merchant a Merchant ID and will assign the Acquirer BIN for that merchant to accept and process payments.
The gateway/processor most likely has direct access to the Acquirer. For that processor/gateway to process payments, the Acquirer would have to assign them an Acquirer BIN (assigned to that Acquirer by each card scheme).
Why is it important for 3DS/CCA?
During 3DS authentication, the Acquirers Bank Identification Number (Merchant.acqBINfield) is required in the Verify Enrollment Request (VEReq). This field must be the merchant’s acquiring BIN for each card network.
The Merchant Identifier (ID) Number (Merchant.merID field) must be the Merchant ID assigned by the acquirer to the merchant for use in Visa, MC, Discover, and AMEX transactions.
The Merchant BIN field validates the merchant as a participating member of 3DS. If the BIN field is incorrect, the merchant will NOT receive 3DS program benefits.
If this value is not known, merchants need to ask their acquirer or merchant processor representative.
Who Can Help Find the Acquirer BINs?
The merchant can explain their relationship (direct or indirect) with their
Merchant Acquirer.
The merchant uses a processor, ISO/MSP or gateway and this will help
understand which service provider can furnish the Acquirer BINs.
An ISO/MSP is a 3rd party provider and NOT bank association members, and would be registered by the acquirer (using the acquirer’s assigned BINs from the networks)
The ISO is often the service provider best known by the merchant. Reviewing the ISO’s web site should expose the actual acquirer and the ISO should have contacts at this bank.
Send the Bank Identification Number (BIN) information below for ISO’s each sponsor bank
Acquirer ID
Description
Visa Acquirer ID
This is the Visa BIN . All Visa BINs will begin with ‘4’ and are 6 digits long. (4XXXXX).
MC Acquirer ID
This is the MasterCard SecureCode BIN . All BINs are 6 digits long and can begin with any digit since 2022.
JCB Acquirer ID
This is the JCB BIN . JCB BINs are typically 8 digits in length.
AMEX Acquirer ID
This is the Amex SE# or BIN . Amex BINs are 11 digits in length.
Diners or Discover Acquirer ID
This is the Diners or Discover BIN . All BINs are 6 digits long.
China Union Pay (UPI) Acquirer ID
This is the UPI BIN .
AMEX MID
A Merchant ID, commonly abbreviated as MID, is a 15 digit unique identification number assigned to a business or merchant by a payment processor.
Please ensure that the provided information is accurate and up-to-date. Once obtained, kindly send it to
product@dejavoo.io
.
How to Enable:
In order to enable 3DS, first you would need to enable the integration for the Merchant’s profile and then, enable the parameter on each desired TPN within this Merchant’s profile.
For Existing Merchants:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Select the
Merchant Module
Use the search box at the top to find and select the merchant for whom you wish to enable 3DS.
Click on the merchant DBA and select
Edit Merchant
.
Click on the Integrations drop-down menu and select
3D Secure
.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
For New Merchants:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Select the
Merchant Module
Click on the menu button on the top left corner inside the module. Select
Add Merchant
.
Fill out all required fields
Click on the
Integrations
drop-down menu and select
3D Secure
.
Enable from “Edit Parameter” for the TPN:
Click on S.T.E.A.M, then
Edit Parameters
.
Use the search box to find and select the TPN for which you desire to enable this feature. Then, click
Edit Parameter
.
Click on the
Integrations
Tab and enable
3D Secure
.
Click
Save
and perform a parameter update for the changes to take effect.
Enable 3D Secure from Edit Parameter -> Integrations
How does 3D Secure work on iPOSpays?
Transaction Initiation:
When a customer enters their card details on our secure hosted payment page (HPP) or generic hosted payment page (GHPP), their browser sends a request to our servers.
Authentication Request:
Our system forwards this request to their card issuer, who determines if 3DS authentication is required based on specific factors.
Cardholder Verification:
(if required): If 3DS authentication is necessary, the card issuer will redirect the customer to their secure authentication page. The customer will be prompted to provide additional authentication details to verify their identity using a one-time password sent to the customer’s phone number.
Transaction Completion:
Once their identity is verified by the card issuer, the transaction is completed securely.
Benefits of 3D Secure on iPOSpays
The implementation of 3DS will provide several benefits to ISOs and their merchants:
Enhanced Security:
Protects customer’s sensitive card information from unauthorized access.
Reduce Fraudulent Transactions:
3DS adds an extra layer of verification during online transactions, making it harder for fraudsters to use stolen card information.
Increase Authorization Rates:
By verifying the cardholder’s identity, 3DS can help reduce the number of declined transactions due to suspicion of fraud.
Improve Compliance:
Many card networks mandate 3DS compliance for card-not-present transactions. Implementing 3DS ensures adherence to these regulations.
Reduced Chargebacks:
Fraudulent transactions often lead to chargebacks, which incur fees for merchants. Implementing 3DS can significantly reduce these costs.
Improved Customer Confidence:
Increased security fosters trust among customers, leading to a better overall shopping experience.
Compatibility
This feature works on the following.
Send Payment Link
Hosted Payment Page
Generic Hosted Payment Page

pages.title: By the Store for Untipped and Server Reports | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
By the Store for Untipped and Server Reports | Release Notes iPOSpays
Want to Simplify Tip Management for Your Merchants?
Required POS Build: 10118
Managing tips across multiple TPNs just got easier! The new
Adjust by Store
enhancement allows your merchants to quickly access untipped and server reports for all TPNs within the same store—saving time and effort. Merchants can effortlessly identify TPNs with untipped transactions and use the server report to streamline tip pooling among employees – all in one place. Help your merchants simplify their tip management today!
Untipped and Server Report on a Dejavoo P3 Terminal
Compatibility:
Any Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminal.
Points to Keep in Mind:
This option needs to be enabled only for
payment terminals
and is not required for CloudPOS.
To View “Untipped “and “Server Report” for transactions tied to another TPN within the same store, the ISO must enable the “Tip” option for those TPNs in the
“Edit Parameters”
section.
How to Enable For a Payment Terminal:
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using an ISO account.
Click on S.T.E.A.M then “Edit Parameters.”
Search and select the payment terminal TPN for which you want to enable this feature.
Click Edit Parameter -> Tip & Taxes -> Enable “Tip” -> Enable “Adjust by store (POS only).” Click “Save” and perform a parameter update on the terminal for the changes to take effect.
How it works:
Untipped Transactions:
Merchants can now view untipped transactions either for a specific TPN (Terminal Processing Number) or for all TPNs associated with the same store by using the
By the Store
option.
How to Use:
Click on Favorites -> Reports -> Open Batch -> Un-Tipped Txn -> choose
By the Store
Server Reports:
Similarly, merchants can generate server reports for a single TPN or consolidate reports for all TPNs under the same store.
How to Use:
Click on Favorites -> Reports -> Open Batch -> Server Report -> Choose
By the Store

pages.title: L2 L3 Data in Spin Requests | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
L2 L3 Data in Spin Requests | Release Notes iPOSpays
Help Your Merchants Save Big With L2/L3 Data in SPIn Requests
Required POS Build: 10118
This enhancement allows merchants using SPIn integration to transmit Level 2 and Level 3 transaction data within SPIn requests from the cash register application to their integrated Dejavoo payment terminals. By sending this additional information to the processor, merchants can qualify for lower interchange fees.

pages.title: Auto Populate for Saved Cards on Google Pay | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Auto Populate for Saved Cards on Google Pay | Release Notes iPOSpays
Auto-Populate for Saved Cards on Google Pay
This update is for merchants using our secure Hosted Payment Page (HPP) or Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP). Before this update, when a merchant sent a payment link and the customer chose Google Pay as their preferred payment method, they would need to manually enter their street and ZIP code after entering their card details. Now, the street and ZIP code are automatically populated, making it easier and faster for customers to complete their transactions.

pages.title: Reminder Emails for Send Payment Link | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Reminder Emails for Send Payment Link | Release Notes iPOSpays
Reminder Emails for Send Payment Link
This update is for merchants using the Send Payment Link feature to process transactions. Merchants can choose from three expiration times for payment links: one day, one week, or one month. With this update, when a payment link is set to expire in one week or one month, a reminder email will automatically be sent to the customer’s phone number or email address one day before the link expires. This eliminates the need for merchants to manually remind customers about pending payments and approaching expiration, ensuring a smoother experience for both parties.

pages.title: Accurate Email Displayed in Invoice | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Accurate Email Displayed in Invoice | Release Notes iPOSpays
Accurate Email Displayed in Invoice
This update resolves an issue where, if a merchant updated a customer’s email address while sending a payment link (even when the phone number and email were already saved in the database) from their CloudPOS, the invoice for the transaction would display the old email instead of the newly entered one. With this fix, the email address on the invoice will now reflect the most recent one used during the transaction, ensuring accuracy.

pages.title: Accurate Tip and Tax Displayed for Itemized Billing Receipts | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Accurate Tip and Tax Displayed for Itemized Billing Receipts | Release Notes iPOSpays
Accurate Tip & Tax Displayed for Itemized Billing Receipts
This update addresses an issue in CloudPOS’s Itemized Billing feature. When Custom Fee (Fixed) and Tip were enabled for a TPN, the receipt did not accurately display the fee and tip amounts. With this fix, both the fee and tip will now be correctly reflected on the receipt.

pages.title: Special Characters Accurately Reflected in Email Communications | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Special Characters Accurately Reflected in Email Communications | Release Notes iPOSpays
Special Characters Accurately Reflected in Email Communications
This update addresses an issue where, when special characters in the middle of the merchant DBA (e.g., “Rec8ipt”) in the receipt header of the Edit Parameter section of the iPOSpays portal, they were not displayed correctly in the invoice after a send payment link transaction. Instead of appearing as intended, spaces were added between the special characters. This fix ensures that special characters are now accurately reflected in email communications.

pages.title: Fixed Downloading Filtering Report in Transactions Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fixed Downloading Filtering Report in Transactions Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fixed Downloading Filtering Report in Transactions Module
Previously, when users applied a filter in the “Transactions” module of the iPOSpays portal and clicked “Download Report,” the system would download the entire transaction report instead of the filtered results. This update resolves the issue, ensuring that only the filtered data is downloaded as intended.

pages.title: Fixed Last 4 Digits Pan Filter in Invoices Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fixed Last 4 Digits Pan Filter in Invoices Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fixed Last 4 Digits PAN Filter in Invoices Module
This update resolves an issue in the CloudPOS Invoices module where the PAN filter failed to display results when a merchant entered the last 4 digits of the PAN. The filter now functions correctly, ensuring accurate results are displayed.

pages.title: Consistent Data in Merchant Dashboard | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Consistent Data in Merchant Dashboard | Release Notes iPOSpays
Consistent Data in Merchant Dashboard
This update resolves an issue where the data displayed on the closed batch card in the merchant dashboard did not match the data shown when the “Closed Batch” was opened. The fix ensures that the information on the closed batch card and inside the batch is consistent and accurate.

pages.title: Renamed View by Summary to View by Trend | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Renamed View by Summary to View by Trend | Release Notes iPOSpays
Renamed “View by Summary” to “View by Trend”
This update renames the “View by Summary” filter within the cards of the iPOSpays Dashboard module to “View by Trend,” providing a clearer and more intuitive representation of data trends.

pages.title: Changed Default Filter within Dashboard Cards | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Changed Default Filter within Dashboard Cards | Release Notes iPOSpays
Changed Default Filter Within Dashboard Cards
Previously, when ISO or Agent Office users clicked on any card in the Dashboard module, the default filter was set to “View by Customer.” With this update, the “View by Device” filter is now displayed by default, ensuring a more streamlined user experience when interacting with dashboard cards.

pages.title: Ach Support in Recurring Payments | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ach Support in Recurring Payments | Release Notes iPOSpays
ACH Support in Recurring Payments
This update introduces ACH as a payment option for recurring payments. Previously, only card details could be used to set up recurring payments. Now, after securely tokenizing a customer’s bank account, merchants can set up recurring payments using the bank account number through the My Customers module.
How to Tokenize ACH:
The customer’s account details will be tokenized after completing an ACH payment, as there is no option to add the account details manually from the My Customers module. This will also save the card under the customer’s profile.
How to Use:
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account.
From the dashboard, go to “My Customers.”
Search for the customer’s name, phone number or email address from the search box at the top.
On the right side of the screen, click on “More” and select “Add Recurring Payment.”
Set Up Recurring Payment Using ACH:
Enter the payment amount
Select the Terminal Profile Number (TPN).
Select “Bank Accounts” from the Card Type drop-down menu
Select the desired bank account number from the drop-down menu. Optionally, enter an external reference ID.
Select the preferred option from the “Frequency” drop-down menu. A message will display when the next payment cycle will start.
Choose “Terminal Type,” which will be automatically set based on the previous transaction that was processed for this bank account.  It is important to note that the terminal type can only be changed if previous transactions were made using different terminal types.
For example, if the last transaction was an ACH transaction with a TEL ID, the terminal type will default to TEL. The terminal type can only be changed if previous transactions were made using different terminal types.
After setting the start and end dates, click “Setup Recurring Payment” to complete the process.
If you wish you can edit/cancel the recurring payment as well:
Go to CloudPOS -> Recurring Payments.
Search for the customer’s phone number or email address from the search box at the top.
Click “Edit Recurring Payment.”
You can change the frequency or delete the Recurring Payment.

pages.title: Email Templates Redesigned | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Email Templates Redesigned | Release Notes iPOSpays
Email Templates Redesigned
We’ve refreshed our email templates to align with our updated brand theme—purple! With this enhancement, all email communications sent from iPOSpays will feature a modernized look and feel, reflecting our new brand identity.

pages.title: Ach Refund Issue Resolved | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ach Refund Issue Resolved | Release Notes iPOSpays
ACH Refund Issue Resolved
This update addresses functionality issues with the
Paya ACH Refund
transactions. With this fix, merchants can now issue refunds using Paya ACH more seamlessly and without interruptions.

pages.title: Delete Customer Issue Resolved | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Delete Customer Issue Resolved | Release Notes iPOSpays
Delete Customer Issue Resolved
This update resolves an issue that prevented merchants from deleting customers within the “My Customer” module. Previously, the deletion process would become stuck in a continuous loading state. With this fix, merchants can now delete customers without interruption.

pages.title: L2 L3 Commodity Code Issue Resolved | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
L2 L3 Commodity Code Issue Resolved | Release Notes iPOSpays
L2/L3 Commodity Code Issue Resolved
This update resolves an issue where, in certain scenarios, selecting a commodity code from the “Other” category while setting up Level 2/Level 3 auto-filling from the iPOSpays portal resulted in an empty line item being transmitted to the TSYS processor. This update ensures the correct commodity code is transmitted in the line item.

pages.title: Whitelabel Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Whitelabel Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
White Label iPOSpays
ISOs can now offer the iPOSpays portal, POS terminals, and iPOSgo! (Tap to Pay on iPhone and Android) under their own brand identity, gaining full control over the appearance and user experience. Customize the platform to align with your brand and deliver an exclusive, tailored experience for your customers—enhancing loyalty and positioning your business as a comprehensive solution provider. Click on the link below to learn more about this feature.
Learn More

pages.title: Feed Activation from Portal | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Feed Activation from Portal | Release Notes iPOSpays
FEED Activation From Portal
With this update, we’ve introduced the ability to activate FEED directly from the portal. FEED uses webhook to provide live tracking for various platforms, including POS, CloudPOS, Tap to Pay on iPhone, and Tap to Pay on Android, enabling seamless monitoring and control from the iPOSPays portal.
How to Enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Go to “Settings” -> FEED.
Enter the merchant’s webhook URL -> Choose the preferred authentication: HMAC or Basic Authentication.
If the merchant has already provided the key, enter it into the designated field. If not, click the “Generate Key” checkbox and the key will be generated for you.
For Basic Auth, enter the “Client Key” and “Secret Key” provided by the merchant. If not, click the “Generate Key” checkbox and the keys will be generated for you. Click “Test” to complete the process.

pages.title: Dashboard as a Privilege | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Dashboard as a Privilege | Release Notes iPOSpays
Dashboard as a Privilege
With this update, ISOs and Merchant Admins can control access to the iPOSpays Dashboard by enabling or disabling it as a privilege when creating new users. This flexibility allows for more granular user management based on roles or operational requirements.
Note:
If the View Dashboard privilege is disabled for a Merchant Admin, all users created by that Admin will not have the View Dashboard privilege available. But if the Dashboard is enabled for the Admin, this user will have the capability to allow or revoke this privilege to the users they create.
How to Use (ISO Admin):
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Go to the
Merchants
module. Search and select the merchant using their DBA from the search box at the top.
Click “Edit Merchant.” Enable/Disable the “Dashboard” from the
Modules and Privileges
drop-down menu.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
How to Use (Merchant Admin):
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a Merchant Admin account.
Go to “User Management.”
For
new users
, click on the menu button -> Add User -> Enter the new user’s name, phone number, email address, and user role.
Disable “Dashboard” and “View Dashboard.” Click “Submit” to complete the process.
For
existing users
, search and select
the user
using their name/email address using the search box at the top.
Click “Edit User” -> Under “Modules and Privileges,” enable/disable the “Dashboard” and “View Dashboard.”
Click “Save” to complete the changes.
Note:
The Merchant Admin cannot enable or disable privileges in the “Modules and Privileges” section for an add-on user (Merchant Admin). However, privileges for other user roles (such as Merchant Operator, Multi-Store Manager, and Store Manager) can be configured.

pages.title: Cloudpos Customization | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Cloudpos Customization | Release Notes iPOSpays
CloudPOS Customization
Previously, merchants processing transactions on the
CloudPOS-Pay Now
screen were required to enter either the customer’s
phone number
or
email address
. With this update, merchants can now
customize
the requirement to make both fields either
optional
or
mandatory
based on their needs. This flexibility ensures smoother transactions, allowing customers who prefer not to provide a phone number or email address to complete payments seamlessly.
How to Enable:
Log in to the
iPOSpays
portal using a merchant account.
Go to
Settings -> Cloud POS
.
Set the requirement to:
Optional
: Allows transactions without the phone number and email address.
Mandatory
: Requires both the phone number and email address to complete a transaction.
Click
Save
.

pages.title: Get Customer Signature for Cloudpos Pin Pad | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Get Customer Signature for Cloudpos Pin Pad | Release Notes iPOSpays
Get Customer Signature for CloudPOS + PIN pad
POS build No - 10117
This update is for merchants using the SPIn cloud integration. A new checkbox has been added to the CloudPOS. When enabled before starting a transaction, the connected POS terminal will display a signature capture screen after the transaction has been completed. If the checkbox is unchecked, the signature capture screen will not appear.
How to Enable::
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account.
Click on CloudPOS, then “Payments.”
Set Action to “Quick Pay.”
Set Payment Type to “Pinpad” and configure the Payment Type and Print Receipt options as desired.
Select the Transaction Type.
Choose your preferred TPN from the drop-down menu.
Enter the transaction amount.
Enable the “
Get Customer Signature
” box to display the signature capture screen on the terminal.
Click “Process” to view the transaction breakdown, where you can apply any discounts or tax adjustments as needed. Click “Process” once again to send the transaction request to the PIN pad.
The transaction amount will be sent to the PIN pad terminal for customer payment.
The Signature screen will appear on the terminal screen and the customer can provide their signature to finalize the transaction.

pages.title: Ecard Added as a Gift Host | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ecard Added as a Gift Host | Release Notes iPOSpays
ECard Added as a Gift Host
POS build No - 10117
ECard gift host has been added to the iPOSpays portal. ISOs can now offer merchants the option to accept gift cards through ECard as an additional payment method on Dejavoo gateway-powered payment terminals.
How to Enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
S.T.E.A.M -> Edit Parameters -> Search and select the desired TPN from the search box at the top of the screen -> Edit Parameter.
Click on Integrations -> Enable “
Gift Host
” -> Select “
ECard
.”
Enter the User ID and Password.
Click “Save” and perform a parameter update on the merchant’s payment terminal for the changes to take effect.
How to Perform a Transaction:
From the idle payment screen, enter the transaction amount and click “OK.”
Select “Card” as the payment method.
Choose “Gift” from the presented options. Swipe or enter the gift card details to process the transaction.
The appropriate transaction status (Success or declined) will be reflected.

pages.title: View by Device | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
View by Device | Release Notes iPOSpays
View By Device
Now, the “View By Device” filter in the “Merchants Boarding” card for ISOs on the iPOSpays Dashboard can be displayed as a pie chart. This will provide a clear, device-wise breakdown of the onboarded merchants, offering more detailed insights into device usage trends.
How to Use
:
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using an ISO account.
Click on the “
Merchants Boarding
” card.
Click on the
side menu
and select “
View by Device Type
.”
A pie chart will display the device-wise breakdown of the merchants onboarded for the chosen card (Today, This Month, Last Month, and Overall).

pages.title: White Label Ipospays Bug Fixes | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
White Label Ipospays Bug Fixes | Release Notes iPOSpays
White Label iPOSpays Bug Fixes
iPOSpays Logo Issue:
Issue
: The iPOSpays logo appeared in the top left corner of the Hosted Payment Page (HPP) and Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP) when no logo was uploaded.
Update
: The logo will now only display if it has been uploaded, ensuring that no logo appears when none is provided.
Fav Icon Disappearance:
Issue
: The fav icon at the top of the browser tab disappeared after completing a transaction on the HPP or GHPP.
Update
: The fav icon now remains visible, regardless of the transaction status.
Incorrect Recipient Name:
Issue
: Previously, the email containing the MFA code for special operations displayed the incorrect recipient name.
Update
: This update resolves the issue, ensuring that the recipient name selected by the ISO is now displayed correctly.

pages.title: Store Dba Name Fixed in Invoice | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Store Dba Name Fixed in Invoice | Release Notes iPOSpays
Store DBA Name Fixed in Invoice
This update resolves an issue where the merchant’s
store DBA name
, containing special characters, was not properly displayed in the
E-invoice email
. With this update, the store DBA name will now appear correctly, even with special characters.

pages.title: Updated Password Reset Box Messages | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Updated Password Reset Box Messages | Release Notes iPOSpays
Updated Password Reset Box Messages
Previously, the password reset box on the iPOSpays portal login page displayed a generic “
Invalid password
” error message for incorrect entries. With this update, the message will now provide specific feedback based on the user’s input.
Examples:
Password less than 12 characters:
“Password must be at least 12 characters long.”
Password missing uppercase letters:
“Password must contain at least one uppercase letter.”
Password with only alpha characters:
“Password must contain at least one number.”
Password missing special characters:
“Password must contain at least one special character.”

pages.title: Ipospays Password Reset Glitch Fixed | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ipospays Password Reset Glitch Fixed | Release Notes iPOSpays
iPOSpays Password Reset Glitch Fixed
This update resolves a glitch that occurred during the password reset process on the iPOSpays portal.
Issue:
When users clicked the password reset link from their email while another tab with the iPOSpays portal was open, the reset link redirected to the
login screen
of the open tab with the user’s current username, instead of the
password reset page
.
Fix
:
The glitch has been resolved to ensure that the password reset link now correctly opens the
password reset page
, regardless of other open portal tabs.

pages.title: Duplicate Settlement Report Issue Fixed | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Duplicate Settlement Report Issue Fixed | Release Notes iPOSpays
Duplicate Settlement Report Issue Fixed
This update resolves an issue where, in some cases, merchants received
duplicate settlement reports
via email after settling a batch.
Fix:
The issue has been corrected to ensure that only
one settlement report
is sent per batch settlement, preventing confusion caused by duplicate emails.

pages.title: Google Pay Issue Resolved | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Google Pay Issue Resolved | Release Notes iPOSpays
Google Pay Issue Resolved
This update addresses functionality issues with the
Google Pay
feature. With this fix, merchants can now process
Google Pay transactions
more seamlessly and without interruptions.

pages.title: Cash Transactions Support for CloudPOS | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Cash Transactions Support for CloudPOS | Release Notes iPOSpays
Cash Transactions Support for CloudPOS
This update introduces an additional payment option for merchants using CloudPOS, allowing them to accept cash transactions through the
Pay Now
section. With this enhancement, merchants can now offer greater flexibility to their customers by accommodating cash payments alongside existing payment methods. This option is enabled by default for CloudPOS merchants.
How to Enable/Disable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Go to S.T.E.A.M ->
Edit Parameters
.
Search and select the TPN for which you want to enable this option.
Click
Edit Parameters
->
Transaction
-> Under
Card Type
, enable/disable
Cash
.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account.
Go to
CloudPOS
->
Payments
.
Select a TPN for which you wish to process the transaction from the drop-down menu.
Select
Pay Now
as the payment
Enter the transaction amount in its respective field. Choose
Cash
as the payment method.
Enter the customer’s contact information.
Enter the cash amount the customer provided. If the amount is higher than the transaction amount, the balance amount owed to the customer will be automatically updated in the
Change
field.
Click
Process
to complete the transaction.
Once the transaction is complete, go to the
Transactions
module. Use the
Alternative Payments
filter to identify the cash transaction if you process multiple transactions.

pages.title: Quarterly Recurring Payments | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Quarterly Recurring Payments | Release Notes iPOSpays
Quarterly Recurring Payments
The
Recurring Payments
feature has been updated to allow merchants to set up quarterly payments. With this feature, merchants can now schedule payments every three months from a chosen start date. This gives more options for managing subscriptions and billing.
How to Use:
Log in to the iPOSpays portal with a merchant account.
Go to
CloudPOS
->
My Customers
.
Find the customer by searching their email, phone number, or name.
Click
More
on the right side, then select
Add Recurring Payment
.
Set Up Recurring Payment:
Enter the payment amount, TPN (Terminal Profile Number), card type, card number, and (optional) external reference ID.
From the
Frequency
drop-down menu, choose
Quarterly
. A message will show when the next payment is due.
Select the start date using the calendar view.
Set an end condition:
Never
– The payment continues until canceled.
End by Date
– The payment stops on the date you choose.
End After Successful Payments
– Enter the number of payments; the cycle stops after this many successful transactions.
Edit/Cancel Recurring Payment:
Go to
CloudPOS
->
Recurring Payments
.
Click
Edit Recurring Payment
.
You can change the frequency or delete the Recurring Payment.

pages.title: Card Validation Risk Rule | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Card Validation Risk Rule | Release Notes iPOSpays
Card Validation Risk Rule
iPOS S.A.F.E has a new risk rule that checks for incorrect CVV and/or ZIP codes. If a transaction is approved but fails either check, it will be automatically voided, and the funds will be released back to the customer’s account. This update enhances security by ensuring that only transactions with valid CVV and AVS verifications are processed, increasing fraud prevention for merchants and customers.
To ensure this rule works as intended, it’s essential to make CVV and ZIP code mandatory to complete transactions.
How to Enable/Disable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Go to iPOS S.A.F.E. -> Risk Rules.
Select the desired TPN.
Scroll down and enable/disable
Card Validation
.
CVV Check
: When enabled, this rule will validate the CVV response obtained from the processor and if the response code received is equivalent to a mismatch, the transaction will be declined with code R12. This will trigger our system to automatically void the transaction and release the funds to the cardholder.
ZIP Code Check
: When enabled, this rule will validate the AVS Response obtained from the Processor and if the response code received is equivalent to a mismatch, the transaction will be declined with code R12. This will trigger our system to automatically void the transaction and release the funds to the cardholder.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
When this rule is triggered, the R12 risk code will be displayed on the merchant’s POS, Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!, Tap to Pay on Android With iPOSgo! and CloudPOS.
Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!
Tap to Pay on Android With iPOSgo!
Dejavoo Gateway-Powered Payment Terminals
Merchant CloudPOS
R12 risk code is printed on the transaction receipt

pages.title: Display Merchant and Store Logos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Display Merchant and Store Logos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Display Merchant and Store Logos
Previously, only the ISO logo could be shown on a Dejavoo gateway-powered payment terminal’s home screen. With this update, the ISO can now choose to display either the merchant or store logo.
How to Use:
Ensure the merchant has uploaded their logo to their account.
Log in to the iPOSpays portal with an ISO account.
Navigate to
S.T.E.A.M
->
Edit Parameters
.
Search for the payment terminal’s TPN, then click
Edit Parameter
.
Go to
Miscellaneous
->
Upload Logo
.
Select the desired merchant/store logo and click
Save
.
Download the parameters on the payment terminal to apply the changes.
Uploaded logo will appear on the merchant’s payment terminal

pages.title: Excel File Attached to Settlement Report Email | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Excel File Attached to Settlement Report Email | Release Notes iPOSpays
Excel File Attached to Settlement Report Email
When the merchant settles the batch, a settlement report is generated and sent as a PDF to the merchant admin’s email address. With this update, the email now includes an attached Excel version of the report in addition to the previously sent PDF. This update applies to all settlement report emails for POS, CloudPOS, and iPOSgo! apps.

pages.title: Configurable Merchant VAT Registration Number | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Configurable Merchant VAT Registration Number | Release Notes iPOSpays
Configurable Merchant VAT Registration Number
The VAT Registration Number is a Level 3 data field, required for Level 2 & Level 3 payment processing, which helps merchants qualify for lower interchange fees. Previously, merchants processing Level 3 transactions with TSYS were automatically assigned a default VAT registration number. With this update, ISOs can now configure the Merchant VAT Registration number under the Edit Store section of the iPOSpays portal. A maximum of 20 characters are allowed. This enables merchants to include their unique VAT  Registration Number with every transaction.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Click
Merchant
-> Search and select the merchant using the search box.
Under
Stores
, click on the merchant DBA and choose
Edit Store
.
Enter the
Merchant VAT Reg. NO
.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.

pages.title: Fixed Expected Average Column | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fixed Expected Average Column | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fixed Expected Average Column
Previously, in some cases, when users downloaded an Excel report of the
Decline Sales Trend
, the Expected Average column was incorrectly labeled as
Last 90 Days
. However, the Decline Sales Trend data table correctly displayed it as
Expected Average
. This issue has been fixed, and the downloaded Excel report now correctly shows
Expected Average
, matching the column on the data table.

pages.title: Invoice Date for Send Payment Link Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Invoice Date for Send Payment Link Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Invoice Date for Send Payment Link Transactions
Previously, when a Send Payment Link transaction was initiated, the automatically generated invoice displayed an incorrect generation date. With this update, the issue has been resolved, ensuring that the invoice generation date is accurately shown for Send Payment Link transactions. This fix improves the accuracy of invoice records in the Invoices Module, helping merchants maintain correct transaction logs.

pages.title: New Payment Method: Google Pay | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
New Payment Method: Google Pay | Release Notes iPOSpays
New Payment Method: Google Pay
Required POS Build: 10114
Required iOS Version: 1.0.42
Merchants processing with CloudPOS, Gateway-powered payment terminals, and Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! now supports Google Pay as a payment method for Send Payment Link and GHPP (Generic hosted payment page) transactions. This enhancement provides customers with additional flexibility and convenience to make payments. The feature is automatically enabled for all merchants processing with TSYS, requiring no additional setup or configuration.
Google Pay allows customers to pay using the cards saved to their Google accounts, such as those stored in Google Play, YouTube, Chrome, or Android phones. The Google Pay API fetches the tokenized card from the customer’s Google account, allowing them to pay conveniently using their saved card.
Google Pay is compatible with all iPOSpays customer-not-present scenarios, including sending payment links through a virtual terminal, recurring payments, and Hosted Payment Pages for eCommerce.
Note:
Transactions paid using Google Pay will be reported under
Minor Channel
in the
Transactions
module as “
Google Pay
”.
Send Payment Link and Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP) transactions initiated from the merchant CloudPOS, Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminals,
Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!
app, and Pre-Sale transactions also support this feature.
Currently, this payment method is only available for merchants processing with TSYS Host.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account.
Go to
CloudPOS
->
Payments
.
Select a TPN from the drop-down menu.
Click on “
Quick Pay
,” followed by “
Send Payment Link
,” and then select “
Sale
.”
Enter the total transaction amount.
Input the customer’s contact information, such as their phone number and email address.
Specify the link’s expiration time.
Optionally, include a reference number and a detailed transaction description (e.g., products or services purchased).
Add a disclaimer to be displayed on the secure payment page (optional).
Upload a pre-prepared invoice (optional).
Click “
Send Payment Link
” to share the link with the customer.
When the customer clicks the payment link, they will be redirected to our secure payment page.
If the tip option is enabled for the TPN, the customer can add a tip by clicking the ”
⌄
” icon.
The customer can then select
Google Pay
as their preferred payment method and complete the transaction.
To track payment status, reference  the “Invoices” module on the Merchant’s portal
Check the payment status (successful or pending).
Click “View Transaction” for detailed payment information.

pages.title: Additional Info in Batch Settlement Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Additional Info in Batch Settlement Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
Additional Info in Batch Settlement Report
Previously, the batch settlement report only provided information about the transaction type and number of transactions. With this update, we’ve expanded the report to include additional details such as Card Brand, Subtotal, and Fee.
How to View:
Direct Email
: The settlement report is sent directly to the merchant admin’s email address as a PDF attachment.
Batches Module:
Go to
Batches
->
Closed Batch
.
Select the desired batch and click
View Batch
.
Click on the download button ->
Save as PDF
.
The PDF will automatically open, displaying the Card Brand, Subtotal, and Fee under the
Summary
section.
Note
: this summary will also be visible when the report is exported as Excel reported as individual tabs ( Transaction Type and Card Brand)

pages.title: Customizable Tags for GHPP | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Customizable Tags for GHPP | Release Notes iPOSpays
Customizable Tags for GHPP
The tag that appears in the
GHPP
(Generic Hosted Payment Page) was set to  “
Customer Name
” by default. We have updated this tag to be customizable, which can be edited to suit the merchant’s needs. Simply enter the desired name in the Generate Payment Form settings and click
Save
. The changes will be immediately reflected on the GHPP.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant admin account.
Go to
Settings
->
Generate Payment Form
.
Choose a TPN from the drop-down menu and click
Generate URL
.
Backspace “
Customer Name
,” enter the desired tag name, and click
Save
.
Copy and paste the generated URL into your browser. The updated GHPP tag will be displayed at the bottom of the page.

pages.title: Signature Upload Shown for Declined Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Signature Upload Shown for Declined Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Signature Upload Shown for Declined Transactions
Previously, the Signature Upload column in the Transactions module of the merchant’s CloudPOS did not display details for declined transactions. This update ensures that the Signature Upload now includes all relevant information.
How to View:
Navigate to the
Transactions
module.
Choose the declined transaction you want to view.
The Signature Upload column will be visible on the right side of the screen.

pages.title: Signature Upload Fix for Gift Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Signature Upload Fix for Gift Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Signature Upload Fix for Gift Transactions
Required POS Build: 10114
This update ensures that the customer’s phone number and email address are accurately captured in the
Signature Upload
for Gift transactions within the
Transactions
module of the merchant’s CloudPOS. Previously, this information was not captured when merchants entered it to send a customer copy of the receipt.

pages.title: Feed | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Feed | Release Notes iPOSpays
FEED
iPOSpays now offers a new feature called “Feed,” which streams all transaction data into your existing systems from various payment channels, including POS, CloudPOS, Tap to Pay on iPhone, and Tap to Pay on Android.
Benefits for ISOs:
Enhanced Transaction Monitoring
: Feed enables ISOs to monitor transactions in real-time across various channels, including POS, CloudPOS, Tap to Pay on iPhone, and Tap to Pay on Android.
Custom Dashboard Development
: ISOs can leverage their existing CRM systems to create tailored dashboards and reports, allowing for in-depth analysis of transaction data.
Improved Transaction Management
: Feed offers greater flexibility for ISOs to manage transactions and settlements for their merchants.
How it Works
: Feed provides a live feed of transactions, allowing ISOs to monitor activities across different payment channels. This real-time data can be integrated with existing systems to provide a comprehensive view of business operations.
Please contact your iPOSpays representative for more information and to activate this feature for your account.

pages.title: Tokenization for Paya Ach | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Tokenization for Paya Ach | Release Notes iPOSpays
Tokenization and Run a Sale for Paya ACH
This update is for merchants using Paya ACH integration. Merchants can now tokenize their customer’s bank account details for future payments and use the “Run a Sale” feature to autofill those details to perform Paya ACH transactions.
When customers provide their phone number and email address, their account details are automatically saved to the “My Customers” database. This means that the next time the same customer visits, the merchant can simply use the “Run a Sale” function to autofill the customer’s account details, eliminating the need for manual entry.
How to Tokenize Paya ACH:
First, the merchant must have made an ACH transaction as there is no option to add it manually from the My Customers module. Once that’s done, the customer’s account details will be tokenized and saved in the “My Customers” database.
How to Use Run a Sale:
From the dashboard, go to “My Customers.”
Search for the customer’s phone number or email address from the search box at the top.
On the right side of the screen, toggle to “Bank Accounts”  by clicking the “Cards” button.
Choose the desired bank account number and click “Run a Sale.”
The system will automatically direct you to the CloudPOS payments screen where you can choose the preferred customer authorization.
The customer’s account details will have been automatically filled and you can enter the transaction amount to complete the transaction.

pages.title: Whitelisting Domain Names Capability for Isos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Whitelisting Domain Names Capability for Isos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Whitelisting Domain Names Capability for ISOs
Previously, the ability to whitelist domain names was only available to merchants. This update extends that functionality to ISOs. The ‘Whitelisted Domains’ feature lets ISOs specify the websites their merchants want to integrate with Freedom to Design.
How to Use:
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO admin account.
Go to
Settings
>
Merchant Whitelisted Domains
.
Select the desired merchant’s DBA from the Merchant dropdown menu.
Enter the domain name in the Domains field.
Click Save to apply the changes.

pages.title: Fresh Look and Feel | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fresh Look and Feel | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fresh Look and Feel
We’ve refreshed the look and feel of our iPOSpays Portal to provide a more streamlined user experience. Here’s what you’ll notice:
Updated UI Colors: We’ve implemented a new color scheme that enhances readability and clarity.
New Logo: Our new logo reflects our evolving brand identity and fosters a sense of familiarity.
Improved Loader Animation: The loader animation has been redesigned to provide smoother visual feedback during transactions.

pages.title: Mandatory 12 Digit Password for Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mandatory 12 Digit Password for Ipospays | Release Notes iPOSpays
Mandatory 12-digit Password for iPOSpays
We have updated the iPOSpays login password minimum requirement  from 10 to 12 characters.

pages.title: Otp Required for Tpn Change | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Otp Required for Tpn Change | Release Notes iPOSpays
OTP Required for TPN Change at POS.
Previously, terminals did not require additional authentication to change the TPN downloaded on the POS device. To strengthen security, a one-time passcode (OTP) is now required to change the Terminal Profile Number (TPN) on a POS terminal. The OTP can be obtained from the “Edit Parameters” Module on the iPOSpays Portal.
Example:
If the user wishes to replace the TPN on their POS terminal, they must:
Remove the old TPN and enter the new one
iPOSpays portal -> Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN from the search box at the top
Copy the OTP
Input the OTP on the POS Terminal when prompted.
If the OTP has expired, click the refresh button to obtain a new code.

pages.title: Mfa for Manual Refunds | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mfa for Manual Refunds | Release Notes iPOSpays
MFA for Manual Refunds
To enhance security, manual refunds will now require Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for each new session. Once authenticated, subsequent manual refunds within the same session will not require repeated MFA verification.
Example:
First manual refund: User logs in and is prompted for an MFA code. After entering the code, the refund is processed.
Subsequent manual refunds within the same session: No additional MFA required until the session expires.
New session (e.g., after closing the browser tab or logging out): User must re-enter the new MFA code for the next manual refund.

pages.title: Recurring Payments Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Recurring Payments Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
Recurring Payments Module
We’ve enhanced the stability of the Recurring Payments module. Previous glitches have been resolved, ensuring a smoother experience for merchants.

pages.title: Expanded Payment Notification Recipients | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Expanded Payment Notification Recipients | Release Notes iPOSpays
Expanded Payment Notification Recipients
Merchants can now add up to 10 email addresses to receive payment notifications, an increase from the previous limit of four.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account.
Click on “Settings.” Scroll down to enable “Payment Notifications.”
Choose a store and minor channel from the drop-down menu.
Add the email recipients to which the email notifications must be sent.
Click “Save” for the changes to take effect.

pages.title: Decimal Points for Factor 4 Gift Cards | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Decimal Points for Factor 4 Gift Cards | Release Notes iPOSpays
Decimal Points for Factor 4 Gift Cards
The Factor 4 Gift card system has been updated to support two decimal points for loyalty points. Previously, loyalty points were restricted to whole numbers. This update ensures greater precision in loyalty point calculations.

pages.title: Incremental Auth for Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Incremental Auth for Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Incremental Auth for CloudPOS
With this update, merchants using CloudPOS can utilize Incremental Auth. This feature allows merchants to increase the amount initially authorized in a Pre-Auth transaction.  They can add up to five incremental auth, where each increment will be added to the original Pre-Auth amount.
Note:
This feature is currently limited to merchants using TSYS and Fiserv North processors.
Only Visa, Mastercard, and Discover cards are accepted for this transaction. American Express is not currently supported.
How to Use:
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account.
Go to the transactions module and select the Pre-Auth transaction you wish to modify.
Select “More” and click “Incremental Auth.”
Enter the amount you wish to add and click “Process.”

pages.title: Pay Now Option for Ipospays Mobile Users | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Pay Now Option for Ipospays Mobile Users | Release Notes iPOSpays
Pay Now Option for iPOSpays Mobile Users
Previously, merchants using the mobile version of CloudPOS only had the option to select “Send Payment Link” transaction. With this update, merchants will also have the “Pay Now” option, allowing them to process a customer present transaction.

pages.title: New Risk Rules | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
New Risk Rules | Release Notes iPOSpays
New Risk Rules
With this update, we have included two new risk rules to the iPOS S.A.F.E module:
International card – Refund on CloudPOS
and
International card – Refund on POS
. These rules allow the ISO to determine if a TPN can accept international cards for manual refunds, whether processed on CloudPOS or POS terminals.
How to Enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a primary or add-on ISO admin account.
Click on “Risk Rules” under iPOS S.A.F.E.
Scroll down to find the two new risk rules.
Choose “Yes” to allow, or “No” to restrict.
Note:
When either of these two rules is triggered, the R11 risk code will be displayed as the transaction response on the merchant’s CloudPOS, payment terminal, and receipt.
“International card – Refund on CloudPOS” is set to “No” by default. This is due to the increased risk of cyberattacks associated with online transactions. However, ISOs have the option to set it to “Yes” to allow manual refunds on International cards.

pages.title: Mfa for New User Creation | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mfa for New User Creation | Release Notes iPOSpays
MFA For New User Creation and Edit User
This update is for users who have configured MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication) for their account on the iPOSpays portal. With this update, users will be required to enter a 6-digit security code to complete the user creation process and when editing an existing user. This requirement applies to all primary and add-on users including ISOs, Agent Offices, Merchant Admins, Merchant Store, and MultiStore Managers.
Note:
MFA will prompt once per logged-in session, for example:
If a merchant user adds a new user, on the first user creation, the system will request MFA, but if a merchant user needs to create additional users, they will not be prompted for MFA for those additional users as long as the user has not logged out and back into the system.
How to Add a User:
Log in to iPOSpays with a primary or add-on user account.
Click on “User Management.”
Select the action box and click “Add User.”
Fill in the user’s details: first name, last name, email address, phone number, and user role.
Click “Submit.”
A 6-digit security code will be sent to your registered email address.
You will be prompted to enter the 6-digit code sent to your registered email address.
Enter the 6-digit code to complete the user creation process.
How to Edit a User:
Log in to iPOSpays with a primary or add-on user account.
Click on “User Management.”
Search and select the user using the search box at the top.
Select “Edit User.”
After editing the user details, click “Submit.”
A 6-digit security code will be sent to your registered email address.
Enter the 6-digit code to complete the process.

pages.title: Recurring Payment Time Changed | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Recurring Payment Time Changed | Release Notes iPOSpays
Recurring Payment Time Changed
Previously, recurring payments were processed at 12:00 AM EST. With this update, we’ve adjusted the processing time to 3:15 AM EST to accommodate merchants in various time zones.

pages.title: More Item Support for Inventory | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
More Item Support for Inventory | Release Notes iPOSpays
Increased Inventory Limit
The inventory limit has been increased from 70 to 200 items, allowing merchants to expand their product inventory. This update applies to the inventory module in the iPOSpays portal and Register Lite on the POS.

pages.title: Updated Account Number Field in Paya Ach | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Updated Account Number Field in Paya Ach | Release Notes iPOSpays
Updated Account Number Field in Paya ACH
This update is for merchants using Paya ACH. The Paya ACH account number field on the CloudPOS payments module, Send Payment Link transaction, and GHPP (Generic Hosted Payment Page) has been updated to require a minimum of 6 and a maximum of 17 digits.
Pay Now screen on the CloudPOS payments page
Payment page during the “Send Payment Link” transaction
Generic Hosted Payment Page

pages.title: Ipos S a F E | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ipos S a F E | Release Notes iPOSpays
iPOS S.A.F.E
iPOS S.A.F.E is a risk management tool that is crucial for ensuring transactional security. It’s designed to protect merchants by spotting and stopping potential fraud or suspicious activity before it occurs, rather than after the damage is done – a significant improvement compared to traditional systems.
With this feature, ISOs can configure Risk Rules per TPN or set Templates to apply to existing or newly created TPNs. These Rules, when triggered, will automatically deactivate the affected TPN. The ISOs may reactivate it, by selecting the “Activate” option located on the iPOS S.A.F.E module -> Risk Rules -> locate desired TPN. Before reactivating a TPN we strongly suggest the ISO confirm the transactions that triggered this “Risk Flag” by communicating with the merchant to validate the legitimacy of these transaction attempts.

pages.title: Email When New Users Are Created | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Email When New Users Are Created | Release Notes iPOSpays
New User Email Notification
This update will provide iPOSpays Admin users an automatic email notification when a new user is created under their account either by a primary or an add-on user. This notification ensures that the registered Admin User is always aware of new user accounts created under their account.
Example:
ISO User created a new user (ISO Admin, ISO Operator, Agent Office, Merchant Admin) -> ISO Admin will receive email notification
Merchant User creates a new user ( Merchant Admin, Merchant Operator, Multi Store Manager, Store Manager) -> Merchant Admin will receive email notification
Email sent to the primary user’s registered email address

pages.title: Additional Lines for Receipt Header | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Additional Lines for Receipt Header | Release Notes iPOSpays
Additional Lines for Receipt Header
Required POS Build: 10111
Previously, merchants using gateway-powered terminals had the option to write up to 4 lines in the header. With this new update we are allowing 2 additional lines, for a total of 6 header lines. Each line supports up to 24 alphanumeric characters.
Enter the desired text to be displayed on the receipt

pages.title: Multi Factor Authentication | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Multi Factor Authentication | Release Notes iPOSpays
Multi-Factor Authentication
To enhance security, the iPOSpays portal now uses multi-factor authentication. When logging in, users will receive a one-time code via their registered email address and/or registered phone number as SMS that they will need to enter for verification. This feature is enabled by default for ISOs.
Users who can enable this feature for other users:
ISOs can enable this feature for their non-default user, Agent Office, and Merchant Admin.
A non-default ISO can enable this feature for Agent Office, and Merchant Admin.
An Agent Office can enable this feature for their non-default user, and Merchant Admin.
A default Merchant Admin can enable this feature for their non-default user, Merchant Operator, Multi Store Manager, and Store Manager.
A non-default Merchant Admin can enable this feature for Merchant Operator, Multi Store Manager, and Store Manager.
Security code is sent to the registered user
Enter the security code that was sent to the registered email address
Although it is strongly recommended that this feature is not disabled
, in the event it is required, the feature can be disabled.
How to Disable
: Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO/Agent/Merchant Admin login. To disable this feature for existing users,  go to User Management -> Search and select the user -> Toggle off MFA.
For new users, click on the action box -> Add User -> Toggle off MFA.

pages.title: New Parameters in Dual Pricing | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
New Parameters in Dual Pricing | Release Notes iPOSpays
New Parameters in Dual Pricing
With this update, ISOs can now give their merchants the option to:
Apply a Dual Pricing fee to tips.
Apply tax to the Dual Pricing fee.
Both options can be enabled/disabled from the “Edit Parameter” section of the iPOSpays portal. This is available for merchants who use CloudPOS and Gateway-powered payment terminals.
How to enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO/Agent account.
Click on S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters.
Search and select the TPN from the search box for which you wish to enable this feature.
Click on Edit Parameter.
Click on the “Fee” tab -> Enable/disable “Apply fee on tip” and “Apply tax on fee.”

pages.title: Additional Commodity Codes | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Additional Commodity Codes | Release Notes iPOSpays
Additional Commodity Codes
With this update, we have included more commodity codes when setting up Level 2 & Level 3 Autofilling from the “Edit Parameters” section of the iPOSpays portal. This has been implemented to ensure that the ISOs have a comprehensive list of all the commodity codes. This way, they will have more options to choose from when setting up a merchant.
Click on the highlighted area to view the full list
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO login.
Click on S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters.
Search and select the TPN.
Edit Parameter -> Value Add -> Enable L2/Autofilling.
Click on “Other”, then “List” -> Choose the suitable commodity code.

pages.title: Change User Roles for Merchant Admin | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Change User Roles for Merchant Admin | Release Notes iPOSpays
Change User Roles for Merchant Admin
Before:
A default merchant admin could change the roles of non-default admins and operators after they were created, but only to non-default admin or operator roles.
After:
Default merchant admins can now adjust the roles of all non-default users (merchant admin, operator, store manager, multi-store manager). This means the default merchant admin can assign them to any of these roles.
Choose the required user role from the drop-down menu
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account -> User management -> Action box -> Edit user

pages.title: Invoice Tab Gets a New Filter | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Invoice Tab Gets a New Filter | Release Notes iPOSpays
Invoice Tab Gets a New Filter
The invoice section on the iPOSpays portal now includes a ‘Store’ filter. This allows merchants with multiple stores to easily find invoices for a specific store.
Enter the store name in the highlighted area to retrieve the invoices
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account -> Invoices -> Filter icon -> Enter the store name in the “Store” field.

pages.title: UI Enhancements for Mobile Users | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
UI Enhancements for Mobile Users | Release Notes iPOSpays
UI Enhancements for Mobile Users
For mobile users of the iPOSpays portal, we have made some enhancements to ensure the UI is fluid and responsive on all smartphones despite their size.

pages.title: Skip Breakup Screen for Spin | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Skip Breakup Screen for Spin | Release Notes iPOSpays
Skip Breakup Screen for SPIn
Required POS Build: 10109
Previously, during SPIn transactions, the integrated PIN pad terminal would display a breakup screen by default if the tip/tax/fee were included in the transaction. Now, with this update, we have introduced a new option that allows ISOs to enable/disable the breakup screen based on their merchant’s preference from the iPOSpays portal.
How to Use: Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO login. Click on S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN -> Edit Parameter -> Integration -> Enable SPIn -> Enable/Disable “Breakup Screen.”
When the breakup screen is disabled, the device will automatically direct the user to the Sale section

pages.title: Simplified User Choice Response | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Simplified User Choice Response | Release Notes iPOSpays
Simplified User Choice Response
Required POS Build: 10109
This update is for merchants who used their own POS application with our Gateway-powered payment terminals through SPIn. Previously, when the merchant initiated the card mode (Credit/Debit/User choice), they would have to click on “OK” from their POS application for it to reflect on the integrated PIN pad terminal. This update removes the extra step of having to click on the “OK’ button. A simple click on the payment type would ensure that the action is reflected on the payment terminal.

pages.title: Settle Batch for Alternate Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Settle Batch for Alternate Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Settle Batch for Alternate Transactions
Previously, CloudPOS merchants could not settle batches that exclusively consisted of transactions performed using alternative payment methods, such as; cash, ACH, or gift cards. It was required, for a batch to be settled, to at least include one card payment. However, with this update, CloudPOS merchants can now settle batches even in the absence of any card transactions.
(Image 1.0)
How to Use: From the merchant login of the iPOSpays portal, go to batches -> Open Batch -> Settle Batch. (Image 1.0)

pages.title: Void Transactions Visible in Batches | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Void Transactions Visible in Batches | Release Notes iPOSpays
Void Transactions Visible in Batches
This update addresses a minor issue where, in certain instances, a void transaction declined by the processor did not appear in the Open & Closed Batches. The update ensures that these transactions are now correctly displayed.

pages.title: Ghpp Customizable Tag | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ghpp Customizable Tag | Release Notes iPOSpays
GHPP Customizable Tag
This release introduces the capability to include a customizable tag when creating a Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP). Merchants can now add a custom tag within the GHPP and label it according to their preferences directly from the iPOSpays portal. Additionally, they have the flexibility to configure it as either optional or mandatory for completing the transaction.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account -> Settings -> Generate Payment Form -> Choose a TPN from the dropdown -> Under shorten URL, name the custom field. Click Save for the changes to take effect.
Image (1.1)
Image (1.2)

pages.title: Accurate Store Name Address | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Accurate Store Name Address | Release Notes iPOSpays
Accurate Store Name & Address
Before this update, when the merchant sent a payment link to the customer, the email containing the payment link would feature the merchant’s email and address. Similarly, this information was reflected in the invoice issued upon completing the transaction. This update replaces the merchant’s email and address with the store name and address for enhanced clarity.
Image (1.1) Store Name & Address in Email
Image (1.2) Store Name & Address in the Invoice

pages.title: Updated Customer Support Contact | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Updated Customer Support Contact | Release Notes iPOSpays
Updated Customer Support Contact
This update has changed the customer support details found in the terms and conditions of Paya ACH in Send Payment Link transaction. The updated customer support contact details include the store’s phone number and email address.

pages.title: Surcharge Limit | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Surcharge Limit | Release Notes iPOSpays
Surcharge Limit
Required Build: 10107
Previously, when configuring the fee percentage for Surcharge, the system automatically rounded off decimal digits. For instance, if you had input the fee percentage as 2.56%, the system would round it to 2.6%. This update alters this setting to maintain the percentage without rounding (Image 1.1). Moreover, ISOs will now only be able to set the Surcharge fee to a maximum of 3%, which cannot be exceeded (Image 1.2). This policy applies to all of our payment terminals and CloudPOS transactions.
Where to Enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account. Then, go to S.T.E.A.M -> Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN from the search box -> Edit Parameter -> Fee -> Surcharge -> Set the fee percentage and click Save for the changes to take effect.
Image (1.1)
Image (1.2)

pages.title: Custom Fields in Paya Ach Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Custom Fields in Paya Ach Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Custom Fields in Paya ACH Transactions
This update introduces the inclusion of custom fields in the Paya ACH transactions. These fields are available on the Pay Now screen and the payment page when using the “Send Payment Link” feature. This enhancement enables merchants to incorporate additional custom fields alongside the existing ones on the payment page. Moreover, the fields support Alphanumeric characters as well.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account -> CloudPOS, then Payments -> Choose a TPN from the drop-down menu -> Enter the amount -> ACH -> Enter the desired text in the custom fields. Then, complete the transaction as you normally would.
Image (1.1) Custom Fields in Paya ACH Transactions – Pay Now
Image (1.2) Custom Fields in Paya ACH Transactions – Sent Payment Link

pages.title: Enhanced Invoice Retrieval for Quickbooks Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Enhanced Invoice Retrieval for Quickbooks Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Enhanced Invoice Retrieval for QuickBooks Integration
Previously, merchants using QuickBooks integration could only search for invoices by entering a specific invoice number. This update introduces a new way to find invoices: search by customer name.
Now, merchants can easily view and retrieve pending invoices associated with a particular customer. This allows them to:
Identify customers with outstanding balances.
Process transactions efficiently.
The overall process remains the same. Once you find the invoice using the customer’s name, you can complete the transaction as usual.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account
Click on CloudPOS, then Payments.
Search and select the TPN from the drop-down menu.
Enter the customer’s name in the “Customer Name” field.
Click on the customer’s name to view the balance due amount.
Click “Use” to retrieve the invoice and complete the transaction as you normally would.

pages.title: Transaction Summary in Batch Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Transaction Summary in Batch Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
Transaction Summary in Batch Report
This update introduces a new feature to the Batch Report: the ‘Transaction Summary.’ Now, in addition to the store and batch summaries, you’ll have a detailed breakdown of every transaction from each store. This summary neatly organizes essential information like transaction type, ID, external reference ID, amount, and more, offering a clear and thorough overview of the transactions.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account. Go to Reports -> Batch Reports -> Action Box -> Download -> Inside the Excel file click on “Transaction Summary.”

pages.title: Invoice Pending Icon | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Invoice Pending Icon | Release Notes iPOSpays
Invoice Pending Icon
Previously, there were instances where merchants completing an ACH transaction found that the invoice status remained pending. This update resolves that issue, ensuring the invoice status accurately reflects the transaction status.

pages.title: Delete Cards for Store Manager | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Delete Cards for Store Manager | Release Notes iPOSpays
Delete Cards for Store Manager
Before this update, when merchants logged into the iPOSpays portal with a store manager login and attempted to use the ‘Delete card’ button, there were instances where it didn’t respond as expected. This update addresses the issue, ensuring the button now functions reliably.

pages.title: Whitelisted Domain Names | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Whitelisted Domain Names | Release Notes iPOSpays
Whitelisting Domain Names
This update introduces a new feature to the merchant login on the iPOSpays portal. The “Whitelisted Domains” feature will allow merchants to specify the website URL they wish to integrate with Freedom to Design. This functionality ensures the secure handling of vital card details. The integration of Freedom to Design not only enhances security but also empowers merchants to collect payment information securely, customize the checkout experience, and seamlessly integrate with their existing transaction API for the completion of transactions. All these capabilities are delivered while maintaining rigorous standards of data security.
How to Use:
From the merchant login, go to Settings -> Whitelisted Domains -> Enter the URL of the website -> Click Save for the changes to take effect.

pages.title: Tax Removal on Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Tax Removal on Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Tax Removal on CloudPOS
Previously, only merchants using Gateway-powered payment terminals and CloudPOS merchants using True Cash Discount could remove the tax from a transaction. With this update, ISOs will now have the ability to enable their CloudPOS merchants to remove the tax from a transaction if they wish regardless of the fee type they use. (Image 1.0)
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M -> Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN from the search box at the top -> Edit Parameter -> Tip & Taxes -> Click “Yes” from Merchant Can Remove Tax. Click Save for the changes to take effect.
(Image 1.0)
How to Use:
First, enter the amount in the designated field
Click on the amount within the Cards icon
Click on the (X) icon and click on the trash icon to remove the tax from the transaction

pages.title: Treat Debit Card as Cash for Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Treat Debit Card as Cash for Cloudpos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Treat Debit Card as Cash for CloudPOS
We’re excited to announce an enhancement to the dual pricing feature on CloudPOS. Previously, merchants could implement Dual Pricing for credit card transactions, allowing customers to choose between cash and card pricing. However, we’re now extending this functionality to include debit card transactions as well. This means that customers using debit cards can also benefit from Dual Pricing, providing greater flexibility and convenience for merchants and customers.
By encouraging customers to use debit cards for transactions, merchants can reduce transaction costs associated with credit card processing fees. This can result in significant savings over time, benefiting the bottom line.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M, then edit parameters -> Search and select the TPN -> Edit Parameter -> Fee -> Dual Pricing -> Enable “Treat Debit Card as Cash.” Click Save for the changes to take effect.

pages.title: Fortellis Invoice Number Filter | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fortellis Invoice Number Filter | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fortellis Invoice Number Filter
In this update, the Transactions tab is now equipped with a new filter, allowing merchants to narrow down search results based on the Fortellis invoice number.
How to Use:
From the merchant login, go to Transactions -> Click on the filter icon at the top right corner of the screen -> Integrations -> Fortellis -> Enter the invoice number and click “Search.”

pages.title: Dba Reference Id Filter in My Customers Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
