Dba Reference Id Filter in My Customers Module | Release Notes iPOSpays
DBA & Reference ID Filter in My Customers Module
In this update, the My Customers module is now equipped with two new filters, allowing merchants to narrow down search results based on the DBA and external reference ID.
How to Use:
From the merchant login, go to My Customers -> Click on the Filter icon at the top right corner of the screen -> Enter the DBA or External Reference ID and click “Search.”

pages.title: Recurring Payment Notifications for Transaction Failures | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Recurring Payment Notifications for Transaction Failures | Release Notes iPOSpays
Recurring Payment Notifications for Transaction Failures
Previously, if the merchant had notifications enabled for recurring payments, they would receive an email notification only when a recurring payment was successful. This update will now send an email notification to the merchant if the recurring payment is not successful as well.
Where to Enable:
From the merchant login, go to Settings -> Recurring Payment Alert -> Enable “Email Notification” and Click Save.
Enable Email Notification for Recurring Payment Alert:
Email Notification for Recurring Payments Transaction Failure:

pages.title: Negative Sign Settlement Status Shown in Transaction Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Negative Sign Settlement Status Shown in Transaction Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
Negative Sign & Settlement Status Shown in Transaction Report
For better clarity and simple interpretation, the amount shown in the Refund and Void column of the Transaction Report will now have a negative sign. (Image 1.0)
This update also includes the settlement status of each transaction within the transaction report under a separate column called Settlement Status. “Settled” indicates that the batch containing this transaction has been settled and “Open” means that the batch is still open. (Image 1.1)
How to Download Transaction Report:
From the merchant login, go to Transactions -> Click on the action box -> Select “Download.”
(Image 1.0)
(Image 1.1)

pages.title: Adjust Price in Dual Pricing | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Adjust Price in Dual Pricing | Release Notes iPOSpays
Adjust Price in Dual Pricing
Before this update, merchants who made transactions on CloudPOS with Dual Pricing could not remove the fee from a transaction. With this new update, ISOs will now have the option to enable Adjust Price for their merchants, enabling them to remove the fee from a Dual Pricing transaction on CloudPOS.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M, then edit parameters -> Search and select the TPN -> Edit Parameter -> Fee -> Dual Pricing -> Enable “Adjust Price”. -> (Image 1.0)
Image 1.0
How to Use:
Enter the amount.
Click on the Plus (+) icon. (Image 1.1)
Click on this (✕) icon next to Sale to remove the fee. (Image 1.2)
Image 1.1
**Image 1.2
**

pages.title: Purchase Order Number | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Purchase Order Number | Release Notes iPOSpays
Purchase Order Number
Required POS Build: 10090
With this update, merchants now can input the purchase order (PO) number while conducting manual card entry transactions. This will streamline order management for merchants, providing them with a convenient way to record and monitor their orders. The PO number is Level 3 payment processing information, enabling merchants to save on interchange costs.
The tag label for the PO number is fully customizable, and users can choose whether to make it a mandatory or optional field to finalize the transaction. The capability to add a purchase order number is available for both CloudPOS and payment terminal transactions.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M, then edit parameters -> Search and select the TPN -> Edit Parameter -> Transactions -> Enable “Purchase Order Number” -> Customize the label if you wish -> Configure it as mandatory/optional. -> (Image 1.0)
Image 1.0
Enter the Purchase Order number in the highlighted area
From the Sale section, choose “ENTER CARD”
After entering the card details choose “No”
Enter the Purchase Order number and complete the transaction
PO number in the receipt

pages.title: Four Decimal Places in L2 L3 Tax Rate | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Four Decimal Places in L2 L3 Tax Rate | Release Notes iPOSpays
Four Decimal Places in L2/L3 Tax Rate
Before this update, ISOs/agents were restricted to adding up to two decimal places for the tax rate in Level 2 & Level 3 Auto Filling feature on the iPOSpays portal. This functionality has now been improved, allowing for up to four decimal places to be added. This enhancement ensures greater precision and fairness in tax rate calculations, providing more accurate and detailed information for users.
How to Use:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M, then Edit parameters -> Search and select the TPN -> Edit Parameter -> Value Add -> L2/L3 Autofilling -> Enter the Tax Rate. -> (Image 1.0)
Image 1.0

pages.title: Renamed Sms Button to Text | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Renamed Sms Button to Text | Release Notes iPOSpays
Renamed SMS Button to TEXT
Required POS Build: 10089
The word SMS has been renamed to TEXT for better clarity. This change in wording is applicable in the iPOSpays portal and across all payment terminals.
Where this word has been changed:
In the iPOSpays portal, go to S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN in the search box -> Edit Parameter -> Receipt -> Under Customer Copy ->
Text
In the payment terminal, the SMS button that appears after a transaction

pages.title: State Surcharge Tax Compliance | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
State Surcharge Tax Compliance | Release Notes iPOSpays
State Surcharge Tax Compliance
Required POS Build: 10089
Previously, the tax amount was added to the base and tip amount (If the tip was enabled). With this update, there is an additional option called
State Surcharge Tax Compliance
in the Surcharge Fee section. If enabled, the tax amount will be calculated on the base amount + tip (if enabled) + fee collected, to ensure compliance with specific state laws.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, click on S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN in the search box -> Edit Parameter -> Fee -> Surcharge ->
Enable State Surcharge Tax Compliance

pages.title: Dual Pricing Fixed Fee | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Dual Pricing Fixed Fee | Release Notes iPOSpays
Dual Pricing Fixed Fee
Required POS Build: 10089
Previously, the Dual Pricing fee could only be configured as a percentage. This update introduces the ability to set a fixed Dual Pricing fee. For example, if you set the fee amount to $1.00, it will be applied to all transactions regardless of the amount.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, click on S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN in the search box -> Edit Parameter -> Fee -> Dual Pricing -> Click on % -> Select $. Then, enter the fixed Dual Pricing Fee in its respective field.
Choose $ from the drop-down menu
Enter the fixed Dual Pricing fee

pages.title: Added Filters to the Invoice Tab | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Added Filters to the Invoice Tab | Release Notes iPOSpays
Added Filters to the Invoice Tab
In this update, the Invoice tab is now equipped with new filters, allowing merchants to narrow down search results when seeking a specific invoice.
Newly added filters:
Phone Number: Find invoices using the customer’s phone number
Email: Locate invoices using the customer’s email address
Tag Value: Search for invoices based on recorded tag values
Card Holder Name: Find invoices using the cardholder’s name
Performed By: Search for invoices by the email address of the user who performed the transaction
PAN (Last 4 Digits): Locate invoices by entering the last 4 digits of the card used in the transaction
How to Use:
From the merchant login, Click on CloudPOS, then Invoices -> Click on the Filter icon at the top right corner of the screen -> Select the filter -> Click Search

pages.title: Capital Letter Error in Email Field | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Capital Letter Error in Email Field | Release Notes iPOSpays
Capital Letter Error in Email Field
Previously, in the merchant login, when the merchant entered an email address or pasted it from elsewhere in the send receipt section, an error message would appear if the email started with or included a capital letter. This update fixes that issue to ensure that the receipt can still be sent even if the email started with or included a capital letter.
Where this error was fixed:
From the merchant login, click on Transactions ->Choose a transaction -> View Receipt -> Customer copy -> Actions -> Email

pages.title: Cloudpos Pinpad Transactions Updated to Quickbooks | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Cloudpos Pinpad Transactions Updated to Quickbooks | Release Notes iPOSpays
CloudPOS + PIN pad Transactions Updated to QuickBooks
Required POS Build: 10089
Previously, when using CloudPOS with a PIN pad integration and running a transaction through the PIN pad, the transactions were not being updated in the merchant’s QuickBooks. This update fixes this issue and ensures the transaction is recorded in the merchant’s QuickBooks.

pages.title: Worldpay | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Worldpay | Release Notes iPOSpays
Worldpay (Next Sprint)
iPOSpays gateway is now certified on Worldpay’s processing platform. ISOs can now onboard merchants on the Worldpay processor through the iPOSpays portal. This certification brings expanded payment options, improved security features, and access to valuable data and analytics for both ISOs and merchants.

pages.title: L2 L3 | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
L2 L3 | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introducing L2 & L3
Required POS Build: 10084
We have implemented L2 and L3 enhancements across all transactions on the iPOSpays portal (CloudPOS) and Gateway-powered payment terminals. This initiative aims to benefit merchants who run B2B businesses by lowering their interchange costs associated with each transaction. After the transaction, the tax amount will be combined and displayed in the receipt as Tax Reported. (Image 1.2)
**How to Enable: **
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters
Search and select the TPN from the search box at the top
Click on Edit Parameter -> Value Add -> Enable L2/L3 Autofilling
Choose the commodity CODE, then enter the tax rate.
Please note that the tax rate must be equal to or lower than 22%.
(Image 1.1)
(Image 1.2)

pages.title: Shortened URL | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Shortened URL | Release Notes iPOSpays
Shortened URL
The URL for hosted payment page integrators has been shortened so that it doesn’t take up too much space on the integrator’s database. (Image 1.1) Moreover, merchants will now have access to a shorter URL when creating a generic hosted payment page URL from the portal. (Image 1.2)
How to Generate GHPP:
From the merchant login, go to Settings -> Generate Payment Form -> Choose a TPN -> Click Generate URL -> Copy Shortened URL.
(Image 1.1)
(Image 1.2)

pages.title: Tag Values | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Tag Values | Release Notes iPOSpays
Tag Values
Required POS Build: 10084
Tag values entered against transactions are now displayed in the receipt and the email notification for the merchant after a transaction is complete. This can be especially useful for automobile merchants who wish to print the model/VIN of a vehicle on the receipt after a transaction.
(Please note that Tag Value names can be modified to fit the merchant’s needs. For example, it can be Invoice No, or a Table No.)
Where to Enable:
S.T.E.A.M-> Edit Parameters-> Search and select the merchant-> Edit Parameter-> Value Add -> Enable Tag the tx.

pages.title: Invoice Displayed | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Invoice Displayed | Release Notes iPOSpays
Invoice Displayed
The Fortellis invoice number will now be displayed in the Open Batch, under Fortellis signature in the Transactions tab, and downloaded transaction and batch reports under the title Additional fields.
How to View Open Batch:
From the merchant login, go to batches -> Toggle the Open Batch button on -> Click on View Batch.
Invoice in Open Batch
How to View Fortellis Signature:
Go to the Transactions tab -> Search and select the TPN in the search bar -> The invoice will be displayed under Fortellis.
Invoice in Transaction Tab
How to Download Transaction Report:
Go to the Transaction tab -> Click on the action box at the top left -> Click on Download -> Open the Excel file and you’ll find the Fortellis invoice number under Additional Details.
How to Download Batch Report:
Go to Batches -> Click on the action box -> Select download Open Batch.

pages.title: Invoice Type Renamed | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Invoice Type Renamed | Release Notes iPOSpays
Invoice Type Renamed
During a Fortelllis transaction, in the CloudPOS screen, the merchant would select the Invoice type from a drop-down menu. The names of the invoice types have been changed from Services to Service Department and Spare Parts to Parts Department for more clarity.

pages.title: Retain Customer Contacts in Run a Sale | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Retain Customer Contacts in Run a Sale | Release Notes iPOSpays
Retain Customer Contacts in Run a Sale
Previously, during the Run a Sale transaction, when the merchant entered the QuickBooks invoice number to fetch the transaction details from QuickBooks, the saved phone number and email address would automatically be removed from the fields. Since the fields cannot be edited in the Run a Sale transaction, merchants could not proceed with the transaction.
This issue has been acknowledged and resolved so that entering the QuickBooks invoice number will no longer remove the phone number and email address allowing merchants to complete transactions seamlessly.
How to Access:
From the merchant login, go to the My Customers page -> Select the customer -> Click on Action Box under Card Type -> Select Run a Sale

pages.title: Added Terms and Conditions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Added Terms and Conditions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Added Terms and conditions
We have introduced terms and conditions for Paya ACH in our Hosted payment page and generic hosted payment page. The customer must read all the terms and conditions and then click Process to proceed with the transaction.

pages.title: Change in Login Capabilities | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Change in Login Capabilities | Release Notes iPOSpays
Change in Login Capabilities
Before this update, ISOs could choose a merchant and log into their account to make a Paya ACH transaction using the ‘Login as’ feature on the iPOSpays portal. To enhance security, this update changes this method and now ISOs can only log in to the merchant account using the merchant’s credentials to use Paya ACH.
How to Login:
From the home screen, tap on the user name icon at the top right corner (Next to the settings icon) -> Click Logout -> Enter the merchant’s credentials and select Login.

pages.title: Security Enhancement for Payment Pages | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Security Enhancement for Payment Pages | Release Notes iPOSpays
Security Enhancement for Payment Pages
We’ve integrated reCAPTCHA v2 to safeguard both our Generic Hosted Payment Page (GHPP) and Hosted Payment Page (HPP) against fraudulent activities, spam, and abuse. For the GHPP, it is mandatory for the customers to complete reCAPTCHA v2 to process a transaction. If Google detects bot activity during the initial reCAPTCHA process, the customer will be prompted to complete an additional image challenge. On the other hand, for our hosted payment page, the reCAPTCHA v2 will only be triggered if bot activity is detected in the HPP.

pages.title: Optimized Interchange | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Optimized Interchange | Release Notes iPOSpays
Optimized Interchange
For card not present scenarios, we have made certain changes in the backend for interchange optimization.

pages.title: Paya Ach Enhancements | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Paya Ach Enhancements | Release Notes iPOSpays
Void and Refund for Paya ACH
Paya ACH gets two new functionalities:
Void and Refund.
Merchants utilizing Paya ACH will now be able to void or refund a transaction from the iPOSpays portal. This feature is available on the Transactions tab of the merchant login.
Click the link below to learn how to use this feature

pages.title: Improved Search Bar Functionality | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Improved Search Bar Functionality | Release Notes iPOSpays
Improved Search Bar Functionality
This update addresses a specific issue concerning the search bar functionality in the iPOSpays portal. Previously, entering the store DBA with spaces between each word in the search bar would cause the filter to fail in retrieving search results. This problem has been rectified, and the search results will now function as intended.
How to Use:
From the portal, go to S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters -> Search for store DBA in the “Search by DBA” box at the top of the screen -> Click Search.

pages.title: Expanded Domain Compatibility | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Expanded Domain Compatibility | Release Notes iPOSpays
Expanded Domain Compatibility
In CloudPOS, under Send Payment Link, there was an issue with the email ID field, where some email addresses concluding with different domain names were flagged as invalid. This issue has been successfully resolved. The email ID field now supports a broader range of domain names, ensuring compatibility with various email addresses.
How to Use:
From the merchant login, go to CloudPOS, then Payments -> Click on Send Payment Link from Payment Type -> Enter the email address in the respective field.

pages.title: Additional Fields in Transaction Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Additional Fields in Transaction Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
More Fields Added to Transaction Report
The downloaded transaction report has been updated to include two additional fields:
Term ID and MID (Merchant ID)
.
How to Download:
From the home screen, go to the Transactions tab -> Click on the action box (Horizontal three lines) -> Select Download. Once the report is downloaded, the two fields will be visible next to each other along with the list of transactions.

pages.title: Void Support for Pre Auth Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Void Support for Pre Auth Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Void Support for Pre Auth Transactions
Merchants utilizing CloudPOS for Pre Auth transactions can now void them directly from the portal.
How to Void:
From the merchant login, Go to Transactions -> choose a Pre Auth transaction -> click on Ticket/Void -> Choose Void. The transaction is now voided.

pages.title: Expanded Character Limit for Descriptions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Expanded Character Limit for Descriptions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Expanded Character Limit for Descriptions
The CloudPOS description now accommodates a greater number of characters and will be presented in a line-by-line format. In addition, during a Send Payment Link transaction, the description will be formatted as a list in the email for enhanced clarity.
How to Add a Longer Description:
From the merchant login, go to Cloud POS -> Enter the product details -> Click “Enter” to move to the next line, allowing for a more extensive product description.

pages.title: Introducing Paya Ach | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Introducing Paya Ach | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introducing Paya ACH
Paya ACH is the cash equivalent for customer-not-present transactions. By using Paya ACH, ISOs can provide compliant payment solutions for situations where the customer isn’t present, like MOTO and website payments. When cash isn’t an option for these scenarios, Paya ACH allows ISOs to offer compliant cash discount programs to merchants and their customers.
Please note that the merchant must be enrolled with Paya and the ISO must “Enable ACH Transactions” at the store and TPN level from the Edit Parameters section under Card Type.
How to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to the Merchants tab
Search and select the merchant DBA from the search box at the top
Click on Edit Store
Click on Enable ACH Transactions
Next, go S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters
Search and select the TPN from the search box at the top
Click on Edit Parameter
Navigate to the Transaction tab
Under Card Type, enable ACH

pages.title: New Date Filter Option in Invoice Tab | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
New Date Filter Option in Invoice Tab | Release Notes iPOSpays
New Date Filter Option in Invoice Tab
The Invoice tab in the merchant login now features an additional date filter, allowing merchants to conveniently search for invoices based on the specific date of their generation.
How to Use:
From the merchant login, go to Invoices -> Click on the filter icon at the top right corner of the screen -> Click on the calendar icon (Refer to the image below), and choose the date you wish to filter by, based on invoice generation date.

pages.title: Void and Refund | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Void and Refund | Release Notes iPOSpays
Void and Refund
In this update, the privileges for “Void” and “Refund” on the iPOSpays portal have been categorized separately for convenience.
How to Disable:
From the Merchant login, go to User Management
Search and select the user you wish to modify (Merchant, Store Manager, or Multi Store Manager) from the search box at the top
Click on Edit User
Select Modules and Privileges -> Scroll down, and click on Transactions-> Enable/Disable Refund Transaction and Void Transaction.

pages.title: Disable Pay Now | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Disable Pay Now | Release Notes iPOSpays
Disable Pay Now
This update enables ISOs/Agents and merchants to disable the Pay Now functionality under Modules & Privileges.
How to Disable:
From the Merchant login, go to User Management
Search and select the user you wish to modify (Merchant, Store Manager, or Multi Store Manager) from the search box at the top
Click on Edit User
Select Modules and Privileges -> Scroll down, and click on CloudPOS ->Disable Pay Now

pages.title: Run a Sale | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Run a Sale | Release Notes iPOSpays
Run a Sale
This update enables ISOs/Agents and merchants to disable the Run a Sale functionality under Modules & Privileges.
How to Disable:
From the Merchant login, go to User Management
Search and select the user you wish to modify (Merchant, Store Manager, or Multi Store Manager) from the search box at the top
Click on Edit User
Select Modules and Privileges -> Scroll down, and click on My Customer->Disable Run a Sale

pages.title: Enhancements to Send Payment Link | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Enhancements to Send Payment Link | Release Notes iPOSpays
Enhancements to Send Payment Link
Merchants using the Send Payment Link feature faced an issue where customers weren’t receiving the payment link due to URL issues. We’ve resolved this issue by updating the URL, ensuring a seamless transaction experience for the customer.

pages.title: Accurate Dba in Settlement Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Accurate Dba in Settlement Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
Accurate DBA in Settlement Report
Previously, when a merchant downloaded a settlement report, the DBA name was not accurately reflected. This issue has now been fixed, ensuring that when the merchant downloads a settlement report, the DBA name will be correctly displayed.

pages.title: Additional Details in Batch and Transaction Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Additional Details in Batch and Transaction Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
Additional Details in Batch and Transaction Report
With this update, when merchants download the batch report and transaction report, they will notice a new section labeled “Additional Details.” Within this section, the
Fortellis invoice number
is now included for enhanced clarity and reference.

pages.title: Dual Pricing and Tbp Support for Recurring Payment | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Dual Pricing and Tbp Support for Recurring Payment | Release Notes iPOSpays
Dual Pricing and TBP Support for Recurring Payment
Before, when merchants set up recurring payments through the portal, they couldn’t choose a CloudPOS TPN with Dual Pricing or Tender Based Pricing (TBP) from the drop-down menu. This update fixes that limitation, allowing merchants to now select any TPN with Dual Pricing or TBP enabled when setting up recurring payments.
Please note that the merchant TPN must have either Dual Pricing or TBP enabled before setting up recurring payments for a customer.

pages.title: Extended Ach Support | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Extended Ach Support | Release Notes iPOSpays
Extended ACH Support
Paya ACH is now available as a payment option when using the “Send Payment Link” feature on all iPOSpays-powered payment terminals and for Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo. This means that when a merchant sends a payment link to their customers, Paya ACH can be selected as the method for completing the transaction.

pages.title: Non Default Agent Office User Privilege | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Non Default Agent Office User Privilege | Release Notes iPOSpays
Non-Default Agent Office User Privilege
This update enables Agent Office users to onboard and assign privileges to Non-Default Agent Office users.
How to Onboard Agent Office With Privilege:
Go to User Management> Click on the drop-down Action box and choose Add Customer. Then, you can add the agent’s details and set the privileges from the Modules and Privileges drop-down box.

pages.title: Reset Password Capability for Isos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Reset Password Capability for Isos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Reset Password Capability for ISOs
In cases where a merchant forgets their portal password, ISOs now have the capability to initiate a password reset directly from the portal. ISOs can send a password reset link from the portal, allowing the merchant to reset their password easily.
How to Send a Password Reset Link:
Select the User Management Tab-> Search and select the merchant-> Click on Reset Password

pages.title: Alpha Numeric Password for Manual Apn | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Alpha Numeric Password for Manual Apn | Release Notes iPOSpays
Alphanumeric Password for Manual APN
Before, when setting up the manual APN configuration and entering a password, it could only contain numbers. With this update, we’ve fixed that problem, and now you can use both numbers and letters in your password.
How to configure: S.T.E.A.M.
-> Edit Parameters -> Miscellaneous-> APN Details -> Manual -> Enter the alphanumeric password and other details-> click
Save
.

pages.title: Signature Upload Fix in Transactions Tab | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Signature Upload Fix in Transactions Tab | Release Notes iPOSpays
Signature Upload Fix in Transactions Tab
A recent bug that caused signature upload failures in the transactions tab has been resolved. The signature upload will now accurately reflect the details of its corresponding transactions.

pages.title: Clerk Id Filter | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Clerk Id Filter | Release Notes iPOSpays
Clerk ID Filter
We’ve fixed a problem with the Clerk ID filter that used to reject more than a single digit search in the transactions tab. Now, you can search with more than one digit, and it will work accurately, showing you the results as expected.

pages.title: Bin Check for Reward Pay | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Bin Check for Reward Pay | Release Notes iPOSpays
BIN Check for Reward Pay
Previously, merchants using Reward Pay experienced an issue where, during transactions made using saved customer card data, the fee amount was not calculated correctly due to a problem with the BIN validation process. We are pleased to inform you that this issue has been resolved, and transactions with saved customer data will now accurately reflect any applicable fees.

pages.title: Avs Response Handled for Fiserv Omaha and North | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Avs Response Handled for Fiserv Omaha and North | Release Notes iPOSpays
AVS Response is Handled for Fiserv Omaha and North
Now, the response for the check between the entered street and zip code against the issuer’s bank is visually presented on the transaction signature page during manual keyed transactions.
Response Messages
For zip match and address mismatch, the response now displays ‘ZIP MATCH.’
For address match and zip mismatch, the response now displays ‘ADDRESS MATCH.’
For both address and zip mismatch, the response now displays ‘NO MATCH.’
For both address and zip match, the response now displays ‘MATCH.’”

pages.title: Ipospays Fortellis Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ipospays Fortellis Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
iPOSpays + CDK/Fortellis Integration
Required POS Build: 10075
iPOSpays has successfully integrated with Dealer Management System CDK/Fortellis. If you are dealing with merchants in the automotive industry, this integration is designed for you. All your merchant’s transactions will be automatically posted to their Fortellis account, revolutionizing how automotive dealerships handle transactions.
Video

pages.title: User Privilege Management Update | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
User Privilege Management Update | Release Notes iPOSpays
User Privilege Management Update
With this update, Merchants now have the ability to easily enable or disable the privilege for Store Managers and Multi Store Managers to set up and cancel a recurring payment. These privileges can be configured by the merchant within the ‘Modules and Privileges’ section when creating a store manager user.
For existing store managers, follow these steps to manage privileges:
Select the User Management tab -> Select the store manager-> Edit User -> Modules and privileges -> CloudPOS.

pages.title: Merchants Can Set Weekly Recurring Payments | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Merchants Can Set Weekly Recurring Payments | Release Notes iPOSpays
Merchants Can Set Weekly and Multiple Recurring Payments
An update to the merchant login, now allows merchants to set up recurring payments on a weekly basis, enhancing flexibility and convenience. Furthermore, they can set up multiple recurring payments using tokenized cards.
How to set up:
Go to My Customers tab -> Select the customer for which you want to set up recurring payments -> Click More -> Select Add Recurring Payment -> Then, Set up recurring payment.

pages.title: Egift | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Egift | Release Notes iPOSpays
EGift
Latest Build: 10072
This release introduces an addition to our payment options – EGift. Once through the portal, merchants will have the capability to accept EGifts, providing customers with a convenient alternative payment option.
How to Enable:
From Edit Parameters, select the merchant -> Edit Parameter -> Integration -> Enable Gift Host -> Select EGift and click Save.

pages.title: Cvv Not Mandatory for Run a Sale Transaction | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Cvv Not Mandatory for Run a Sale Transaction | Release Notes iPOSpays
CVV Not Mandatory for Run a Sale Transaction
In the past, merchants needed to input the CVV for saved cards during a “Run a Sale” transaction. However, a recent update has eliminated this requirement, streamlining the process for merchants. This enhancement simplifies transactions for regular customers with saved card details, enhancing overall convenience.
How to Use:
From the merchant login, click on My Customers -> Select Saved Customer -> Click on the Action button under Card Details -> Run a Sale -> Enter the sale amount and process.

pages.title: Support for Qd Devices | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Support for Qd Devices | Release Notes iPOSpays
Support for QD Devices
Required POS Build: 10072
Previously, integrating QD devices as Pinpads in CloudPOS was not feasible. However, the recent update has addressed this limitation. Merchants can now select any P series or QD series devices when selecting a PinPad in the integrations menu.
How to Enable:
S.T.E.A.M -> Edit Parameters -> Integration -> Enable SPIn ->Select QD from the device drop-down menu and click Save.

pages.title: Mcc 5969 | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mcc 5969 | Release Notes iPOSpays
Tap to Pay on iPhone Now Available for MCC Codes 5964-5969
The merchant category codes 5964 to 5969 (Direct marketing) now support Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! Merchants with these category codes can now make transactions using the iPOSgo! on their iPhones.

pages.title: Edit Store Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Edit Store Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
Edit Store Bug Fix
This release addresses and resolves an issue that has only merchant stores that were registered on the iPOSpays portal a long time ago. When merchants tried to change the DBA of their store which was onboarded a long time ago, the changes could not be saved. Now, they can easily change their store names and save them on the iPOSpays portal.

pages.title: Recurring Payments Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Recurring Payments Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
Recurring Payments Bug Fix
Previously, when processing recurring payments, only the custom fee amount was calculated. In contrast, the rest of the fee types such as Surcharge, Tender Based Pricing(TBP), Dual Pricing, and True Cash Discount were not collected. This update addresses and resolves that issue.

pages.title: Partial Approval for Gift Cards | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Partial Approval for Gift Cards | Release Notes iPOSpays
Partial Approval for Gift Cards
In this update, we’ve improved our partial approval feature, making it compatible with gift card payments. Now, when customers use a gift card for payment, any remaining balance will automatically trigger a new transaction, clearly highlighted in red. This enhancement simplifies the checkout process for customers who prefer to split payments between cash and gift cards, providing a more seamless and efficient experience.

pages.title: Debit Contactless Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Debit Contactless Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
Debit Contactless Bug Fix
Previously, there was an issue in processing contactless debit cards. We’ve fixed this problem with an update. To reduce decline rates for Visa contactless debit card transactions, enable the “Enforce US Debit AID” parameter if your merchant faces a debit contactless transaction decline.
How to enable:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit Parameters -> EMV-> enable ‘Enforce US Debit AID’ and click Save.

pages.title: Promo Code Integration during Merchant Onboarding | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Promo Code Integration during Merchant Onboarding | Release Notes iPOSpays
Promo Code Integration During Merchant Onboarding
During the onboarding process for new merchants, ISOs now have the capability to add promo codes to their merchants’ profiles. This feature facilitates tracking of merchants who are onboarded as part of a certain promotion.
How to configure:
When onboarding a new merchant enter the promo code in the respective box and click Save.

pages.title: Streamlined Quickbooks Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Streamlined Quickbooks Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Streamlined QuickBooks Integration
Previously, QuickBooks integration was available to all merchant accounts. In this update, we’ve streamlined the process. Now, the option to integrate with QuickBooks will be available in the merchant’s login only if the ISOs/agents enable it for their merchants. Please note for merchants who have already integrated with their QuickBooks this will be turned on automatically.
How to enable (For new merchants):
When onboarding a new merchant, click on Integrations -> Select QuickBooks and click Save.
For existing merchants:
Merchants -> Edit Merchant -> Integrations -> Select QuickBooks and click Save.
Video

pages.title: Agent Tagging during Merchant Onboarding | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Agent Tagging during Merchant Onboarding | Release Notes iPOSpays
Agent Tagging During Merchant Onboarding
When onboarding new merchants, ISOs can now onboard the merchant under a specific agent as part of the process, streamlining management.
How to configure (For new merchants):
When onboarding a new merchant, click on Owner -> Select the agent and click Save.
For existing merchants:
Merchants -> Edit Merchant -> Owner -> Select the agent and click Save.

pages.title: Agent Office Privilege Control | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Agent Office Privilege Control | Release Notes iPOSpays
Agent Office Privilege Control
With this update, ISOs gain greater control over agent offices. They can now adjust agent office privileges. For example, they can restrict certain agent offices from onboarding new merchants.
How to configure (For new agents):
When onboarding a new agent, click on Modules and Privileges -> Choose the privileges and click Save.
For existing agents:
Agent Office -> Edit -> Modules and Privileges -> Choose the privileges and click Save.

pages.title: Four Decimal Places for Fee Types | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Four Decimal Places for Fee Types | Release Notes iPOSpays
Four Decimal Places for Fee Types
Before this update, ISOs/agents could only add up to two decimal places for all fee types. We’ve enhanced this functionality to allow for up to four decimal places, ensuring precision and fairness in fee calculations.
How to Use:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit Parameters -> Fee-> Enter the fee percentage and click Save.

pages.title: Merchant Tpn Geo Location View | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Merchant Tpn Geo Location View | Release Notes iPOSpays
Merchant/TPN Geo-Location View
In the iPOSpays dashboard, we’ve introduced a new feature. Now, ISOs can easily view merchant/TPN locations by using the ‘View by Geo-location‘ filter, which displays a map indicating the state where each merchant/TPN is located. This feature provides valuable geographical insights for efficient management.
How to Use:
Dashboard -> Merchant/TPN -> Click the drop-down menu and select View by Geo-location

pages.title: Mcc Code | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mcc Code | Release Notes iPOSpays
MCC Code
ISOs have the flexibility to change the MCC code for merchants. However, this update introduces a new requirement: ISOs can only modify the MCC code once the current batch is settled. This change is necessary because the MCC code at the time of the transaction differs from the one the ISO intends to apply. This update serves as a precautionary measure to prevent processing failures and prompts ISOs to settle the current batch before proceeding with any MCC code changes.
How to Use:
First, settle the current batch. Then, go to Merchant -> Edit Store -> Change the MCC Code and click Save.

pages.title: Manual Apn Configuration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Manual Apn Configuration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Manual APN Configuration
Before, some SIM cards’ APN numbers weren’t recognized by devices, necessitating manual configuration. With this update, ISOs/Agents can manually set the APN in the “Edit Parameters” section, labeled as APN configuration. So, when the parameters are downloaded for the TPN, the device will automatically detect the APN and work seamlessly.
How to configure:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit Parameters -> Miscellaneous-> APN Details -> Manual -> Enter the details and click Save.

pages.title: Onboard Puerto Rican Merchants Using Tsys Fiserv Processors | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Onboard Puerto Rican Merchants Using Tsys Fiserv Processors | Release Notes iPOSpays
ISOs Can Onboard Merchants in Puerto Rico Using TSYS and FISERV Processors
With this update, iPOSpays has completed the fiscal implementation required to onboard Puerto Rico merchants. ISOs/Agents can now easily onboard Puerto Rico merchants using TSYS and Fiserv processors through a dedicated field in the iPOSpays portal. This field incorporates the Hacienda Merchant Identification (MID), which is crucial for Puerto Rico’s tax compliance.
Integrating the Hacienda MID ensures that every transaction by a Puerto Rico merchant includes this identifier, simplifying tax adherence, improving accuracy in records, and reducing errors. As Puerto Rico’s state tax varies for prepared foods, we have introduced a new ‘Reduced Tax’ parameter to support this.  We also added support for Puerto Rico local card schemes, so merchants can accept all private label cards.
How to Add Hacienda MID:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit Parameters -> Enter the TPN -> Edit Processor Info -> You’ll see Hacienda Status and MID -> Enter the Hacienda MID and click Save

pages.title: Enhanced Quickbooks Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Enhanced Quickbooks Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Enhanced QuickBooks Integration: Automatic Recording of Cash Transactions
Previously, cash transactions made on the payment device by a merchant did not reflect in their QuickBooks. However, with the latest update, all
cash transactions are now automatically recorded in the merchant’s QuickBooks. This improvement ensures more efficient bookkeeping and accurate financial records.
CLICK HERE
to watch the video and learn the process.
Note:
This is for merchant logins on the iPOSpays portal

pages.title: Tcd for All Cloudpos Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Tcd for All Cloudpos Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
TCD is Now Available for all CloudPOS (Virtual Terminal) Transactions
Explore what is True Cash Discount!
Note: This is for merchant logins on the iPOSpays portal

pages.title: Invoice Module Enhancement | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Invoice Module Enhancement | Release Notes iPOSpays
Invoice Module Enhancement: Resend and Cancel Invoice Links for Improved Flexibility
Merchants now have the ability to resend or cancel invoice links from the Invoice module. This new update allows merchants to easily resend an invoice to a different email address in case they sent it to the wrong one. Additionally, they can also choose to cancel the invoices altogether if needed.
How to resend and delete invoice for TPNs:
CloudPOS -> Invoices ->
Select on the three dots on the transaction -> Click Resend Link to resend the invoice (or) Cancel Invoice to delete the invoice.
Note:
This is for merchant logins on the iPOSpays portal

pages.title: Printing Customer Copy in Cloudpos Pay Now Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Printing Customer Copy in Cloudpos Pay Now Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Printing Customer Copy in Cloud POS Pay Now Transactions
Now, merchants will be able to print the customer copy along with the merchant copy after completing a transaction in Cloud POS under the Pay
Now transaction.
Note: This is for merchant logins on the iPOSpays portal

pages.title: Transaction Tag Values for Cloud Pos Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Transaction Tag Values for Cloud Pos Transactions | Release Notes iPOSpays
Transaction Tag Values for CloudPOS Transactions
Before this update, merchants cannot enter transaction tag values for Cloud POS transactions. With the new update, merchants can enter transaction tag values for all Cloud POS transactions including Send Payment Link.
Note:
This is for merchant logins on the iPOSpays portal

pages.title: Isos Can Now Transfer Tpns between Stores | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Isos Can Now Transfer Tpns between Stores | Release Notes iPOSpays
ISOs Can Now Transfer TPNs Between Stores
The latest update enables ISOs to transfer TPNs from one store to another. This functionality proves highly beneficial for ISOs who may have mistakenly onboarded a TPN under the wrong merchant or encountered similar errors. With this update, ISOs have the flexibility to transfer any TPN to the correct store, effectively rectifying the error and ensuring smooth operations.
How to change the store for TPNs: Merchants -> Search and select the merchant under which the TPN is currently in -> Under Devices,  click More -> Change Store -> Select the TPN(s) you wish to move and select the store to which you wish to move them from the drop down.

pages.title: Fiserv Omaha | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fiserv Omaha | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fiserv Omaha
We are excited to announce the release of Fiserv Omaha on iPOSpays, following Fiserv North. ISOs now have the capability to onboard merchants on both Fiserv North and Omaha processors through the iPOSpays portal.
CLICK HERE
to watch the demo.

pages.title: Change Primary Merchant Email Address | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Change Primary Merchant Email Address | Release Notes iPOSpays
Change Primary Merchant Email Address
Previously, changing the merchant’s primary email address was not possible on iPOSpays portal. However, with the latest update, users now have the ability to modify the primary email address associated with a merchant.
Follow these steps to make the necessary changes:
Step 1: Go to the Merchants tab.
Step 2: Select the specific merchant for which you want to change the email address.
Step 3: Click Edit Merchant Info
Step 4: Select Edit Email and change the email address.
Step 5: Finally select save.

pages.title: Integrate Zip Codes for Additional Locations | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Integrate Zip Codes for Additional Locations | Release Notes iPOSpays
Integrate Zip Codes for Additional Locations
ISOs or merchant users can now add zip codes if they are not available in the dropdown list. By clicking the plus button, as shown in the figure, you can input the required zip code. It will be automatically added to our zip code database.

pages.title: Reward Pay Tax Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Reward Pay Tax Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Reward Pay Tax Integration
Required POS Build: 10067
Previously, tax was not calculated for transactions conducted on physical terminals for Reward Pay users. However, if the tax is enabled in the parameters, as shown in the image below, tax will now be calculated for users of the Reward Pay program.
To apply the tax settings, please follow these steps:
Step 1: Go to Edit Parameters
Step 2: Enable Tax
Step 3: Enter the State and Local Tax
Step 4: Click Save

pages.title: Reward Pay 8 and 10 Digit Bin Fee Calculation | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Reward Pay 8 and 10 Digit Bin Fee Calculation | Release Notes iPOSpays
Reward Pay 8 and 10 Digit BIN Fee Calculation
Required POS Build: 10067
For users of the Reward Pay program, we have enhanced the fee calculation process to support 8 and 10 digit BIN verification. This improvement streamlines fee calculation for such users.

pages.title: Tpn Notes for Iso Agent Interaction | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Tpn Notes for Iso Agent Interaction | Release Notes iPOSpays
Enhanced Support: TPN Notes for ISO/Agent Interaction
With TPN notes, we have made it more convenient for ISOs/agents to log notes against specific TPNs or merchants. These notes will be viewed by our support team across multiple locations to ensure a more streamlined communication process between ISOs/agents and the support team, making it easier to address any queries or errors they might have.
How To Add Notes:
Merchants->select merchant->TPN no->add notes.

pages.title: Improved Batches Displaying Cardholder Names | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Improved Batches Displaying Cardholder Names | Release Notes iPOSpays
Improved Batches: Displaying Cardholder Names
With this update, the cardholder’s name will now be displayed in the batches section. If a customer has made a transaction before and provided their name, the database can retrieve that information and include it in the batches. For instance, if a customer previously made a transaction using cloud POS and provided their name, the database can effortlessly fetch this information and add it to the batch section the next time they make a transaction.
How To View: Batches->view batch

pages.title: My Customers | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
My Customers | Release Notes iPOSpays
Module: My Customers
Customer names visibility: In the “My Customers” module, we have made an enhancement to display the customer’s first name and last name. Now, when a merchant performs a virtual terminal transaction through CloudPOS and enters the customer’s details or adds a customer under the “My Customers” module, their names will be visible.

pages.title: Transaction Mode in Transactions Excel File | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Transaction Mode in Transactions Excel File | Release Notes iPOSpays
Transaction Mode in transactions Excel file:
Transaction Mode column: Previously, the downloaded transaction details Excel file from the “Transactions” module did not include a Transaction Mode column to indicate if the transaction was done through a credit mode or debit mode. We have now introduced this column for better transaction tracking and analysis.

pages.title: Reward Pay Dual Pricing Tender Based Pricing | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Reward Pay Dual Pricing Tender Based Pricing | Release Notes iPOSpays
Reward Pay, Dual pricing, and Tender Based Pricing for all Cloud POS Transactions:
Fee type enhancement: We have addressed an issue related to the fee type for merchants using CloudPOS TPNs and utilizing Tender Based Pricing or Dual Pricing. Previously, this fee type was not applicable to certain CloudPOS payment types, except for virtual terminal + PinPad users. However, with this enhancement, we have ensured that Dual Pricing, Reward Pay, and Tender Based Pricing are now applicable to all payment types, including Send Payment Link, Pay Now, and Itemized Billing under CloudPOS

pages.title: Merchant Privilege Partial Void | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Merchant Privilege Partial Void | Release Notes iPOSpays
Merchant Privilege – Partial Void
Privilege customization: In order to provide ISOs with more flexibility, we have made a change to the Partial Void privilege. While creating a merchant user, ISOs now have the option to choose whether or not to grant the Partial Void privilege to merchants. This allows ISOs to tailor the privileges based on their specific requirements. To revoke Partial Void privilege to existing merchants. Go to Merchants -> Select the Merchant -> Edit Merchant -> Modules and Privileges -> Transaction -> Partial void Transaction.
These enhancements are now available in the latest version of our system. No additional actions are required to enable these features, as they are enabled by default. For more details or to make any necessary configurations, please refer to the provided instructions.

pages.title: Command and Communication Center | Knowledge Base iPOSpays
pages.text:
Command and Communication Center | Knowledge Base iPOSpays
Explore Our Features

3D Secure on iPOSpays

Generate Payment Form on iPOSpays

Third Party Integrations

Pockyt Integration on iPOSpays

Commodity Codes
POS Features
Command and Communication Center
Command and Communication Center
The
Command and Communication Center
is a feature on the iPOSpays portal that enables
ISOs (Independent Sales Organizations)
to remotely control merchant payment terminals and communicate with them by sending commands or messages.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
: 10145
Supported Processors
: Not applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not Applicable
Supported Terminals
: Dejavoo P8, P12, P17, P18, P1& P3 (With Android 11)
OS Requirement
: Android 8 and above
How It Works
Enable Control Center and set up the Terminal
Log in to
ipospays.com
using an ISO account.
Go to S.T.E.A.M →
Edit Parameters
.
Use the search box to find and select the target POS TPN → Edit Parameter.
Go to
Value Add → Control Center.
Click
Save
for the changes to take effect.
Ensure the TPN has a build number of
10145
or higher.
Enter the TPN on the merchant’s payment terminal and perform a
Parameter Update
.
A message will appear on the status bar:
“
NATS JetStream
Listening for JetStream messages…”
This confirms the device is ready to receive remote commands and messages.
Access From the Portal
Log in to
ipospays.com
using an ISO account.
Select
Control Center
from the side menu. (If it doesn’t appear on your screen, contact your Dejavoo sales executive to enable it for you.)
Use the
search box
to find terminals by:
a.
TPN/Device Label
b.
Merchant DBA
Select one or multiple TPNs.
Under the
Command and Communication Center
, choose an action.
Available Actions
A. Commands
Choose a command from the drop-down menu:
Command
Description
APP UPDATE
Remotely update the DvPayLite app
PARAM UPDATE
Perform a remote parameter update on the terminal
REBOOT
Restart the merchant’s terminal remotely
GET TIME
Retrieve the current time set on the terminal
SET TIME
Set the time on the terminal remotely
APP UPDATE:
Choose the
APP UPDATE
command.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
A banner will be displayed on the terminal before the command is executed.
If you leave the banner untouched, the terminal will automatically execute the command once the timer runs. You can also tap
UPDATE
to immediately execute the command or click
LATER
to ignore it.
The result will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
PARAM UPDATE:
Choose the
PARAM UPDATE
command.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
A banner will be displayed on the terminal before the command is executed.
If you leave the banner untouched, the terminal will automatically execute the command once the timer runs. You can also tap
UPDATE
to immediately execute the command or click
LATER
to ignore it.
The result will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
REBOOT:
Choose the
REBOOT
command.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
A banner will be displayed on the terminal before the command is executed.
If you leave the banner untouched, the terminal will automatically execute the command once the timer runs. You can also tap
UPDATE
to immediately execute the command or click
LATER
to ignore it.
The result will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
GET TIME:
Choose the
GET TIME
command.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
The time currently set on the terminal will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
SET TIME:
Choose the
SET TIME command
.
Enter a
Job Name
(helps track the command later in the Device Alerts section).
Choose the time zone you wish to set on the terminal
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Click
Confirm
when the
Confirm Submission
prompt appears.
A banner will be displayed on the terminal before the command is executed.
If you leave the banner untouched, the terminal will automatically execute the command once the timer runs. You can also tap
UPDATE
to immediately execute the command or click
LATER
to ignore it.
The result will be displayed on the screen along with the details of the command.
B. Communication
You can send instant messages or images to the merchant’s terminal.
1. Instant Message
Enter the desired message in the text field.
“
Display until user acknowledges
” is enabled by default, so the message stays on the terminal screen until the merchant interacts with it. You can check or uncheck this option to change that behavior.
2. Image Upload
Upload an image.
Ensure the image matches the
resolution
and
file size requirements
displayed for each device model.
“
Keep showing until user interacts
” is enabled by default, so the message or image stays on the terminal screen until the merchant interacts with it. You can check or uncheck this option to change that behavior.
Steps to Execute:
Choose
Instant Message
or
Image Upload
.
Enter the required details or upload the file.
(Optional) Enable/disable “
Keep showing until user interacts.
”
Click
SEND COMMAND
.
Limitations
TPN Limit
: Maximum of
10 actions per TPN per day
.
User TPN Limit
: Maximum of
30 actions across all TPNs per user per day
Action (Command / Communication)
Limit per TPN (per day)
APP UPDATE
2

2

2

2

POS Features
Multi MID
Multi MID
Multi MID
allows merchants with multiple
TPNs
under a store or multiple stores to use multiple
merchant IDs (MIDs)
on the same terminal. This feature is especially useful for merchants who want to manage multiple
MIDs
on a single terminal and perform transactions individually using different
TPNs
.
With Multi MID, merchants can:
Access multiple
MIDs
from a single terminal.
Settle batches for
TPNs
individually. (However, if
Settle By Store
is enabled for the master
TPN
, all
TPNs
within the store can be settled at once.)
Feature Compatibility
Minimum required POS build
- 10130 and above.
Supported Processors
: Supported by all processors
Supported Fee Types
: Supported by all fee types
Supported Terminals & Configurations
: Compatible with CloudPOS with PIN pad, SPIn with POS applications, and Dejavoo P-Series and QD-Series devices.
Key Benefits:
Shared Terminal Costs
:
For businesses or independent professionals sharing a space, a multi-merchant setup allows for cost savings by sharing the same credit card terminal, rather than each needing their own.
Streamlined Operations & Financial Control
:
A multi-merchant setup simplifies payment management by consolidating transactions from multiple merchants into a single terminal, saving time and effort. This also allows for better financial control, as you can monitor transactions and view comprehensive reports for each merchant.
Scalability
:
As businesses grow, multi-merchant accounts provide the scalability needed to accommodate expansion. You can easily add new businesses or brands to the account, ensuring a seamless payment experience for customers.
Independent Merchant IDs
:
Each merchant in the setup can have their own Merchant Identification Number (MID), ensuring that transactions are properly attributed to the correct business and payments are deposited into the correct accounts. After performing transactions, each merchant can log in individually to their account on the iPOSpays portal to review and manage the transactions.
Real-Time Scenario
A hair salon has three stylists: A, B, and C. Each stylist serves their own clients, and payments need to be processed individually. However, the salon uses a single terminal for all transactions. Multi MID helps streamline this process efficiently.
How Multi MID Works in This Setup
Assigning Individual TPNs
Each stylist is assigned a unique
Terminal Processing Number (TPN)
linked to the same terminal:
TPN 1
– Stylist A
TPN 2
– Stylist B
TPN 3
– Stylist C
Transaction Processing
When a client is ready to make a payment, the terminal prompts the user to select the appropriate stylist’s
TPN
or
device label
on the home screen.
The payment is then processed under the
selected TPN
.
Settlements and Reporting
Transactions are settled individually for each TPN, ensuring accurate financial tracking for each stylist.
Parameter Flexibility
The terminal allows each TPN to operate with its own individual parameters while remaining compatible under the same terminal.
Independent Parameter Configuration:
Each TPN can have specific settings tailored to the stylist’s needs, such as:
Fees
Tips
Tax rates
Receipt headers
Store Accounts for Each Stylist
Each stylist has a unique store manager account linked to their TPN.
Example
:
Stylist A
– Accesses transactions under TPN 1
Stylist B
– Accesses transactions under TPN 2
Stylist C
– Accesses transactions under TPN 3
Transaction Access:
Stylists can log in to the iPOSpays portal using their
Store Manager
credentials to view their individual transaction history.
Setup & Functionality
Enabling Multi MID:
ISOs must enable Multi MID for merchants, using their ISO account.
Master TPN Configuration:
One TPN must be set as the master during setup.
The master TPN is responsible for downloading parameters and initializing the DvPayLite application on the payment terminal.
If the master TPN is not used for the parameter update, the device will display an error message and will not proceed.
Batch Settlement:
By default, merchants can settle batches for each TPN individually on the device when Multi MID is enabled.
If Settle By Store is enabled for the master TPN, merchants can override this limitation and settle batches for all TPNs within the same store simultaneously.
How to Set Up Multi MID for Your Merchant
Accessing Multi MID Settings:
Log in to the
iPOSpays porta
l using an
ISO account
.
Navigate to the
Merchants
module.
Use the
search box
to find and select the merchant.
Click on
Merchant DBA → Multi MID
.
You can add multiple
Multi MIDs
for a single merchant if they operate multiple stores and terminals. Each
Multi MID
can be customized with a unique name.
Creating a Multi MID for a Single Store
Select a
device model
and
processor
.
For Multi MID to work properly, all TPNs must use the
same device model
, be configured with the
same processor
, and be assigned the
same build number
.
If the merchant’s device model or processor is unavailable, an error message will notify you.
If the merchant has
multiple stores
, select the relevant store. You can add Multi MIDs for other stores later.
Select the
TPNs
using the checkbox → Click the
right arrow
to add them to the Multi MID.
By default, the first
TPN
added becomes the
Master TPN
.
The
Master TPN
is used to download parameters and run the
DvPayLite app
.
Transactions can be processed across different
TPNs
, but
only the Master TPN
can download parameters.
Click
Save
to apply the changes.
Creating a Multi MID for Multiple Stores
Once a Multi MID has been created, an “Add ⊕” option will appear at the top of the
Multi MID
tab. Click on it to save the configuration.
Click
Save
to apply the changes.
Editing or Deleting a Multi MID
Filter and Access Multi MID
Navigate to the
Merchants
module.
Click on the
filter icon
at the top-right corner of the screen.
Select
Search by Feature
→
Multi MID
.
Only merchants with
Multi MID
enabled will be displayed.
Edit Multi MID
Click on the
Multi MID label
on the right side of the screen.
Select
Edit MID Configuration
to modify the setup.
Delete Multi MID
Click on the
Multi MID label
on the right side of the screen.
Select
Delete Multi MID
to remove it.
Alternatively, you can delete a
Multi MID
from inside the
Multi MID configuration
module:
Click on the
label name
at the top-right corner.
Select the
Multi MID label
from the drop-down menu.
Click on the
trash 🗑️
icon to delete it.
Using Multi MID for Transactions
Download the device parameters using the Master TPN for the desired Multi Mid configuration.
After downloading the parameters, you may see a prompt on the screen explaining the current limitations of the Multi MID feature.
This prompt appears when any of the following features are enabled for any TPNs part of the Multi MID:
SPIn
Register Lite
Split Payments
Offline Mode
To use Multi MID, please disable these features and perform another parameter download.
When the
DvPayLite app
relaunches, a
drop-down menu
will display the available TPNs.
Select the desired TPN to process a transaction.
Enter the transaction amount → Tap OK.
If tip options are enabled, select the desired tip.
If a fee is enabled, a breakdown of the transaction will appear.
Choose the preferred payment method → Complete the transaction.
Once the transaction is complete, you can switch between
TPNs
to process new transactions by repeating the steps above. Device will be set to the last TPN selected from the drop-down menu.
Batch Settlement for All MID Profiles
Multi MID
allows merchants to settle batches across all TPNs under the same Multi MID configuration.
Enabling Settle By Store
Enabling this option allows the merchant to perform batch settlement across all TPNs configured under the same Multi MID setup and all TPNs that are part of the same store.
To enable this feature:
Log in
to the iPOSpays portal using your
ISO account
.
Navigate to
S.T.E.A.M
→
Edit Parameters
.
Use the
search box
to locate and select the
Master Multi MID TPN
→ click
Edit Parameter
.
Go to the
Settlement
section →
Enable Settle By Store
.
Click
Save
and perform a parameter update on the payment terminal for the changes to take effect.
To perform a batch settlement for all profiles:
From the idle payment screen, tap ☆ to access
Favorites
.
Select
Show/Settle Batch
.
A confirmation message will appear, identifying the merchant profile selected from the dropdown menu on the home screen.
The selected username will be displayed. Click
Proceed
to continue.
The following settlement options will be available:
Settle Store
→ Displays if the primary TPN on the terminal has “Settle by Store” enabled. Allows the merchant to settle batches across all TPNs under the same store.
Settle All Profiles
→ Enables the merchant to settle batches across all TPNs configured under the same Multi MID.
Username
→ Shows the currently selected merchant user and allows settlement for that user’s TPN.
Select
Settle All Profiles
→ Tap
OK
to proceed.
The settlement screen will now display a list of all TPNs associated with the Multi MID:
Tap the checkbox next to “
TPN”
to select or deselect all.
Use the
Search
box to locate and settle a specific TPN, if needed.
Tap
Settle
to initiate simultaneous batch settlement for the selected TPNs.
Viewing and Printing Reports Using Multi MID
Using a Payment Terminal
From the
idle payment screen
, tap ☆ to access
Favorites
.
Select
Reports
. The following options will be available:
Storewide
→ Allows the merchant to view and print reports across all TPNs under the same store.
All Profiles
→ Enables the merchant to view and print reports across all TPNs configured under the same Multi MID.
Username
→ Displays the currently selected merchant user (User 1) and allows report access for that user’s TPN.
Select
Settle All Profiles
→ Tap
OK
to proceed.
Choose the desired TPN
to view and print its reports.
Repeat the process for any other available
TPNs
using the same terminal.
Using CloudPOS
You can view
Tip Reports
and
Batch Reports
for Multi MID setups by logging into the iPOSpays portal with a merchant account.
View Tip Report Using Multi MID
To generate a tip report for a Multi MID setup:
Log in to the
iPOSpays
portal using your
merchant credentials
.
From the
Merchant Dashboard
, navigate to
Reports → Tip Report
.
Click the
Multi MID
drop-down menu and select the appropriate Multi MID configuration.
Choose the desired
date range
for the report.
Set the
time zone
for the report, if needed.
Click
Search
to generate the report.
View Batch Report Using Multi MID
To generate a batch report for a Multi MID setup:
Log in to the
iPOSpays
portal using your
merchant credentials
.
From the
Merchant Dashboard
, navigate to
Reports → Batch Report
.
Click the
Multi MID
drop-down menu and select the appropriate Multi MID configuration
Choose the desired
date range
for the report.
Set the
time zone
for the report, if needed.
Click
Download
to generate the report.
The report will display the usernames and their corresponding TPNs.
Using Multi MID With CloudPOS + PIN pad
To activate CloudPOS Multi MID, SPIn must be enabled for the master TPN:
Log in to the iPOSpays portal as an ISO.
Go to
S.T.E.A.M
→
Edit Parameter
.
Search for and select the master CloudPOS TPN.
Click
Edit Parameter
→
Integration
.
Enable
SPIn
and select
Cloud
as the SPIn Mode.
Choose your PIN pad device from the drop-down menu that appears and click
add.
Click
Save
, enter the TPN on the connected PIN pad terminal, and perform a parameter update.
How to Use
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using merchant credentials.
Go to
CloudPOS → Payments
.
From the
TPN dropdown
, select the desired TPN linked to the MID you wish to transact with.
Choose
Pinpad
as the payment type and enter the transaction amount.
If fees are enabled, a confirmation prompt will appear. Click
Okay
to proceed.
Click
Process
to send the transaction to the connected PIN pad.
The customer completes the payment on the terminal.
To process a transaction for a different MID, select another TPN and repeat the steps.
The device will show a Profile Loading animation whenever the merchant profile is switched.
All transactions can be viewed under the
Transactions
module.
Multi MID on DvPayLite Deep Linking - Intent Mode SDK
Multi MID is also available for ISVs using DvPayLite Deep Linking Integration - Intent Mode SDK.
For full details of how this works on the SDK, including request/response structures, please refer to the updated documentation here:
DvPayLite Deep Linking Integration - Intent Mode

POS Features
Register Lite
Register Lite
Register Lite
is a simplified, built-in cash register experience available on all Dejavoo Gateway-powered payment terminals and iPOSgo!(iOS). It enables merchants to efficiently manage inventory, apply pricing rules, and process itemized transactions—all from the payment terminal itself.
With Register Lite, merchants can:
Add products manually or by scanning barcodes (
If using a Dejavoo device with a built-in scanner or the camera of their iPhone
)
Build and manage inventory
Apply taxes, discounts, and all supported fee types
Generate itemized bills with both
cash
and
card prices
Register Lite ensures transactions are fast, accurate, and compliant—especially for businesses using fee-based pricing models.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum Required POS Build
- 10107 and above
Minimum Required iPOSgo! App (iOS) Versions
: 1.0.93 and above
Supported Processors
: Not applicable for Register Lite. However, the iPOSgo! app (iOS) only supports TSYS and Fiserv Omaha
Supported Fee Types
: Supported by all fee types
Supported Terminals
: Compatible with Dejavoo
P Series
and
QD Series
devices, as well as
iPOSgo! (iOS)
.
Key Features
Centralized inventory that syncs across POS, CloudPOS, and Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!
Itemized receipts clearly showing
Dual Pricing
breakdowns
Supports
Level 2 and Level 3
data for reduced interchange fees on transactions made with eligible cards.
Seamless integration with
Master + PIN pad
setups using
True Cash Discount (TCD)
Minimum required POS build
: 10107
Compatible with all major
fee types
:
Custom Fee
Surcharge
Tender-Based Pricing (TBP)
Dual Pricing
True Cash Discount (TCD)
Receipt layouts vary slightly based on fee type.
Benefits
Efficiency and Speed
: Add items to the cart quickly, reducing checkout times and keeping lines moving. This improves customer experience and allows staff to serve more people.
Accuracy
: Eliminate the possibility of human error in manually calculating change or totals. This reduces the risk of giving incorrect change to customers and ensures accurate accounting.
Simplicity
: Designed to be user-friendly, making them easy for employees to learn and operate. This reduces training time and ensures smooth operation.
Cost Saving
: Eliminates the need for a separate cash register by offering inventory management and transaction processing in a single payment terminal. No need to maintain separate receipts. Access all of them from a single place using the payment terminal.
Portability
: Lightweight and convenient to carry, allowing merchants to process transactions or manage inventory on the go, anytime, anywhere.
Overall, Register Lite is a versatile tool that can streamline checkout processes and improve accuracy.
Where to Enable Register Lite (ISO Access)
The setup steps remain the same for Register Lite on both iPOSgo! (iOS) and Dejavoo terminals.
Watch this video for a visual walkthrough of the steps mentioned in this document.
Log in to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Then, go to
S.T.E.A.M
→
Edit Parameters
.
Search and select the POS/Apple Pay TPN from the search box.
Edit Parameter
→
Value Add
→ enable
Register Lite
.
Additional Settings
:
Show breakup on PinPad
– Displays the price breakdown on a connected PIN pad
Set as default screen
– Makes Register Lite the default screen on the terminal
Perform a
parameter update
on the payment terminal for changes to take effect.
It’s important to note that the fee percentage and tax set in the ISO account of the iPOSpays portal don’t apply to Register Lite. This percentage is specifically for standalone payment terminal transactions.
Setting Up Register Lite (Merchant Access)
Step 1: Configure Pricing Settings
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
with a merchant account
Click on
Inventory
Select your store from the drop-down (if multiple stores are present)
Click
Configure Inventory
or go to
Menu → Settings
Under
Inventory Settings
→
Pricing
, set the
Default Price Entry Mode
:
For
Custom Fee, Surcharge, TBP, Dual Pricing
→ select
Cash
For
True Cash Discount (TCD)
→ select
Card
Enter the
markup rate for card
(percentage)
Click
Save
Step 2: Configure Taxes
Go to Inventory Settings → Taxes
Enter a custom tax name and percentage
Designate it as State or Local
Click + to add another tax (if needed)
Click Save
Step 3: Add Items to Inventory
Go to Menu → Add Item
Enter:
Product name
Product code
Cash price (card price is auto-calculated)
Tax, unit of measure, commodity code
Optional description
Click Save or Save and Add Another to add another item
After items are added, you can
edit
or
delete
them from the Inventory module.
After the items have been added to the inventory, you can edit or delete the items from the Inventory module.
Using Register Lite on the Terminal
1. Launch Register Lite
From the
payment screen
, tap the
cart icon
to open Register Lite.
2. Sync Newly Added Inventory
Tap the
menu button
→
Refresh
to sync any items recently added to the inventory from the iPOSpays portal.
3. Edit Inventory Items (From the Terminal)
Tap the menu button → Edit Item
Click on the pen icon to edit the items
4. Add Items to Inventory (From the Terminal)
Tap the
menu button
→
Add Item
Enter the
product name
and
product code
manually or scan the barcode by tapping on the scanner icon.
Enter the
card price
(the system will auto-calculate the cash price if TCD is enabled)
Select the appropriate:
Unit of measure
Tax
Commodity code
Tap ✅ to
save
the item to the terminal’s inventory
5. Make a Sale Transaction
From the
Register screen
, tap
Add
to add items to the cart
If the item is sold by quantity (e.g., in lbs), enter the desired quantity
Tap
Discount
to apply a discount (either as a percentage or fixed amount)
If you wish, you can enable
Make card price as cash price
to provide a discount on the card price
Tap › to proceed to checkout
6. Customer Checkout Flow
Present the terminal to the customer
On the
breakup screen
, the customer will see:
Card Price
Cash Price
Tap CHECK OUT to continue
The customer selects their preferred payment method
Complete the transaction as usual
With
True Cash Discount
, a discount is automatically applied when the customer selects cash.
Multi MID

POS Features
Settle by Store
Settle By Store
Settle By Store
is a powerful feature that enables merchants with multiple
TPNs
under a single store to settle batches instantly for all
TPNs
at once or select specific ones to settle. This eliminates the need to manually locate and settle each
TPN/device
individually, saving time and improving efficiency.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum required POS build
- 10127 and above.
Minimum Required iPOSgo! App (iOS) Versions
: 1.0.97 and above
Supported Processors
: Not applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not applicable
Supported Terminals & Configurations
: Compatible with CloudPOS, iPOSgo! (iOS), and Dejavoo P-Series and QD-Series devices.
Enabling Settle By Store for Merchants
The setup steps remain the same for Register Lite on both iPOSgo! (iOS) and Dejavoo terminals.
To enable
Settle By Store for the merchant’s payment terminal TPN
, follow these steps:
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using an
ISO account
.
Navigate to
S.T.E.A.M → Edit Parameters
.
Use the
search box
at the top of the screen to find and select the
merchant’s payment terminal TPN.
.
Click on
Edit Parameter → Settlement → Enable/Disable Settle By Store
.
Follow these steps on all the TPNs you wish to enable this feature for within the store.
Using Settle By Store in CloudPOS
This feature is available by default for
CloudPOS TPNs
and does not require ISOs to enable any additional options for it to function.
Log in to the
iPOSpays portal
using a
merchant account
.
Navigate to
Batches
.
If the merchant has multiple stores, use the
search box
to find and select the specific
merchant store
.
Click
Settle Store
→ A tab will appear displaying the
TPNs
linked to the store.
Check/uncheck the
TPNs
you want to include in the batch settlement.
Click
Settle
to finalize the process.
The batch status will update from
Yet to Start
to
Batch Settled
.
Using Settle By Store on a Payment Terminal
From the
idle payment screen
, tap ☆ to access
Favorites
.
Select
Show/Settle Batch
.
A prompt will appear with two options:
Settle Store
: Settles the batch for all
TPNs
linked to the same store.
Settle TPN
: Settles the batch for the
specific TPN
registered to the payment terminal.
Choose
Settle Store
→ Tap
OK
.
Select
all TPNs
or choose specific ones using the search box, then tap
Settle
to process the batch settlement.
To print the batch report for the selected
TPNs
, tap the
print icon
at the top of the screen.
How to View Reports Using Settle By Store
With
Settle By Store
, you can view and print reports for all TPNs part of the same store. To do this:
From the
idle payment screen
, tap ☆ to access
Favorites → Reports
.
A prompt will appear with two options:
By the Store/For Store (On iPOSgo! for iOS)
→ Allows the merchant to view and print reports across all TPNs under the same store.
By this Device/For this Device (On iPOSgo! for iOS)
→ Allows report access for the TPN currently used on the device.
Choose
By the Store/For Store
→ Tap
OK
.
Choose the specific TPN for which you wish to view the reports.
Then, choose the report you want to view.
Register Lite

Third Party Integrations
