pages.text:
Fixed Spring Framework Error during Incremental Auth | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fixed “Spring framework” Error During Incremental Auth
Required POS Build: 10118
This update resolves an issue where a “Spring Framework” error would appear on the merchant’s Dejavoo payment terminal, interrupting an Incremental Authorization transaction and preventing it from being completed. The issue has now been fixed, ensuring smooth processing of Incremental Auth transactions.

pages.title: Tip Adjust by Store | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Tip Adjust by Store | Release Notes iPOSpays
Tip Adjust by Store
POS build No - 10117
Merchants can now adjust the tip after processing a transaction for any TPN within the same store using their POS or CloudPOS terminals. This feature allows them to adjust the tip for multiple TPNs from a single device or batch, as long as all TPNs belong to the same store.
Points to Keep in Mind:
This option needs to be enabled
only for payment terminals
and is not required for CloudPOS.
To adjust the tip for a transaction tied to another TPN within the same store, the ISO must enable the “Tip” option for those TPNs in the “
Edit Parameters
” section.
How to Enable For a Payment Terminal:
Log in to the
iPOSpays
portal using an ISO account.
Click on S.T.E.A.M then “Edit Parameters.”
Search and select the payment terminal TPN for which you want to enable this feature.
Click Edit Parameter -> Tip & Taxes -> Enable “Tip” -> Enable “Adjust by store (POS only).” Click “Save” and perform a parameter update on the terminal for the changes to take effect.
How to Use on a Payment Terminal:
From the idle payment screen, tap the
Favorites
icon to access “
Adjust Tip
.”
Swipe to view and adjust tips for TPNs belonging to the same store.
View Tipped Reports Based on Device & Store:
When “
Adjust by Store
” is enabled for a TPN, the terminal containing that TPN will show two options under “
Tipped Txn
” from the
Open Batch
:
By this device
: Displays tipped transactions made on the current device.
By the store
: Displays all tipped transactions for any TPN that is part of the same store.
How to Use on CloudPOS
Go to “Batches.”
Select a store and click “View Batch” for any of the TPNs.
To adjust the tip for the selected TPN, click “Tip Adjust.”
To adjust the tip for all TPNs under the same store, click “Tip By Store.”
All transactions for TPNs belonging to the same store with “Tip” enabled will be displayed.
Scroll down, select any transaction, and adjust the tip as you normally would.

pages.title: Display Cart Items on Device for Spin | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Display Cart Items on Device for Spin | Release Notes iPOSpays
Display Cart Items on Device for SPIn
POS build No - 10117
With this update, merchants using SPIn integration can now include cart items in the request sent from their cash register application to their Dejavoo payment terminals, enabling the terminals to display the cart items along with the card and cash prices of the transaction.
What You’ll Notice:
ITEMS
When the merchant initiates the request from the cash register, the request will include the cart items with their card and cash prices, or just the card price. The display of both prices depends on the register’s configuration, and the request will be sent to the payment terminal accordingly.
Once the request is initiated, the payment terminal will display each cart item with its respective amount. The item list can be scrolled, allowing the customer to review and confirm the items before proceeding with payment.
Both the card and cash prices of the transaction will be displayed so the customer can easily compare the difference between paying with a card and cash.
Options Available to the Customer:
TO REGISTER
: If the customer wishes to remove any item, the merchant can tap this button to send the items back to the register for modification.
CHECKOUT
: Proceed to complete the transaction.

pages.title: Asset Protection | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Asset Protection | Release Notes iPOSpays
Asset Protection
This feature safeguards Dejavoo gateway-powered terminals by registering the terminal’s serial numbers to an ISO Office, preventing other unauthorized ISO Offices from downloading their Terminal Profile Numbers (TPN) into the protected asset.
The ISO requests Asset Protection from their Dejavoo Sales Representative, who will enable the feature as an add-on to the ISO’s iPOSpays account. Once the serial number is downloaded, it will automatically register the serial number as an asset of the ISO account that boarded the TPN.
The registered ISO can enable and disable Asset Protection on a serial number as needed. If an unauthorized ISO attempts to configure the protected asset with their TPN, the terminal will display an error message during the parameter download, preventing the unauthorized download.
Protected assets can download other TPNs created under the registered ISO office.
How to Use:
Once asset protection has been enabled for the serial number created for the ISO, the ISO can enable/disable asset protection for the serial number, and even choose which serial numbers will/will not have asset protection.
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO admin/Add-on account.
Go to S.T.E.A.M -> Assets
The asset-protected terminals will be displayed.
Tap on the three dots to edit, enable/disable Asset Protection.
If the ISO has sub-ISOs or agent offices, they can control asset protection for any new TPNs created by these entities. To do this, the ISO can select the desired sub-ISO or agent office from the “Customer” drop-down menu and enable or disable asset protection.

pages.title: Fee Report for Backward Compatibility | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fee Report for Backward Compatibility | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fee Report for Backward Compatibility
Required POS Build: 10114
This update is for merchants who recently migrated from
Non-Gateway
to
Gateway-Powered
Dejavoo terminals. Previously, for Dual Pricing transactions, the SPIn response code would combine the base amount and fee into a single value (e.g.: Amount=14.00, Fee=0.00). However, with this update, the base and fee amounts are now reported separately (e.g.: Amount=10.00, Fee=4.00) in the POS application when the
Fee Report
parameter is enabled under
Backward Compatibility
for the TPN.
How to enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO admin account.
Click S.T.E.A.M -> Edit Parameters.
Search and select the TPN from the search box -> Edit Parameter -> Integration.
Enable
Backward Compatibility
-> Enable
Fee Report
. Click
Save
and perform a
Parameter Update
for the changes to take effect.

pages.title: Print Receipt for DeepLinking Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Print Receipt for DeepLinking Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Print Receipt for DeepLinking Integration
Previously, merchants using Deep Linking integration with our Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminals did not have the option to print the transaction receipt. This update introduces the ability to print both merchant and customer copies of the receipt.

pages.title: Enhanced Tip Screen on POS | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Enhanced Tip Screen on POS | Release Notes iPOSpays
Enhanced Tip Screen on POS
Required POS Build: 10114
The
Tip
suggestions screen on Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminals has been updated so that when a user taps on any of the tip choices, the app will take them to the next screen. Previously, users had to choose a tip of their choice and then tap on the green arrow in the top right corner of the screen to proceed to the next screen.
How to Use:
After entering the transaction amount, tap
OK
.
The tip screen will appear. Select a suggested tip percentage,
No Tip
, or enter a
Custom
tip amount.
The app will automatically advance to the next step.
To modify your tip choice, tap
CHANGE TIP
or
PAY
to proceed with the payment.

pages.title: Customer Copy Print Confirmation for SPIn | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Customer Copy Print Confirmation for SPIn | Release Notes iPOSpays
Customer Copy Print Confirmation for SPIn
Required POS Build: 10114
Previously, when performing a PIN pad transaction, merchants did not have the option to choose whether or not to print a customer copy of the receipt. This update introduces a new feature where if the
Customer Copy Print Confirmation
is enabled for the TPN, the Master device will display a prompt asking the merchant to confirm whether to print the customer copy or not.
How to Enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO admin account.
Go S.T.E.AM -> Edit Parameters.
Search and select the TPN for which you wish to enable this feature from the search box at the top.
Click on Edit Parameter -> Receipt -> Enable Customer Copy -> Paper -> Enable Customer copy print confirmation.
Click Save and download the parameters on the payment terminals for the changes to take effect.

pages.title: Updated Visibility of “Make Card Price Same as Cash Price” | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Updated Visibility of “Make Card Price Same as Cash Price” | Release Notes iPOSpays
Updated Visibility of “Make Card Price Same as Cash Price”
Required POS Build: 10114
With this update, the Make Card Price Same as Cash Price will appear as a check box on the Register Lite break-up screen only if the Merchant Can Remove Fee or Adjust Price option is enabled for their respective Fee types. If the Fee is disabled for the TPN, then the Make Card Price Same as Cash Price will appear on the Register Lite break-up screen. This update applies to
Gateway-powered payment terminals
.
How to Enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO admin account.
Click on S.T.E.A.M ->
Edit Parameters
.
Search and select the TPN from the search box ->
Edit Parameter
.
Click on
Fee
-> Enable the
Fee
type of your choice.
Enable
Merchant Can Remove Fee
for the following fee types.
Custom Fee
Surcharge
Tender Based Pricing
Enable
Adjust Price
for the following fee types.
Dual Pricing
True Cash Discount

pages.title: Get Signature Response Code in SPIn | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Get Signature Response Code in SPIn | Release Notes iPOSpays
Get Signature Response Code in SPIn
Required iOS Version: 1.0.42
This update is for merchants using SPIn integration. When the customer signs on the connected PIN pad terminal, the response code sent back to the POS system was not reflected to the
Get Signature
. This update fixes that issue to ensure that
Get Signature
is reflected in the response code.

pages.title: iPOS Token in Deep Linking Response Code | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
iPOS Token in Deep Linking Response Code | Release Notes iPOSpays
iPOS Token in Deep Linking Response Code
Required POS Build: 10114
For merchants using Deep Linking integration with our Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminal, the response code will now include an “
iPOS Token
” in it.

pages.title: Pre-Auth Transaction Retrieval | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Pre-Auth Transaction Retrieval | Release Notes iPOSpays
Pre-Auth Transaction Retrieval
Required POS Build: 10114
This update resolves an issue where merchants using Deep Linking integration with our Dejavoo Gateway-powered terminal encountered the error “Transaction data not found” when attempting to retrieve a
Pre Auth
transaction when running a
Ticket
transaction after settling the batch. Now, merchants can successfully retrieve Pre Auth transactions when running a Ticket transaction even after batch settlement.

pages.title: Fixed: Discover Card Compatibility with Apple Pay | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fixed: Discover Card Compatibility with Apple Pay | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fixed: Discover Card Compatibility with Apple Pay
Required POS Build: 10114
Previously, customers using Discover cards saved in their  Apple Pay Wallet were unable to complete transactions on our Gateway-powered terminals due to a mismatch in the Discover card’s AID. We have now resolved this issue to ensure that Discover card transactions processed using Apple Pay are fully supported.

pages.title: Incremental Auth for Pos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Incremental Auth for Pos | Release Notes iPOSpays
Incremental Auth for POS
With this update, merchants using Gateway-powered terminals can utilize Incremental Auth. This feature allows merchants to increase the amount initially authorized in a Pre-Auth.  They can add up to five incremental auth, where each increment will be added to the original Pre-Auth amount.
Note:
This feature is currently limited to merchants using TSYS and Fiserv North processors.
Only Visa, Mastercard, and Discover cards are accepted for this transaction. American Express is not currently supported.
How to Use:
First, you must make a pre-auth transaction. To do this,
Tap on the menu button from the top left corner of the screen and choose pre-auth.
Enter the amount. Then, click OK.
Next, the customer can scan, or manually enter their card details to complete the pre-auth transaction.
To make an incremental auth:
Tap on the menu button from the top left corner of the screen.
Choose Ticket/INC AUTH.
Select a transaction
Choose INC AUTH to confirm.
Enter the amount you wish to add to the pre-auth transaction and click OK.
The transaction is now complete and it will be reflected as a separate transaction in the Transactions module.
Please note that if you void a pre-auth transaction, the incremental auth amount will also be reflected as Void in the Transactions module.

pages.title: Dvstore | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Dvstore | Release Notes iPOSpays
DvStore
Required POS Build: 10110
DvStore is a marketplace application designed to simplify payment processing for merchants, only available for our recently launched P8 gateway-powered terminals.
DvStore allows merchants to:
Access and switch between multiple payment applications from a single, convenient location on their P8 payment terminal.
Receive automatic updates within 24 hours or download them manually at their convenience after new updates have been released.
Benefit from a variety of payment applications and integrations to suit their business needs.

pages.title: Incremental Auth through Transact API | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Incremental Auth through Transact API | Release Notes iPOSpays
Incremental Auth Through Transact API
This update is for merchants who have integrated our Dejavoo gateway-powered terminals with Transact API. With this feature, merchants can add up to five incremental auth, where each increment will be added to the original pre-auth amount.
Note:
This feature is currently limited to merchants using TSYS and Fiserv North processors.
Only Visa, Mastercard, and Discover cards are accepted for this transaction. American Express is not currently supported.

pages.title: Social Media like Share Review Prompt | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Social Media like Share Review Prompt | Release Notes iPOSpays
Social media/like/share/review prompt
Required POS Build: 10111
This feature allows merchants to display a QR code to link their social media profiles, review forms, or a website. ISOs can configure to display a QR code on the receipt and/or the payment terminal screen, which, when scanned, will direct the customers to the predefined website, review form, or social media page. Please note that this feature is currently limited to merchants using gateway-powered payment terminals.
How to Enable:
First, log in to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO/Agent account.
Click on S.T.E.A.M, then “Edit Parameters.”
Search and select the TPN you wish to enable this feature from the search box at the top.
Click on “Edit Parameter.”
Click on “Value Add” and enable “Social media like/share/review prompt.”
Enter a suitable message to be displayed on the receipt or the terminal.
Enter the URL provided by the merchant.
Select QR Code Display:
Print on Receipt:
The code appears on your customer’s receipt only.
QR Code:
The code appears on the terminal after the transaction.
Don’t forget to click “Save” and ask your merchants to perform a “Download Parameters” on their payment terminal.

pages.title: Disclaimer Page | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Disclaimer Page | Release Notes iPOSpays
Disclaimer Page
Required POS Build: 10111
This update enables merchants using the SPIn Cloud integration to display a disclaimer page of their choosing on the integrated PIN pad terminal before the customer completes the transaction.
When the merchant sends a request from their POS System, the PIN pad terminal will reflect the configured disclaimer page and provide two options to the customer, “DECLINE” and “AGREE”. The customer’s response will then be sent back to the POS application. Please note that, if the customer declines, the transaction will not proceed. But if they accept, the customer can continue to complete the transaction.

pages.title: Fiserv Omaha for Tap to Pay on iPhone with Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fiserv Omaha for Tap to Pay on iPhone with Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fiserv Omaha for Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! (Apple Pay for iPhone)
Required iOS Version: 1.0.36
ISOs can now board merchants processing on the Fiserv Omaha platform with our iPOSgo! Tap to Pay solution on iPhone.
How to Enable:
For new merchants
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Click on the “Merchants” module.
Click on the Action box and select “Add Merchant.”
Complete “Merchant Info” and “Store Info”, and you’ll land in the device section.
Select the corresponding Manufacturer, Application, and Processor.
Complete the Processor Info, and click “Save.”
For existing merchants
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Click on the “Merchants” module.
Find the desired Merchant and Store.
Click “Add Device.”
Select the corresponding Manufacturer, Application, and Processor.
Complete the Processor Info, and click “Save.”

pages.title: Fixed Tip Adjust for Deep Linking | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fixed Tip Adjust for Deep Linking | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fixed Tip Adjust for Deep Linking
We received reports from merchants that were using Deep Linking integration that were facing issues with Tip Adjust. This update improves the Tip Adjust functionality for those merchants.

pages.title: Mastercards Requirements for Pin Bypass | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mastercards Requirements for Pin Bypass | Release Notes iPOSpays
Mastercard’s Requirements for PIN Bypass Using Contactless Cards
Required POS Build: 10109
This update ensures that all Gateway-powered payment terminals adhere to Mastercard’s requirements for contactless transactions which states that,” PIN Entry Bypass must not be supported for contactless transactions.” With this update, customers paying with a contactless card, regardless of the card brand, will not be able to bypass the pin entry, if prompted, in order to complete the transaction.

pages.title: Register Lite | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Register Lite | Release Notes iPOSpays
Register Lite
Required Build: 10107
Introducing Register Lite – a simplified cash register experience that is integrated into all of our Gateway-powered payment terminals. This feature enables merchants to effortlessly manage their inventory by adding items manually or scanning product codes with the built-in scanner. They can easily include or exclude items from the cart, generate detailed itemized lists displaying both cash and card prices, provide discounts on products, and enjoy additional functionalities. Register Lite also fully supports all fee types.
Designed with simplicity in mind, Register Lite ensures that transactions are swift and seamless, making even large transactions a breeze to handle.
Where to Enable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account. Then, go to S.T.E.A.M -> Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN from the search box -> Edit Parameter -> Value Add -> Enable Register Lite.
Note:
It’s important to note that the fee percentage and tax set in the ISO account of the
iPOSpays portal don’t apply to Register Lite. This percentage is specifically for
standalone payment terminal transactions.
**How to Setup: **
Login to the iPOSpays portal using a merchant account.
Click on Inventory
Select the action button and select Settings
From the Inventory Settings, under Pricing, the Default Price Entry Mode will change
depending on the Fee Type you are using:
a. For Custom Fee, Surcharge, Tender Based Pricing(TBP), & Dual Pricing -> select Cash
b. True Cash Discount -> select Card
Select Taxes in order to configure the Tax options. Enter a custom name for the tax, enter the tax percentage,  and specify whether it’s a state or local tax. You can add another tax by clicking on the plus icon. For instance, designate one as the state tax and the other as the local tax. Click Save for the changes to take effect.
Next, you need to add items to the inventory. To do this, click on the action box again. Then click on Add Item.
Enter the product name and product code. When you enter the cash price of the product, the system will automatically calculate the card price. Then, choose the tax, add a description of the product, choose a suitable unit of measure from the drop-down menu, and finally, choose a commodity code from the drop-down menu.
How to Add Items Using Register Lite on a Payment Terminal:
From the payment screen, click on the cart icon to access Register Lite
Any item that was added to the inventory from the merchant account of the iPOSpays portal will be displayed here. If you have added more items from the portal, click on refresh, and the inventory will also be automatically updated here.
To add items, click on the action box. and choose Add item.
Enter the product name. If you wish, you can scan the bar code from the product to get the product code. Or you can just enter them manually. You can even customize the product description. Based on the entered card price the system will calculate the discounted cash price.
Next, choose a suitable unit of measure from the drop-down menu. Choose a commodity code from the drop-down menu.
Finally, select the tax amount to be added to the item. Once you’re done, click on the ✅icon to add the item to the inventory.
How to Make a Transaction Using Register Lite on a Payment Terminal:
From the Register screen, click on “Add” to add the items to the cart.
You can add as many items as you want. If you wish, you can provide a discount using the “Discount” button. You can provide the discount either as a percentage or a fixed amount.
Click on “>” to proceed.
The card price and cash price will be displayed on the breakup screen. Click on “CHECK OUT” to proceed with the transaction.
If tip is enabled for the TPN, then the tip screen will appear. The customer can then provide a tip of their choice.
Both the card price and the discounted price will be displayed in the Sale section. The true cash discount program will provide a discount for cash payments. The customer can then choose their preferred mode of payment. Once they select the payment mode, complete the transaction as you normally would.

pages.title: Server Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Server Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
Server Report
Required Build: 10107
To streamline waiter tip management, we’ve introduced a new feature across all gateway-powered payment terminals: the Server Report. This functionality allows merchants to effortlessly associate a server’s ID with each transaction, enabling convenient access to the transactions handled by each server.
This feature is accessible from two locations: the Show/Settle Batch and Reports sections. Within the Show/Settle Batch option, merchants can utilize the Server Report filter to locate all transactions linked to a specific server’s ID.
Moreover, this option is available in both the Open and Closed Batch sections of Reports. For ease of use, the server ID is prominently displayed at the top of each transaction.
How to Use:
Click on the Favorites icon -> Reports -> Open Batch -> Server Report -> The tag value will be displayed on top of the associated transaction.
Close Batch -> Most Recent Batch/Last Few Batches -> Server Report -> The tag value will be displayed on top of the associated transaction.
Click on the Favorites icon -> Show/Settle Batch -> Batch Filter -> Enter the Server ID/Clerk ID to retrieve the relevant transactions.

pages.title: Treat Debit Card as Cash in Dual Pricing | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Treat Debit Card as Cash in Dual Pricing | Release Notes iPOSpays
Treat Debit Card as Cash in Dual Pricing
Required POS Build: 10090
We’re excited to announce an enhancement to our Gateway-powered Payment terminal’s dual pricing feature. Previously, merchants could implement Dual Pricing for credit card transactions, allowing customers to choose between cash and card pricing. However, we’re now extending this functionality to include debit card transactions as well. This means that customers using debit cards can also benefit from Dual Pricing, providing greater flexibility and convenience for both merchants and customers.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M, then edit parameters -> Search and select the TPN -> Edit Parameter -> Fee -> Dual Pricing -> Enable “Treat Debit Card as Cash.”  -> (Image 1.0)
Image 1.0

pages.title: Print Tax Reported | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Print Tax Reported | Release Notes iPOSpays
Print Tax Reported
Required POS Build: 10090
Previously, the ability to print the tax reported on the merchant copy for transactions processed as Level 2 & Level 3 was not configurable. With this new update, the ability to print the tax reported on the receipt is now configurable. Enabling this option will include the tax reported on the merchant copy while disabling it will exclude the tax-reported amount from being printed.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M, then edit parameters -> Search and select the TPN -> Edit Parameter -> Value Add -> L2/L3 Autofilling -> Enable “Print tax reported in receipt”. -> (Image 1.0)
Image 1.0
Tax Reported in the Merchant Copy of the transaction receipt

pages.title: Service Busy after Batch Settlement | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Service Busy after Batch Settlement | Release Notes iPOSpays
Service Busy After Batch Settlement
Required POS Build: 10090
This update solves an issue that occurred during SPIn transactions. Previously, after the batch was settled from the POS and the merchant tried to make other transactions, the POS would show a “Service is Busy” message. This update has fixed the issue, ensuring smooth and error-free transactions.

pages.title: Logo Displayed on Pin Pad | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Logo Displayed on Pin Pad | Release Notes iPOSpays
Logo Displayed on PIN pad
Required POS Build: 10090
Before this update, in a Master+PIN pad transaction, the PIN pad faced some issues when a logo was uploaded. This update fixes that issue and now the PIN pad will display a logo without facing any of the previous issues.

pages.title: Batch Error in Standalone Payment Terminals | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Batch Error in Standalone Payment Terminals | Release Notes iPOSpays
Batch Error in Standalone Payment Terminals
Required POS Build: 10090
Previously, in Gateway-powered payment terminals, when the merchant accessed the Show/Settle Batch section by entering the password, some bugs prevented transactions from being displayed in the batch on certain occasions. This issue has been resolved, ensuring that when the merchant enters the password to view the batches, the transactions will appear normally.

pages.title: Signature Compatibility | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Signature Compatibility | Release Notes iPOSpays
Signature Compatibility
Required POS Build: 10089
This update is for merchants who recently migrated from non-Gateway to Gateway-powered payment terminals. Previously, the encodedSign fields inside
<Sign>
and
<ExtData>
in the SPIn response code were sent as URL-encoded base64 sign data. To solve this, we added a new Signature Upload option in the iPOSpays portal. When enabled, the encodedSign fields inside
<Sign>
and
<ExtData>
will be sent as base64 image data, ensuring compatibility with the SPIn register.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, click on S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN in the search box -> Edit Parameter -> Integration -> SPIn ->
Enable Signature Upload

pages.title: Restricted Mode | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Restricted Mode | Release Notes iPOSpays
Restricted Mode
Required POS Build: 10089
Previously, on Gateway-powered payment terminals, an unintended touch on the menu bar or the SPIn Bypass button would cause the device to leave listening mode. This update addresses that issue. With Restricted Mode enabled, the integrated PIN pad device will remain in listening mode, preventing users from accessing the menu and SPIn Bypass.
Where to Enable:
From the ISO login, click on S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN in the search box -> Edit Parameter -> PIN pad ->
Enable Restricted Mode
How to Access Restricted Mode in the Payment Terminal:
Long Press on any empty space in the integrated PinPad device -> Enter the device password -> Choose Restricted Mode. To disable it, simply long press on the empty space of the device screen again, enter the password, and choose Full Access.

pages.title: P1 Threshold Limit | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
P1 Threshold Limit | Release Notes iPOSpays
P1 Threshold Limit
Required POS Build: 10089
Before this update, P1 devices experienced some issues when the transaction threshold limit was exceeded. However, rebooting the device and reopening the application would resolve this issue. Now, this update fixes this issue altogether and the device will run as smoothly as before, eliminating the need to reboot the device.

pages.title: Social Media Share | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Social Media Share | Release Notes iPOSpays
Social Media Share (Next Sprint)
This update brings the option to add a QR code or a message to the transaction receipt or the payment terminal. This will enable merchants to share the QR code of their socials so that the customers can scan the code and follow them on social media. This feature is available for all gateway-powered payment terminals. (Live or not)

pages.title: Last Few Batches | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Last Few Batches | Release Notes iPOSpays
Last Few Batches
Required POS Build: 10084
Required iOS Build: 1.0.28
We’ve added a new filter to make it easier for you to view batches on all our Dejavoo Gateway Powered terminals and Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!. Now, when you access closed batches and click on the filter, it will fetch the details of the last 10 closed batches.
**How to Use: **
-From the payment screen, click on the Favorites icon (⭐)
-Select Reports
-Click on Closed Batch
-Choose ‘Last Few Batches’
Last Few Batches on Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo!
Last Few Batches on Dejavoo Gateway Powered terminal

pages.title: Mini Receipt for Qd Series | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mini Receipt for Qd Series | Release Notes iPOSpays
Mini Receipt for QD Series
Required POS Build: 10084
Before this update, Gateway-powered QD devices did not support mini receipts. To minimize paper waste, merchants will now have the option to print mini receipts for all transactions. These mini receipts contain all the transaction details just like a regular receipt but are smaller, contributing to paper conservation.
Where to Enable:
S.T.E.A.M-> Edit Parameters-> Search and select the merchant-> Edit Parameter-> Receipt-> Enable Mini Receipt.

pages.title: Change Due Shown after Processing | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Change Due Shown after Processing | Release Notes iPOSpays
Change Due Shown After Processing
Required POS Build: 10084
Before this update, in Dejavoo payment terminals, the balance amount was only visible while entering the cash amount during a transaction. With this recent update, the balance amount is now displayed after entering the cash amount on the payment processing screen.

pages.title: Img Tag in Spin Request Code | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text: Img Tag in Spin Request Code | Release Notes iPOSpays Tag in SPIn Request Code Required POS Build: 10084 With this update, customers using gateway-powered devices and the Semi-Integration Solution (SPIn) can now easily print images or signatures on receipts. Previously, if the register sent an tag, the device couldn’t print it on the receipt. Now, with the update, when the register sends an image under , merchants can do so without any problems. (Image 1.1) (Image 1.1)

pages.title: Reference Id Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Reference Id Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
Reference ID Fix
Required POS Build: 10084
Previously, when the integrated SPIn device was not in the listening mode, the response code sent to the SPIn register did not reflect that the service was busy in the code. This issue has been resolved by ensuring that the service is busy code is sent to the SPIn register.

pages.title: Mastercard Integrity Issue | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mastercard Integrity Issue | Release Notes iPOSpays
Mastercard Integrity Issue
Required POS Build: 10084
We’ve addressed a concern where restaurant merchants experienced tip money loss with Mastercard transactions. This update ensures a seamless transaction flow, providing uninterrupted service for our users.

pages.title: Deep Linking Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Deep Linking Integration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Deep Linking Integration
Deep Linking Integration
We’ve launched a new API called
Deep Linking Integration
that lets other payment applications use DvPayLite for accepting payments. We’ve worked hard to ensure that the API follows widely accepted standards, like using REST for access, JSON format for data, and Basic Authentication and Authorization.
View Document
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Customer Signature Added to Spin Response Code | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Customer Signature Added to Spin Response Code | Release Notes iPOSpays
Customer Signature Added to SPIn Response Code
Previously, during SPIn transactions, the response code didn’t include the customer’s signature. Now, after this update, when the response code is sent to the cash register, it will include the customer’s signature too.

pages.title: Ebt Card Balance in Spin Response | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Ebt Card Balance in Spin Response | Release Notes iPOSpays
EBT Card Balance in SPIn Response
Required POS Build: 10081
This update fixes an issue faced by customers who transitioned from the Dejavoo Non-gateway powered terminal to our Semi-Integration Solution SPIn. Previously, in EBT transactions, the SPIn response code sent to the cash register did not accurately reflect the balance amount after using an EBT card. This update resolves the issue, ensuring that the SPIn response code now precisely reflects the available balance in the EBT card.

pages.title: Alphanumeric Support for Tag Values | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Alphanumeric Support for Tag Values | Release Notes iPOSpays
Alphanumeric Support for Tag Values
Required POS Build: 10081
Previously, the payment terminal only accepted tag values containing numbers. With this update, we’ve resolved this limitation, allowing you to use both numbers and letters in your tag values.
How to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M then Edit Parameters
Search and select the merchant DBA from the search box at the top
Choose Edit Parameter and navigate to the Value Add section
Under Keyboard type, select Alphanumeric from the drop-down menu

pages.title: Format Error | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Format Error | Release Notes iPOSpays
Format Error
Required POS Build: 10081
Merchants using non-gateway powered QD devices experienced problems with format errors leading to transaction failures. We’ve tackled and resolved this issue, ensuring transactions now proceed without any errors.

pages.title: Remembering Static Ethernet Configuration | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Remembering Static Ethernet Configuration | Release Notes iPOSpays
Remembering Static Ethernet Configuration
Required POS Build: 10081
Before this update, merchants using a Gateway-powered P Series Terminal faced an issue where if a merchant connected to Ethernet with static configuration and returned to the payment screen, the device would promptly forget the Static IP address. This problem has now been fixed to ensure a more stable and seamless static Ethernet connection experience.

pages.title: Mini Receipt | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Mini Receipt | Release Notes iPOSpays
Mini Receipt
Required POS Build: 10079
To minimize paper wastage, merchants will now have the option to print mini receipts for all transactions. These mini receipts contain all the transaction details just like a regular receipt but are smaller in size, contributing to paper conservation.
Where to Enable:
S.T.E.A.M-> Edit Parameters-> Search and select the merchant-> Edit Parameter-> Receipt->
Enable Mini Receipt
.

pages.title: Breakup Screen on Pinpad Device | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Breakup Screen on Pinpad Device | Release Notes iPOSpays
Breakup Screen on PinPad Device
Required POS Build: 10079
In PinPad transactions, both the Master device and the integrated PinPad used to display a breakup screen. However, this update ensures that the PinPad device will no longer show the breakup screen when tip, tax, and fee are disabled; it will only appear when these features are enabled.

pages.title: Partial Approval Amount Mismatch | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Partial Approval Amount Mismatch | Release Notes iPOSpays
Partial Approval Amount Mismatch
Required POS Build: 10079
Before this update, If a transaction was declined on the main device and the option for partial approval was off, the PinPad screen showed the wrong amount. Now, with this update, the PinPad screen will show the correct amount entered by the user, even if the transaction is declined on the main device. This change helps avoid confusion and makes the transaction process clearer for everyone.

pages.title: Print Receipt Request in Spin | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Print Receipt Request in Spin | Release Notes iPOSpays
Print Receipt Request in SPIn
Required POS Build: 10079
This is for our Dejavoo Terminal and SPIn users. When the ‘Print receipt’ option is enabled for the TPN, a receipt will be printed for every transaction, regardless of the request from your cash register.

pages.title: Serial Number Added to Spin Response | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Serial Number Added to Spin Response | Release Notes iPOSpays
Serial Number Added to SPIn Response
Required POS Build: 10079
In the past, the SPIn response code didn’t include the serial number for P5S payment terminals. With this update, we have addressed this issue, guaranteeing that the correct serial number for all P5S payment terminals are now included promptly in the SPIn response code.

pages.title: Pre Sale Invoice Status | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Pre Sale Invoice Status | Release Notes iPOSpays
Pre Sale/Invoice Status
Required POS Build: 10079
To perform a pre-sale transaction using the payment device, merchants used to navigate to the “Pre Sale/Ticket” section. However, we’ve now improved the terminology to make it clearer. This section, once called “Pre Sale / Ticket,” has been renamed to “Pre Sale/Invoice Status” for easier understanding.

pages.title: Pos Initiated Settlement Issue | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Pos Initiated Settlement Issue | Release Notes iPOSpays
POS Initiated Settlement Issue
Required POS Build: 10079
Merchants who previously encountered problems with POS-initiated settlement will be pleased to know that this update has successfully resolved those issues, ensuring that POS-initiated settlement functions correctly.

pages.title: Surcharge Support for Fiserv Omaha | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Surcharge Support for Fiserv Omaha | Release Notes iPOSpays
Surcharge Support for Fiserv Omaha
Required POS Build: 10075
ISOs and sales agents can now offer
Surcharge
for merchants who use the Fiserv Omaha processor on iPOSpays.
How to Enable Surcharge:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN/Device Label -> Edit Parameters -> Fee -> Enable Fee and select
Surcharge
.
View Press Release

pages.title: Partial Approval Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Partial Approval Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
Partial Approval Bug Fix
Required POS Build: 10074
Previously, when partial approval was disabled for a TPN and the payment terminal declined the transaction, the transaction was successfully declined, but the receipt was printed incorrectly, indicating that the transaction was successful. This bug has now been fixed, and the receipt is printed correctly.

pages.title: Card Ejection Alert | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Card Ejection Alert | Release Notes iPOSpays
Card Ejection Alert
Required POS Build: 10074
You can now enable card ejection to allow for a payment terminal. If enabled, each time when a customer finished the transaction before the print screen the terminal will show a prompt to remove the card and a beep noise will be sounded till the card is removed.
How to Enable:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit Parameters -> Search and select the TPN/Device Label -> Edit Parameters -> Miscellaneous -> Card Ejection Alert -> Enable.

pages.title: Support for Cash Transactions with Any Pinpad | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Support for Cash Transactions with Any Pinpad | Release Notes iPOSpays
Support for Cash Transactions With Any PinPad
Required POS Build: 10072
Merchants who use CloudPOS + Pinpad can now make cash transactions. Moreover, they can do so regardless of what type of PinPad device they are using.

pages.title: Spin Manual Entry Expiry Date | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Spin Manual Entry Expiry Date | Release Notes iPOSpays
SPIn Manual Entry Expiry Date
Required POS Build: 10072
The SPIn response codes will now be sent to the cash registers with the card expiration date whenever a sale is made by swiping, tapping, or inserting the card.

pages.title: Enhanced Backward Compatibility for Spin | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Enhanced Backward Compatibility for Spin | Release Notes iPOSpays
Enhanced Backward Compatibility for SPIn
For customers who previously utilized the Dejavoo Non-gateway powered terminal in conjunction with our Semi-Integration Solution SPIn, the transition to a gateway-powered terminal has been made even more seamless. By enabling SPIn backward compatibility, the terminal will replicate the behavior of your previous Dejavoo Non-gateway powered terminal, ensuring a smooth transition process.
How to enable:  S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit Parameters -> Integration -> enable Backward Compatibility and click Save.

pages.title: Improved Host Response Code Transparency | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Improved Host Response Code Transparency | Release Notes iPOSpays
Host Response Code Enhancement
The response code for all transactions, including those that have failed, is now incorporated within the SPIn response. This response can be conveyed back to the POS system.

pages.title: Cardholder Name Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Cardholder Name Bug Fix | Release Notes iPOSpays
Cardholder Name Bug Fix
For swipe transactions, we’ve resolved an issue where special characters in the cardholder’s name were not transferred correctly to the cash register when using SPIn. With this update, the cardholder’s name will be recorded in the cash register exactly as it appears on the card.

pages.title: Alternate Payments Displayed in the Batch Summary Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Alternate Payments Displayed in the Batch Summary Report | Release Notes iPOSpays
Alternate Payments Displayed in the Batch Summary Report
When a merchant settles a batch, a summary report is printed. Previously, this report showed a breakdown for ‘With Fee’ and ‘Without Fee’ transactions. But now, an additional summary will also be part of the report, making it more comprehensive.

pages.title: Split Check | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Split Check | Release Notes iPOSpays
Split Check
Managing shared expenses can often pose a challenge for restaurant merchants. However, those days of hassle are over. Introducing our innovative Split Check feature designed to streamline and elevate the process of customers dividing their bills. With Split Check, your merchants now have the power to divide expenses effortlessly among customers. This intuitive feature lets them allocate payments in a few clicks. Experience seamless collaboration and transparency like never before.
How to enable send payment link:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit Parameters -> Value Add -> enable Split Payment and click Save.

pages.title: Individual Tip Reports for Restaurant Merchants | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Individual Tip Reports for Restaurant Merchants | Release Notes iPOSpays
Individual Tip Reports for Restaurant Merchants
Before this update, restaurant merchants were unable to generate individual tip reports on the payment device. However, with this update, they can now print tipped reports based on Clerk ID or Waiter ID. This enhancement allows them to streamline and improve their payroll process by easily tracking and managing tips for each employee separately.
Video
How to get the Tip Report: On the payment device, Click Favourites Icon -> Reports -> Tipped Txn -> Enter the Server ID -> Select “OK”.

pages.title: Fixed Tag Values on Pinpad Device | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Fixed Tag Values on Pinpad Device | Release Notes iPOSpays
Fixed Tag Values on PinPad Device
Previously, when tag values were entered in the PinPad device, they wouldn’t be reflected in its master device. This update addresses that issue, ensuring that the tag values entered in the PinPad device are now correctly displayed in the master device as well.
How to enable tag values: S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit parameters -> Enter the TPN -> Edit Parameters -> Value Add -> Tag the Tx -> Enable.

pages.title: Send Payment Link Option on Payment Device | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Send Payment Link Option on Payment Device | Release Notes iPOSpays
Send Payment Link Option on Payment Device
Instead of manually entering the customer’s card details, merchants can now send a payment link to the customer by just clicking the send payment link button on the device. This feature will be helpful for merchants who take mobile and telephone order (MOTO). Merchants also have the option
to include a tip, allowing customers to conveniently add a tip if they desire.
How to enable send payment link:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit parameters -> Enter the TPN -> Transaction -> Tx Type -> Send Payment-> Enable.
Video

pages.title: Flexible Printing Options on Batch Settlement | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Flexible Printing Options on Batch Settlement | Release Notes iPOSpays
Flexible Printing Options On Batch Settlement Reports:
Previously, after the manual batch settlement, an automatic generation of both a summary and detailed report would occur. However, with the latest
update, a prompt will now appear after the settlement report, asking the merchant if they wish to print the detailed report as well. If the merchant
selects “Yes,” a detailed report will be printed; if they select “No,” it won’t be printed.
This enhancement not only reduces paper usage but also provides the merchant with added flexibility in choosing whether to print a detailed report or not, based on their specific needs.

pages.title: Merchant Control over Customer Copies | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Merchant Control over Customer Copies | Release Notes iPOSpays
Merchant Control Over Customer Copies
Merchants now have the choice to determine whether they want to print a customer copy for each transaction. This option helps in minimizing paper
usage and grants merchants the flexibility to decide which copy they wish to print.
Now, we have introduced a parameter to the Receipt section. Under customer copy print, merchants can choose “Yes” if they want a prompt to appear after the merchant copy is printed, and “No” if they don’t want the prompt to appear. If they choose “Yes,” from the prompt a customer copy will be printed; otherwise, it won’t be printed.
How to enable customer copy print confirmation prompt: S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit parameters -> Enter the TPN -> Edit Parameters -> Receipt -> Enable Merchant Copy -> Enable Customer Copy -> Enable Customer copy print confirmation.

pages.title: Simplified Access to Current Batch Summary from Pos | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Simplified Access to Current Batch Summary from Pos | Release Notes iPOSpays
New Batch Reports API: Simplified Access to Current Batch Summary from POS
In the past, merchants faced a challenge as they were unable to view the current batches directly from the Point-of-Sale (POS) application when it was integrated with the payment device using SPIn. However, with the implementation of this new API, the current batch report summary can now be retrieved from the payment device and displayed within the POS application. This enhancement enables merchants to conveniently access and review the current batch summary directly from their POS application.

pages.title: Pos Application Prompting and Transaction Triggering | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Pos Application Prompting and Transaction Triggering | Release Notes iPOSpays
New API Feature: POS Application Prompting and Transaction Triggering
With this API merchants will have access to an additional information box. This box allows merchants to send a prompt from the POS application to the payment device and receive a response from the device back to the POS application. Now, the POS application can use the response to trigger the transaction. For clarification, read the use case below to understand better.
Use Case:
In a restaurant setting, the POS application can send a prompt like asking for the order number, directly to the waiter’s payment device. The waiter responds with the order number back to the POS application. Based on the response, the payment amount will be triggered for that specific order. This will reduce the roundtrip for the waiter from the cash counter to the table.

pages.title: Option to Disable Spin Bypass Button for Merchants | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Option to Disable Spin Bypass Button for Merchants | Release Notes iPOSpays
Option to Disable SPIn Bypass Button for Merchants
Before this update, merchants could always bypass the SPIn and directly use the payment device to accept payments without initiating the transaction from the point of sale application, such as cash registers. However, with this update, we have now provided the option for the merchants to disable the SPIn Bypass button based on their preference. If the SPIn Bypass is disabled, transactions can only be initiated from the point of sale application and cannot be manually entered on the payment device.
How to disable SPIn ByPass option:
S.T.E.A.M. -> Edit parameters -> Enter the TPN -> Edit Parameters ->Integration -> SPIn -> Cloud -> SPIn Bypass -> Disable..

pages.title: Auto Printing of Customer Receipts | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Auto Printing of Customer Receipts | Release Notes iPOSpays
Auto Printing of Customer Receipts
Previously, merchants were required to manually click the “Print Customer Copy” button on the device for each transaction to print a customer receipt. However, with this update, if the customer receipt is enabled in the parameters, the customer copy will be automatically printed after the merchant copy, following the below prompt on the screen.
Please note that if SMS or Email printing options are enabled, the receipt will not be printed automatically. Only the Paper option should be enabled, along with the merchant copy, in the parameters.

pages.title: Display of Fee on Terminal | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Display of Fee on Terminal | Release Notes iPOSpays
Display of Fees on Terminal when “Disclose Fee to Customer” is Disabled
Previously, when users of Custom Fee and Tender Based Pricing had the “Disclose Fee to Customer” option disabled, the fee would not be visible on the terminal. However, we have made improvements to this feature. Now, even if the “Disclose Fee to Customer” option is disabled, you have the flexibility to display the fee on the terminal. However, please note that the fee will not be printed on the receipt. This enhancement allows merchants to provide a fee breakdown to the customer before the “Present device to customer” screen.
This will display the fee breakdown before the “Present device to customer” prompt, allowing merchants (but not customers) to view the fee details.

pages.title: Option to Remove Tax and Fee | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Option to Remove Tax and Fee | Release Notes iPOSpays
Option to Remove Tax and Fee in Dual Pricing and True Cash Discount
We have introduced a new feature that allows merchants to remove fees and taxes during transactions if they desire.To enable this feature, follow these steps: Go to STEAM -> Edit Parameters. Select the TPN and click on the “Edit Parameters” button. Navigate to the Fee section and select either Dual Pricing or True Cash Discount, depending on the fee type offered to your merchants. Finally, enable the “Adjust Price” option.
Once enabled, merchants will see a scissor icon during transactions, as shown below.
By clicking on the icon, merchants can choose to remove taxes and fees if they wish..

pages.title: Timeout for Declined Transaction | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Timeout for Declined Transaction | Release Notes iPOSpays
Timeout for Declined Transactions in Spin & Pin Pad Mode based on STEAM Parameter
In the past, when a transaction was declined in Spin & PinPad mode, the merchant had to manually click “Done” on the screen to reboot back to listening mode.
However, with this update, the declined transaction screen will automatically timeout based on the screen idle time parameter.
Please note that if the merchant copy for the receipt is turned on, the timeout function will not work.

pages.title: Redesigned Hosted Payment Page for Send Link and QR on iPOSgo! (iOS & Android) | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Redesigned Hosted Payment Page for Send Link and QR on iPOSgo! (iOS & Android) | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
Redesigned Hosted Payment Page for Send Link and QR on iPOSgo!
Redesigned Hosted Payment Page for Send Link and QR on iPOSgo! (iOS & Android)
The Hosted Payment Page used for
Send Link
and
QR
transactions in the
iPOSgo! (iOS & Android) app
has been visually refreshed. The updated design aligns with the latest
iPOSpays
brand theme and matches the recently redesigned Hosted Payment Pages.
Feature Compatibility
Minimum iPOSgo! (iOS) Version
: 1.0.100
Supported Devices
: iPhone XS and later
Supported Processors
: Not applicable
Supported Fee Types
: Not applicable
This update is a visual enhancement only and does not change any existing transaction flows or functionality.
iPOSgo! (iOS)
iPOSgo! (Android)
The payment page that appears when the customer scans the QR code on the iPOSgo! app has also been updated.
Bug Fixes

pages.title: External Payment Options in the Iposgo in App Sdk iOS | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
External Payment Options in the Iposgo in App Sdk iOS | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introduction
iPOSpays
POS Terminal
iPOSgo!
Integrations
Bug Fixes
External Payment Options in the iPOSgo In-App SDK (iOS)
External Payment Options in the iPOSgo! In-App SDK (iOS)
We’ve added support for
external payment transactions
in the iPOSgo! In-App SDK for iOS. Developers can now initiate payments using new external payment methods with a consistent structure across flows, such as
Sale, Refund, and Pre-Authorization
.
New Payment Methods
Payment Method
Description
Key-In
Manual card entry, typically used in card-not-present scenarios.
QR Code
The customer scans a QR code generated by the app to pay.
Send Link
A payment link is sent to the customer via SMS or email for remote payment.
Transaction Examples
1. Manual Card Entry (Key-In)
Used when the card is not physically available; the merchant enters card details manually.
2. QR Code
For customer-present scenarios where the app displays a QR code to scan and pay.
3. Send Link
Used when the merchant wants to send a payment link via SMS or email.
Benefits
Developers can now integrate
multiple external payment methods
in a consistent way.
Simplifies transaction handling across different payment flows.
Provides merchants and customers with
greater flexibility
(manual entry, QR, or remote pay).
In-App SDK Integration
Bug Fixes

pages.title: Register Lite for Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! Gets Additional Fee Options | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Register Lite for Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! Gets Additional Fee Options | Release Notes iPOSpays
Register Lite for Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! Gets Additional Fee Options
Required iOS Version: 1.0.42
Register Lite for
Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!
now supports a wider range of fee programs, including Custom Fee, Surcharge, and Tender-Based Pricing, in addition to the existing Dual Pricing and True Cash Discount options. This gives merchants more flexibility when accepting payments on their iPhones.

pages.title: Send E-Receipt for Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Send E-Receipt for Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! | Release Notes iPOSpays
Send E-Receipt for Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo!
Required iOS Version: 1.0.42
Merchants using Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! can now resend receipts for transactions directly within the app, before batch settlement, through the new ‘Send E-Receipt’ section.
How to Use:
Access Favorites
: Click on the “Star” icon to access the
Favorites
tab in your Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! App.
View Transaction History
: Select “Send E-Receipt” to view a list of all your previous approved transactions, before batch settlement.
Resend Receipt
: Click on any transaction -> Select your desired customer copy receipt mode to resend the receipt to the customer.

pages.title: Additional Commodity Codes for iPOSgo! | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Additional Commodity Codes for iPOSgo! | Release Notes iPOSpays
Additional Commodity Codes for iPOSgo!
Required iOS Version: 1.0.42
With this update, we have included more commodity codes for Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! users when setting up Level 2 & Level 3 Autofilling on the iPOSpays portal. This has been implemented to ensure that the ISOs have a comprehensive list of all the commodity codes, providing more options to choose from when setting up a merchant and saving on interchange costs.
How to Use:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO login.
Click on S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters.
Search and select the TPN.
Edit Parameter -> Value Add -> Enable L2/Autofilling.
Click on “Other.”
Then click “here.”
Copy the desired commodity code.
And paste the code in its respective field. Click Save for the changes to take effect.

pages.title: Transaction Status Check | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Transaction Status Check | Release Notes iPOSpays
Transaction Status Check
Required iOS Version: 1.0.42
This update is for Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! users. Previously, if a network connection was briefly lost during a transaction, the transaction would be voided. With this update, when the connection is restored, the app will now show the transaction status—either approved or declined (for reasons other than the network issue).
If the connection takes longer to recover, the transaction will time out, and the app will return to the home screen. Additionally, unlike before, approved transactions will no longer be voided when the network is restored. You can view the approved transaction in the new Send E-Receipt section. If it doesn’t appear, it means the transaction was not approved.
If the transaction is approved or declined (for reasons other than the network issue)  during the network disruption, the respective transaction status will be displayed.
If the connection takes longer to recover, the transaction will time out, and the app will return to the home screen and this prompt will appear when the network is re-established.
If you wish to return the money to the customer, click
Cancel
. However, if the amount was not transferred during the disruption, a refund is not necessary and won’t take place.
If you click
OK
the transaction status will be displayed within the prompt. If the transaction was approved, you will be prompted to check the status in
Send E-Receipt
.
The approved transaction will appear in Send E-Receipt.
If the transaction was not approved, it won’t appear in the Send E-Receipt section.

pages.title: No Receipt Option for Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
No Receipt Option for Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
No Receipt Option for iPOSgo!
Required iOS Version: 1.0.37
Required Android Version: 1.0.18
This feature is for merchants using the iPOSgo! app (Apple Pay for iPhone/Tap on Phone for Android). Previously, after the customer paid for the transaction, three choices would appear on the screen: SMS, Email, and No Receipt. With this update, ISOs can enable/disable the ‘No Receipt’ option based on their merchant’s preference. If the ‘No Receipt’ option is disabled, the merchant will be required to enter the customer’s phone number or email to complete the transaction.
How to Enable/Disable:
Login to the iPOSpays portal using an ISO account.
Click on S.T.E.A.M then, Edit Parameters.
Search and select the TPN from the search box.
Click on “Edit Parameter” then, “Receipt.”
Under ”Receipt Choices” configure the “No Receipt Option”, and select “Yes” to include the option on the receipt section or “No” to exclude it.
Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! (Apple for iPhone)
Tap to Pay on Android With iPOSgo! (Tap on Phone for Android)

pages.title: Level 2 Level 3 Autofilling for Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Level 2 Level 3 Autofilling for Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
Level 2 & Level 3 Autofilling for iPOSgo!
Required iOS Version: 1.0.29
With this update, Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! users can benefit from Level 2 and Level 3 Autofilling. Once configured on the iPOSpays portal, users can leverage reduced interchange costs for each transaction. This initiative aims to benefit merchants who run B2B businesses by lowering their interchange costs associated with each transaction. After the transaction, the tax amount will be combined and displayed in the receipt as Tax Reported. (Image 1.2)
How to Enable:
From the ISO login, go to S.T.E.A.M, then Edit Parameters.
Search and select the TPN from the search box at the top.
Click on Edit Parameter -> Value Add -> Enable L2/L3 Autofilling.
Choose the commodity code, then enter the tax rate.
Please note that the tax rate must be equal to or lower than 22%.
(Image 1.0)
(Image 1.1)

pages.title: App Update Notification | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
App Update Notification | Release Notes iPOSpays
App Update Notification
Required iOS Version: 1.0.29
In this update, merchants using Tap to Pay on iPhone With iPOSgo! will receive notifications within the app whenever a new update is available. To download the latest parameters, users will need to update the app first.

pages.title: Key in on Android with Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Key in on Android with Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
Key-In on Android With iPOSgo!
Required App Version: 10015
Tap to Pay on Android with iPOSgo! app receives a new payment mode in the Sale section called “Key-In.” With this update, merchants can manually enter the customer’s card details using this payment mode to complete the transaction.
How to Use:
From the Sale section, select Key-In -> The app will automatically take you to a hosted payment page (HPP) -> Manually enter the customer’s card details-> Then, click Process to complete the payment.
Choose Key-In
Enter the customer’s card details

pages.title: Key in for Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Key in for Iposgo | Release Notes iPOSpays
Key-In for iPOSgo!
Required iOS Version: 1.0.28
Tap to Pay on iPhone with iPOSgo! app receives a new payment mode in the Sale section called “Key-In,” Merchants can now manually enter the customer’s card details using this payment mode to complete the transaction.
How to Use:
From the Sale section, select Key-In -> The app will automatically take you to a hosted payment page (HPP) -> Manually enter the customer’s card details-> Then, click Process to complete the payment.

Adjust Fee Option for Dual Pricing and True Cash Discount
Required iOS Version: 1.0.26
Merchants using the iPOSgo! app can now choose to remove the fee, if they wish,  for Dual Pricing and True Cash Discount transactions.
To remove a fee please refer to the images below.
Step 1: From the breakup screen, click on the scissor icon at the top
Step 2: Select the fee you wish to remove from the transaction
Step 3: The fee amount is now removed from the transaction

pages.title: Tap to Pay on iPhone Now Supports Dual Pricing and Tcd | Release Notes iPOSpays

Required iOS Version - 1.0.3
Merchants using Tap to Pay on iPhone can now leverage True Cash Discount, a highly compliant cash discount program, or the most commonly used dual pricing on their iPhones with the iPOSgo! App.

pages.title: Send Payment Link for Tap to Pay on iPhone | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Send Payment Link for Tap to Pay on iPhone | Release Notes iPOSpays
Send Payment Link for Tap to Pay on iPhone
Instead of manually entering the customer’s card details, merchants can now send a payment link to the customer by just clicking the send payment link button on their iPhones. This feature will be helpful for merchants who take mobile and telephone orders (MOTO). Merchants also have the option to include a tip, allowing customers to conveniently add a tip if they desire.

Better Handling of Address Info With iPOS Transact
Better Handling of Address Info With iPOS Transact
We’ve added a smart fallback for situations where street and ZIP code details aren’t provided in the transaction request.
Now, when merchants process payments using iPOS Transact without providing this information, the system automatically fills in the gaps. If address verification (AVS) is enabled, iPOS Transact will fetch the missing details from the customer’s saved card profile (via cardToken), allowing the transaction to proceed smoothly, without any extra input.
What’s improved
:
Automatically fills in missing street and ZIP info when needed
Helps reduce declined transactions due to missing address details
Keeps the experience seamless for merchants and their customers

Required iOS Version: 1.0.30
We’ve launched a new API called Deep Linking Integration that lets the Invoking app use iPOSgo! for accepting payments. In designing the API, our goal was to make it simple, intuitive, and predictable. We do our best to adhere to common open and widely accepted standards, including WRAPPER CLASS for access, JSON format for the data, and Basic Authentication and Authorization.

pages.title: Login as Functionality Updated | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Login as Functionality Updated | Release Notes iPOSpays
Login as Functionality Updated
Previously, when ISOs used the “Login As” feature to access their merchant’s account and create a new user, an error message would appear in the top right corner of the screen. This occurred when MFA was enabled for the merchant and their account verification was pending. This update resolves this issue, allowing ISOs to create new users for their merchants regardless of whether their account is activated or pending.

pages.title: Get Sign Feature | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Get Sign Feature | Release Notes iPOSpays
Introducing Get Sign
Required POS Build: 10081
We’ve added a new String called Get Sign. When the cash register sends a Get Sign code to the integrated PinPad device, the device will instantly display a screen to capture the customer’s signature. Once the customer has provided their signature on the screen, it will then be sent as a response, back to the cash register.

pages.title: Multi Refund | Release Notes iPOSpays
pages.text:
Multi Refund | Release Notes iPOSpays
Multi Refund
With the new Multi Refund feature, iPOSpays users can issue up to three refunds for a sale transaction as long as the total amount of all refunds does not exceed the original sale total amount. This feature can only be accessed from the iPOSpays portal and is available for all iPOSpays users. Previously, the system only allowed one refund per transaction.
How it Works:
Let’s say you made a sale for $15. With Multi Refund, you can split the sale into three refunds:
$5
$5
$5
Important Note: If you choose to void one of the $5 refunds, the amount will be automatically added back to the original sale amount. This will leave the remaining balance at $10.
You can enable this feature for your merchants during or after the onboarding process.
How to Enable (For new merchants)
To enable this feature during the onboarding process, click on the “Modules and privileges” drop-down menu > Go to Transaction >  Scroll down and click to enable “Multi Refund.”
How to Enable (For existing merchants)
Go to the “Merchants” tab.
Search and select the merchant using the search box for which you wish to enable this feature.
Click on the merchant DBA and select “Edit Merchant.”
Click on the “Modules and privileges” drop-down menu -> Go to Transaction -> Scroll down and click to enable “Multi Refund.”

iPOSpays Authentication Token API
iPOSpays Authentication Token API Guide
This guide explains how to generate and refresh an authentication token used to securely interact with iPOSpays APIs. The token must be included in the request header when calling any protected endpoints such as payments, refunds, or transaction status APIs.
Generate Auth Token
Purpose
Use this endpoint to generate a new JWT-based access token using your API Key, Secret Key, and Scope.
The apiKey and secretKey are issued by iPOSpays. If you do not have these credentials, contact your
Dejavoo representative
or email
support@dejavoo.io
. These keys are required for authentication and must be used along with the appropriate scope, which defines what type of access the token will authorize (e.g., transactions, batch reports, or card lookups).
End Points
Sandbox URL    :
https://auth.ipospays.tech/v1/authenticate-token
(opens in a new tab)
Production URL :
https://auth.ipospays.com/v1/authenticate-token
(opens in a new tab)
Post head request
{
"apiKey"
:
"your_api_key_here"
,
// Required: Your assigned API key
"secretKey"
:
"your_secret_key_here"
,
// Required: Your assigned secret key
"scope"
:
""
// Required: Specify the API scope you are using.
// Use "PaymentTokenization" when calling the iPOS Transact API.
// Use "BatchReport" for retrieving closed batch reports.
// Use "CardLookUp" for card details lookup via the CardLookUp API.
}
Response
{
"responseCode"
:
"00"
,
"responseMessage"
:
"Success"
,
"createdDt"
:
"1749532846158"
,
"token"
:
"eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzY29wZSI6InBheW1lbnRUb2tlbml6ZSIsInVuaXF1ZUlkIjoiNzQxYzFiM2YtMTUyYS0xMWYwLWI3YTMtMTZhOTc1NjBiMjc1IiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ0ODA3NzAyLCJleHAiOjE3NDQ4OTQxMDJ9.WUXSxe3e4fMY43qmkVC5wpzzpnj78G590E-tG9O1yZD7RhCu2L2giQOSb6qrfpH6w11iP-n2_ZfpZXu0He66Rge-6FyjKAW0wE5Dz-VLrFsZWxSHaIQLMbm900BPqNo_hBhstfESbO_UA-4uQItfBF5lg8PD1cDhS5K5N6tp1yFOEEflQOUysxvven8rLcg2XnimWJDaH-d-i6-tN9RgFgZCj-ZgAfqW4U3NH3MjcvlCASA-mTTnkJ_PvNBC9HRXBl862-Tgzb9AvZVYgc8qypIWD3QkROpwUmlXoCHfIQFlyQFSPp9rcHiFXy73RrMTppmDMzgsPWpzNKAuXpw4bw"
}
Auth Token Error Responses
{
"errorCode"
:
"AUTH_ERR_001"
,
"errorMessage"
:
"API Key is required."
}
{
"errorCode"

"Invalid Credentials, Please Contact Support Team."
}
{
"errorCode"
:
"AUTH_ERR_005"
,
"errorMessage"
:
"Invalid scope provided. Please use a valid scope."
}
Refresh Auth Token
Purpose
Use this endpoint to refresh your existing token before it expires. Tokens typically have a short lifespan for security purposes.
End Points
Sandbox URL    :
https://auth.ipospays.tech/v1/refresh-token
(opens in a new tab)
Production URL :
https://auth.ipospays.com/v1/refresh-token
(opens in a new tab)
The generated token used for transactions remains valid for 24 hours. Make sure to refresh the token daily before initiating any transactions.
Post head request
{
"refreshToken"
:
true
,
"token"
:
"eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzY29wZSI6InBheW1lbnRUb2tlbml6ZSIsInVuaXF1ZUlkIjoiNzQxYzFiM2YtMTUyYS0xMWYwLWI3YTMtMTZhOTc1NjBiMjc1IiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ0MjkxMzEzLCJleHAiOjE3NDQzNzc3MTN9.E2Rrf9D4ZvM9t-llUAPttVR2_paCxXnYW1rL0z1-g3DnekRsBJLlbT3efm0ecKnO6PZI1AXJNrMx3tM-0cjGvSOiT1-PeqRMSWib7c2yxqN-fkM9gYQlSpvwMPY5GZ5X2JJ2XAt5f4KwyzVsYoFRYutf2ADRj8f_gPFfouyrQH-v0EjGCe1qx1lr_IupXPDfjYzys9w1MNMqUTp9ZJEt8hav5NFFBddQf_Tf5sfynmhAP2DB_UgaINhS16KzQG3mpGzMk6NqWk8iCa2HbTUJBVJB7ZQmoaKsW95mUaXwSwR2w9pMUeM0ME1P-VHDMjQ9RbA86MDoHi1DUm-3OwJkvA"

Ensure tokens are refreshed before expiration to avoid authentication errors.
Use only authorized scopes based on your integration level (e.g., PaymentTokenization).
